papers.abstract: In recent years, especially in the energy industry, one of the most exciting technologies is photovoltaic energy. As a result, studying the parameters of the solar cell model is critical for controlling and determining the photovoltaic cell's features. The photovoltaic parameters of the single diode model can be extracted by using three common approaches: Analytical, Numeric or iterative and approach based on the evolutionary algorithms or by using hybrid approach, for example Analytical with Numerical or Analytical with meta-heuristic. The principle objective of this new paper is to investigate and analyze the characteristics of the single diode model by employing a hybrid approach that combines Analytical and Particle Swarm Optimization method based on evolutionary algorithm. The validation of our model was carried out by using the R.C.T France solar Cell and Photowatt-PWP 210 module. The acquired results were compared to other algorithms and demonstrated their durability, outperforming other methods.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: A Simple Analytical MPPT Algorithm for Solar Photovoltaic System
papers.abstract: The increasing emphasis on generating power from renewable and sustainable energy sources is evident, propelled by escalating energy demands and the requirement for environmental conservation. Given its clean and noise-free characteristics, photovoltaic (PV) energy has witnessed a substantial rise in its penetration within the global electricity industry. Traditionally, to maximize energy harvesting, PV systems are typically governed by a maximum power point tracking (MPPT) scheme. Accordingly, in this article, a simple analytical scheme is introduced for achieving MPP tracking in PV systems. The developed approach is based on analytical reasoning of the Ppv − Vpv characteristic curve and the comparison of the MPP voltage VMPP with the PV panel voltage VPV without the need of any controller. The obtained results show high performances of the developed MPPT scheme in both steady-state and dynamic operations subjected to weather conditions variations. Furthermore, in comparison with the traditional P&O algorithm, the developed MPPT confirms its superiority in terms of simplicity, tracking speed and steady-state robustness.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: A Systematic Literature Review on Regression Machine Learning for Urban Flood Hazard Mapping
papers.abstract: Regression and classification serve as indispensable machine learning (ML) tasks in urban flood hazard mapping. While a machine learning classifier can effectively predict flood hazard, employing a regression model proves instrumental in estimating and predicting flood characteristics within urban areas. This approach reduces the computational time associated with the hydrodynamic models. This paper provides a systematic literature review (SLR) of the current state-of-the-art regression ML models applied in urban flood hazard mapping. A total of 30 articles published between 2018 and 2023 were collected from Five online libraries, i.e., Scopus, Web of Science, IEEE Xplore, Springer, MDPI, and Taylor and Francis. The SLR addresses the raised research questions and reveals that Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) emerged as both the most accurate and frequently employed model. Additionally, the SLR extends its scope to investigate aspects such as flood type, the input data, and the spatial scale of the study area.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Advancing Synchronization for Grid-Integrated Renewable Energy Systems: A Comprehensive Overview and Comparative Assessment
papers.abstract: Within this study, four frequently utilized synchronization algorithms designed for Inverters, serving as the power conditioner in grid-connected renewable systems, are outlined. It includes in depth analyses of the algorithmic structures, complemented by detailed explanations through corresponding block diagrams. The research scrutinizes the behaviors of these algorithms, with a specific focus on their reactions to voltage imbalances, frequency variations, and voltage harmonics. Additionally, a comprehensive discussion of the advantages and disadvantages of each algorithm is provided, supported by a thorough analysis of simulations conducted using MATLAB/SIMULINK.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Analysis of the Perception of Innovations Adoption in Public Health Establishments in Morocco: Crossed Perspectives of Healthcare Professionals
papers.abstract: The aim of this study is to identify and analyze healthcare professionals’ perceptions of the adoption of innovations within public health establishments in Morocco. To answer our research question “What is the perception of innovations among healthcare professionals in Morocco?” and to test our hypotheses, we adopted a quantitative methodological approach based on hypothetico-deductive reasoning. The empirical results show that only two objectives positively favor the acceleration of innovation adoption in healthcare facilities. However, the explanatory variable “Perception of innovations” and the explanatory variables “Professional category” and “Age of healthcare professionals” are independent.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Comparative Analysis of Electrical Equivalent Circuit Models for Lithium-Ion Batteries
papers.abstract: Accurate modeling of lithium-ion batteries is crucial for battery management systems in ensuring reliability, efficiency, and performance for system operations such as estimating state of charge and state of health. This study evaluates three different electrical-based models for lithium-ion batteries: Rint, 1RC, and 2RC models. Although there are other models, each with advantages and disadvantages, exploration and analysis using simulation techniques are performed to determine the most suitable model for a particular application. The 2RC model is the most effective and highly accurate due to its comprehensive representation of complex battery behaviors. It represents different types of resistance along with capacitance effects. This consideration of multiple factors allows the 2RC model to reflect the complexities of battery performance more accurately.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Conceptualizing a Model for Information Technology Governance for Successful Digital Transformation in Higher Education: An Approach Focused on Absorptive Capacity
papers.abstract: In a setting where digital transformation is becoming a major strategic issue, information technology (IT) governance is emerging as a key tool for organizations. An innovative model focused on three strategic areas structural capacity, operational capacity, and relational capacity is suggested in this article. An optimal absorptive capacity, which involves acquiring, integrating, transforming, and efficiently using knowledge, is developed through these areas. This absorptive capacity is directly linked to various key success factors in digital transformation. We argue that IT governance should be viewed as a process of organizational learning that directly impacts strategic decisions and actions of organizations. The accumulated experience and knowledge from this IT governance is viewed as key indicators of organizational learning, which influence the success of digital transformation. Without studies adopting this perspective, this article proposes a conceptual framework that is part of an innovative approach, highlighting the importance of absorptive capacity as a key element of digital transformation, especially in the higher education sector.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Digital Solutions for Enhanced Operational Efficiency: NextGen Air Traffic Management Systems
papers.abstract: In the aviation industry, air traffic management has greatly benefited from the optimization of operational efficiency thanks to digital technology. This research investigates the techniques and technologies that characterize the next generation of air traffic management systems while navigating the rapidly changing technological environment. The incorporation of real-world case studies provides insightful knowledge on how to apply digital solutions successfully in a range of operational contexts. Through case study analyses, this paper demonstrates the true benefits and potential of next-generation air traffic management systems for overall aviation performance, efficiency, and safety. This advances the knowledge of the applications of technology in the aviation sector and the future directions that air traffic control will take.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Digital Transformation: What's the Reality in the Moroccan University Context?
papers.abstract: A strategic necessity in today's academic setting is the broad acceptance of digital transformation. It has a profound impact on economies and society while giving businesses the opportunity to increase productivity, boost competitiveness, and streamline operations. Additionally, it increases investment and enhances the standard of living for citizens by promoting connectivity and digital inclusion. Moroccan universities face a formidable struggle in this dynamic environment. Digitalization emerges as a critical means of improving their overall performance. It also paves the way for the creation of Universities 4.0, which are inclusive, high-achieving, networked, and intelligent universities. But before such institutions can be founded, the dematerialization process and the digital and numerical infrastructure need to be mastered. The study looks at the actual digital transformation that Moroccan public institutions are going through and wonders if using these strategies could make them more successful.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Enhancing Student Success in Physical Education Through Educational Data Mining Techniques
papers.abstract: Each year, universities and colleges assess the rates of success in order to improve the academic performance and failure rates of students. Different techniques, including statistical analysis, physical examinations, and in-course data mining are employed to determine these rates. This paper implements an educational data mining approach, which examines large data sets, in order to extricate important information that will enable to optimize students’ academic and physical life. We consider data concerning their performance, class behavior, activity participation, and other traits. The data is harvested through the KNIME platform and other algorithms.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Ensuring Hybrid Pedagogical Continuity in Moroccan Secondary Education Schools amidst the Pandemic: Impacts on EFL Students’ Academic Performances
papers.abstract: Digital transformation has influenced various sectors including education. Digitalized instruction of English as a foreign language has recently been accelerated due to the Covid-19 pandemic. The challenge was to ensure the institutional need for distant pedagogical continuity. The aim of the current research is not to authenticate theories on online or hybrid learning. The aim of the study is dual. Firstly, it seeks to verify if there are any variances in impacts on the students’ achievements in a face-to-face-taught group, online-taught group, and hybrid-taught group at schools belonging to Beni-Mellal-Khenifra, Casablanca-Settat, and Rabat-Sale-Kenitra Regional Academies for Education and Training. Secondly, it aims to delineate the differences in EFL language learning among the three groups vis-à-vis two language components and two language skills. A quantitative methodological approach was adopted to compare online, face-to-face, and hybrid 45 learners’ test performances. The data were statistically analyzed via SPSS 26. The variables age, gender, residential location, Internet accessibility, digital device, and learning outcomes are gauged. Core findings showed that guaranteeing online and/or hybrid EFL instruction in secondary education schools cannot be productive at least currently and can thus be of value for language course suppliers who crave to apply flexible content delivery.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Entrepreneurial, Organizational, and Sectoral Dynamics of Industrial Innovation: Case of Moroccan Firms
papers.abstract: The emergence of new industrialized countries has been fueled by their efforts in the knowledge economy and the promotion of innovation, enabling them to compete with the major industrialized countries. Thus, innovation has become vital for economies to compete and survive. This article contributes to a preliminary understanding of innovation behaviour in Moroccan firms by analyzing entrepreneurial, organizational and sectoral dynamics. In consideration of this, an empirical study was conducted on a sample of 192 firms, involving analysis of variance (ANOVA) and comparison of means. The results expose that all variables have a significant effect on the innovative behaviour of regional firms, except for the entrepreneur's experience, which does not affect the firm's innovative capacity. The results have allowed us to draw up a regional sector profile highlighting the key innovative regional industries. Defining the factors that impact innovation at the regional level is considered an important step in supporting regional operators to propose programs for boosting regional industrial competitiveness.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Exploring Arabic Hotel Reviews: Sentiment Insights Through Deep Learning and BERT Transformers Models
papers.abstract: An application of natural language processing (NLP) called sentiment analysis (SA) has been a more popular field of study in recent years in both scientific research and the business world. Finding the opinion stated in a subjective statement with numerous methods demonstrating some degree of success in identifying sentiment across numerous datasets is the goal of this activity. An opinion is a personal statement of one’s subjective feelings and beliefs. These sensations and ideas might be associated with a certain emotion. Two sentiments have been examined the most: negative and positive. Deep learning and transformer models are the two most recent advancements in sentiment analysis techniques since the attention mechanism in machine learning was introduced. In recent times, transformer-based models, which are attentive encoder-decoder systems, have demonstrated remarkable performance in sentiment analysis and have been regarded as the most advanced model for a variety of languages. Utilizing this property in encoder-decoder architecture has affected transformer models’ efficacy in sentiment analysis and other NLP tasks. Even so, there is still room for further enhancement of Arabic sentiment analysis efficiency. As a result, bidirectional encoder representations from transformers (BERT) and deep learning (DL) models such as convolutional neural networks (CNN) and bidirectional long-short-term memory (bi-LSTM) have been tested in this work. To achieve better performance, an ensemble model has also been incorporated. By including the standard BERT tokenizer and transformer models, the suggested method is improved. Different models were tested. When compared to CNN and Bi-Lstm, the experimental investigation demonstrates the effectiveness of the suggested strategy in terms of realistic performance.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Forecasting Reference Evapotranspiration Using LSTM and Transformer
papers.abstract: Reference evapotranspiration (ET) helps manage irrigation water supply, minimize freshwater waste, maximize crop yields, and promote sustainable agriculture. However, machine learning (ML)-based ET forecasting models are only effective and accurate if localized weather/climatic data are available, which is not always the case in most cases, especially in many parts of Africa. In this study, we obtained a historical climate record of 23 years from the gridded AgERA5 dataset and calculated ET using the Penman-Monteith (PM) method. Thereafter, two different ET prediction algorithms (transformer and long short-term memory network (LSTM)) were trained using the AgERA5 to overcome the challenges caused by limited localized data. According to the results, LSTM outperformed the transformer in terms of forecasting ET, with lower mean absolute error (MAE) values, root mean square errors (RMSE), and a higher correlation coefficient (R2). Therefore, the proposed approach demonstrates how gridded climate observations can be integrated with soft computing techniques for precise ET forecasting within areas that lack access to climate records from local meteorological stations.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: From Flexible Manufacturing Systems to Evolvable Production Systems: Review and Implementation
papers.abstract: This article provides an overview of the evolution of manufacturing paradigms from craft manufacturing to personalization, as well as the evolution of manufacturing systems from flexible manufacturing systems to evolvable manufacturing systems by the emergence of Industry 4.0 technologies. The use of ontology and Semantic Web as tools of Industry 4.0 is then discussed and experimentally implemented in the case of ENSAM manufacturing cell laboratory. It will examine the ways in which ontology can be used to facilitate communication and collaboration between different systems, and to enable the creation of more intelligent and autonomous manufacturing processes which allows the implement of plug and produce systems.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: HR Team Performance in the Realm of AI-HRM
papers.abstract: Improving the performance of team human resources becomes an important factor which is the target of almost every company that uses the application in Human Resource management. The digitalization followed by artificial intelligence added to these organizational structures has brought about changes in their management methods notably in human resources management. This work investigates the effects of AI on HR team performance in Small and Medium Moroccan Enterprises. We found earth-shattering impacts that occur due to the AI which could be witnessed in 84 Moroccan companies we surveyed. The research findings underscore the widespread impact that AI is having across the work of HR teams, from routine administrative tasks to forward-looking responsibilities. Touching on some of the performance indicators influenced by Artificial Intelligence; lower management costs, faster task processing times, and reduced information leakage risks. In addition, Artificial intelligence not only aids in maintaining decision-making accuracy but also strengthens control practices. We introduce a model to help visualize these relationships and how team performance is an integral part of this optimization through Artificial intelligence integration.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Harnessing Artificial Intelligence for Environmental Sustainability: A Comprehensive Review of Its Applications in Biodiversity, Energy, Transportation, and Water Management
papers.abstract: Artificial intelligence has been applied to address a wide scope of environmental sustainability topics such as biodiversity, energy, transportation, and water management. Actions need to be monitored as soon as feasible in the benefits of environmental sustainability. This study is to find out to what extent AI have made participating in these important avenues by conducting systematic literature review. A systematic review uses predefined inclusion and exclusion criteria to identify all eligible scholarly papers, reports and case studies. An overview of the literature exposes the variety of uses of artificial intelligence (AI) technologies for environmental sustainability. Recent advances in machine learning have made it considerably easier to predict environmental services that the global community benefits from as a result of developments in biodiversity research. Additionally, there is a trend that more and more AI Apps and Machine Learning models are being used, especially for predicting and optimizing the conservation of water resources. Nowadays the energy business has a lot of focuses in neural networks, expert systems, fuzzy logic model, and pattern recognition. Further, this industry has come to acknowledge the need for computer vision applications and decision support systems.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Heart Diseases Classification by ECG Trace Images Using Deep Learning
papers.abstract: Recently, many deep learning algorithms have emerged as advanced techniques in the medical field for diagnosing diseases, including heart disease. In this study, an approach was followed that is based on electrocardiogram (ECG) images to detect different heart diseases. Pre-processing was performed for the data images using morphology technology to remove lines from the background ECG paper image to obtain an image containing only the changes of electrical activity for the potion’s heart. The pre-processed data images are trained at a rate of 80% of each class data image in the training stage and 20% of each class image used for the testing stage in the efficiency evaluating stage of each model. Seven classification models have been proposed in binary classification. Models 1–7 have been trained to classify the natural ECG case (Nrm) with the other diseases. Models’ efficiency is calculated using four measures, where the accuracy reaches 100%, the precision reaches 100%, the specificity is 100%, and the f1-score is 100%. For models 6 and 7, the results of the accuracy reached (88.1366 and 91.0978)%, precision (80.7443 and 91.0834)%, specificity (79.1734 and 88.8665)%, and f1-score (79.4476 and 89.8999) %. The proposed diagnostic system is fast, accessible, more sensitive, and harmless. It is also more cost-effective than any other diagnostic method.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Hybrid Cryptography Based on Constellations: Cassiopeia
papers.abstract: Data protection has become the top priority for all organizations and businesses, given the evolution of information systems and the new challenges imposed by the Covid-19 pandemic. Remote access represents a tangible solution for all companies to mitigate the impact and sustain their growth. Nevertheless, many companies have experienced security breaches as a result of remote access. Today, it is imperative to initiate a revolution in securing networks and information systems and to foster innovation in cryptography, as it is the fundamental mechanism for safeguarding information. This document serves as a presentation of the Cassiopeia encryption method, which is based on the ‘Cassiopeia’ constellation, comprising five stars from the northern sky. We will leverage this constellation to develop a hybrid algorithm capable of ensuring confidentiality, integrity, and authenticity.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: IP-Self-Tuning Fuzzy Logic Controller Based on Direct Torque Control to Improve Induction Machine Speed Control
papers.abstract: The current paper outlines a proposal for an IP (Integral Proportional) self-tuning regulator that uses the fuzzy logic controller by adjusting its proportional gain, to enhance the regulation of the speed of the induction machine through the use of a Direct Torque Control (DTC) strategy. The suggested self-tuning mechanism has the capability to adapt one gain, set against the two gains required for a PI-self-tuning controller. The effectiveness or efficiency of the previously mentioned controller was evaluated against that of a traditional IP controller and a fuzzy PI controller (Proportional Integral) in relation to time response, tracking performance, and disturbance rejection capability. All simulations outcomes ac-quired through the utilization of Simulink and fuzzy tools within MATLAB software for an induction machine with a power rating of 1.5 KW showed excellent performances as well as verified the effectiveness of this controller.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Image Encryption Using Hill Cipher Under a Chaotic Vector's Control
papers.abstract: The innovative approach in this study for image encryption focuses on advancing Hill's technique by incorporating a pseudo-random binary vector. The process includes transforming the original image through a vector transition, followed by dividing the image into blocks of three pixels each. To enhance confusion, an invertible matrix, multiple pseudo-random vectors, and an affine transformation are utilized. Moreover, diffusion is attained by introducing a distinct random lattice that doesn't necessarily have to be invertible, thereby fortifying the method against differential attacks. Comprehensive simulations performed on numerous images showcase the outstanding security performance of our algorithm.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Industry 4.0 Implementation in Moroccan Companies: Benefits and Barriers
papers.abstract: Industry 4.0 incorporates a highly advanced concept of the factory of the future, based on the intelligent connection of machines and devices in a network that smoothly merges data and knowledge. To stay ahead in the Industry 4.0 era, companies must embrace digital and technological advances by integrating new technologies into their operations. Meanwhile, Moroccan companies are embarking on their industrial revolution. The goal of this work is to explore the benefits and barriers facing the implementation of Industry 4.0 in Moroccan companies and to achieve this, a structured questionnaire survey was conducted to gather relevant data. A total of 143 valid survey returns were captured and analyzed utilizing the Statistical Package for the Social Sciences (SPSS V25.0 for Windows).
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Innovating Corporate Social Responsibility Integration in Human Resource Practices: A Systematic Review
papers.abstract: In this systematic literature review, an in-depth exploration of the interaction between corporate social responsibility (CSR) and human resource management (HRM) is undertaken. These two crucial elements play a fundamental role in achieving organizational excellence and adopting sustainable practices. The methodology of Tranfield, Denyer, and Smart (2003) is utilized, following a methodical approach from the identification to the detailed analysis of sources to extract key lessons. CSR is viewed as a business philosophy that deliberately integrates social and environmental concerns beyond mere financial considerations, while HRM focuses on the strategic management of talent within the company. The integration of CSR and HRM is recognized as an increasingly relevant area of research, suggesting that ethically rooted HRM practices can stimulate employee engagement and contribute to the sustainable development of the company. The Scopus database was leveraged for its wealth of research, and the analysis commenced with a broad examination of articles. These were progressively narrowed down to 215 relevant studies through rigorous selection criteria focusing on CSR and HRM. Further examination resulted in 41 studies being retained for more detailed analysis. This exploration revealed a complex landscape of interactions, where CSR initiatives intertwine closely with HRM strategies, creating corporate cultures that value employee engagement and strengthen the corporate brand. The strategic merging of HRM and CSR practices is identified as a marker of the modern orientation of organizations, underlining the importance of simultaneously achieving organizational objectives and societal well-being.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Integrating Advanced Combined Numerical Filters for ECG Denoising and Cardiovascular Disease Classification Using Deep Learning
papers.abstract: In this paper, a novel method for ECG signal processing for cardiovascular disease classification using deep learning is proposed. The central element of the study is a new combined numerical filter specifically designed for ECG signal denoising. This filter, a unique fusion of Chebyshev, Butterworth, and Daubechies filters, has been optimized to effectively denoise the ECG signal while preserving its relevant features. This improvement in signal quality is critical for the successful classification of heart disease using advanced artificial intelligence (AI) techniques. The method has been specifically applied to the case of arrhythmias, where a significant improvement in signal quality has been demonstrated. The application of this filter by deep neural networks significantly improves the accuracy and reliability of cardiac abnormality detection and classification, especially in arrhythmia cases. The potential of combining advanced filtering techniques with deep learning for more accurate medical diagnosis is demonstrated, opening up new perspectives in the diagnosis and monitoring of cardiovascular diseases.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Investigating the Robustness of Sliding Mode Control Applied to NPC Rectifier-Based Smart Transformer
papers.abstract: In recent years, the Smart Transformer (ST), defined as an advanced type of power electronic transformer incorporating sophisticated communication and control systems, has received increasing attention in the power distribution system. Its power circuit is a combination of High Frequency (HF) transformers and power electronic converters. In power distribution systems, the three-stage ST topology comprising a medium voltage AC-DC power stage, a HF DC-DC power stage, and a low voltage DC-AC power stage, is selected as the most appropriate due to the availability of DC buses on the medium and low voltage sides. However, it has a low efficiency as multiple power converters are integrated. To enhance the performance of this topology, an ST model comprising a 3-level Neutral Point Clamp (NPC) rectifier, a dual active bridge power electronic converter, and a 3-phase inverter is proposed in this manuscript. To optimize the performance of the studied ST model, the sliding-mode controller is implemented at the NPC rectifier and the 3-phase inverter, while the soft-switching technology is used for the dual active bridge converter. Furthermore, several simulations under disturbed operating modes, including voltage flicker, overvoltage, voltage sag, and parametric variations, are performed to demonstrate the good performance of the ST model under study and the great robustness of the proposed control strategy.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Is AI an Effective “Learning Tool” in Academic Writing? Investigating the Perceptions of Third-Year University Students on the Use of Artificial Intelligence in Classroom Instruction
papers.abstract: Artificial intelligence is widely known around the world and is used in different human fields –from banking and finance to education. However, in the field of education, little is known about its use the Philippine education context. Adopting the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM), this study determined and analyzed the perception of third-year university students on the use of artificial intelligence as a “learning tool” in academic writing. It employed a descriptive-quantitative research design with a 12-item survey questionnaire that was used to measure the perceptions on the three different aspects related to the usage of artificial intelligence in academic writing among the third-year students from four different colleges of a southwestern Philippine university. The findings of the study revealed that the participants had an overall positive disposition towards the usage of AI in academic writing in terms of its perceived use, perceived ease of use, as well as attitude toward usage. The findings further suggest that these university students demonstrate both awareness and readiness on the utilization of artificial intelligence in their academic writing.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Leveraging Artificial Intelligence for Environmental Information Integration in Investment Decision Making
papers.abstract: The growing focus on environmental sustainability in current investment decisions calls for a complete rethinking. Environmental factors are increasingly embedded into how investors calculate risk and opportunities, alongside traditional financial metrics. But incorporating environmental data into investment analysis faces a whole host of challenges, complexity because of inherent diversity, lack of standardization and the list goes on. The present study attempts to investigate the transformative capacity of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in addressing these challenges. As the potential solution, AI burst onto the scene and seemed set to transform environmental data with the promise of novel means to boost its investment relevance. This research employs a rigorous structured methodology, which includes systematic literature review, empirical validation and a theoretical framework development. This study is a comprehensive evaluation of whether AI is practical for using environmental data in investment decisions. Therefore, the research shows how AI can be applied to environmental information intelligence and its actual capabilities in solving problems in the ever-changing world of investment studies.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Leveraging Deep Collaborative Filtering for Advanced Recommender Systems
papers.abstract: In the field of personalized recommendations, collaborative filtering techniques have evolved from traditional methods to advanced recommendation systems powered by deep learning. This study thoroughly investigates this transformative evolution, delving into the com- plex workings of recommender systems. Beyond theoretical discussions, our investigation involves a practical evaluation of these techniques on a variety of datasets, including the Amazon and MovieLens datasets, representing the e-commerce and entertainment domains. Through this perspective, we better understand how recommendation models dynamically adapt, strategically leveraging deep learning to build advanced recommendation systems adapted to various real-world contexts. To assess the predictive capabilities of these models, we use metrics such as RMSE (Root Mean Square Error), MAE (Mean Absolute Error) and MSE (Mean Squared Error), offering qualified insight into the effective- ness of each technique, with a particular focus on the strategic use of deep learning for the development of advanced recommender systems. This analysis transcends exploration, offering practical implications for researchers and data scientists. Contributing to the current debate in the field, our study serves as a definitive guide for the design of recommender systems that strategically exploit deep learning, ensuring their relevance and effectiveness in a variety of application scenarios.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Leveraging Machine Learning for Signal Processing in Surface Electromyography (sEMG) for Prosthetic Control
papers.abstract: In this study, we delve into enhancing prosthetic limb control through surface electromyography (sEMG) via machine learning techniques, addressing challenges like signal selectivity and noise. With the backdrop of exponential growth in computational power and artificial intelligence, we employ convolutional neural networks (CNNs), among other algorithms, for feature extraction and movement classification from sEMG data, showcasing CNNs’ superiority in automating feature extraction despite higher computational requirements. Our innovative approach introduces an estimation method incorporating joint angle measurements to refine hand movement estimation, aiming to overcome the limitations of traditional classification methods. This method marks a significant step towards achieving more nuanced and adaptable prosthetic control. Findings reveal that machine learning enhances the precision and flexibility of prosthetic control systems, with the potential to significantly improve amputee patients’ quality of life by providing more responsive and naturalistic limb control. This study not only contributes to advancing prosthetic control technology but also sets the stage for future research to further optimize prosthetic limb functionality.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Low Lightness Image Enhancement Using HSV Color Based on DCP with Color Restoration and Lightning Stretch
papers.abstract: The poor visibility of low-light images makes them unsuitable for computer vision algorithms and human observation. Many image-enhancing methods have been suggested as techniques for this problem. But it does not restore colors and improve lighting properly. Therefore, in this study, we propose an algorithm that restores colors and improves lightness for low-light images using LOL data, which contains 485 low-light images. The proposed algorithm includes three main steps. The first is to use the brightness low light area technique, and the second is to restore colors by applying two filters, the third is to improve the lighting component based on the HSV color model, where the color components are isolated from the lighting, and then maps are drawn using lightness stretch and adaptive histogram equalization. We have used reference quality standards Peak Signal to Noise Ratio (PSNR) and non-reference quality metrics Entropy (EN)‚ Average Gradient (AG) and Natural Image Quality Evaluator(NIQE). Through analyzing the results, the proposed method succeeded in improving low-light images and color restoration, and the quality standards reached EN (7.004), AG (11.286), NIQE(3.502), and PSNR (14.013) values.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Machine Learning and Big Data for Cybersecurity: Systematic Literature Review
papers.abstract: Today’s advances in wireless communication technology have resulted in the generation of large amounts of data. Networks face a large amount of data every moment. The utilization of advanced technologies, systems, and practices, intends to protect networks, organizations, projects, and data from assaults, attacks, harms, or unapproved access, which is defined as Cybersecurity. Cyberattacks have increased rapidly in various domains. For security reasons, cybersecurity solutions must search through ever-growing mountains of data for possible intrusion patterns. Therefore, it becomes necessary in such environment and conditions to detect intrusions in a fast and accurate way in order to protect data. The extent of this System Literature Review (SLR) includes an investigation of the most well-known artificial intelligence technologies applied to cybersecurity. This research work was conducted on multiple databases (Springer Link, SCOPUS, ScienceDirect, IEEE Xplore, ACM, and MDPI). From these sources, 35 articles published during the range of 2014 and 2023 were chosen, investigated and thoroughly assessed. This survey analyses Machine Learning and Big Data techniques used in cybersecurity and their adequacy in detecting attacks. Different Machine Learning and Deep Learning algorithms are presented and compared. Then, an in-depth analysis based on some metrics criteria: Accuracy, Precision, Detection Rate, False Positive Rate, Training Time and Prediction Time, is performed. Finally, the limitations encountered during this research work are presented along future work related to the studied topic.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Maximum Power Point Tracking and Field-Oriented Control Strategies Applied to PMSG-Based WECS
papers.abstract: In recent days, progress has been made in the development of power electronics and control strategies applied to permanent magnet synchronous generators (PMSG), enabling their large-scale use in wind energy conversion systems. This generator offers many benefits, such as low weight and high efficiency relative to cost. In addition, the low noise in direct drive wind turbine based on PMSG technology, thanks to absence of a gearbox. This study aims to present mathematical modeling and control strategies of the variable speed wind turbine based on a PMSG. Applying the field-oriented control strategy to the machine side converter leads to extracting the maximum of the available wind power via the maximum power point tracking (MPPT) approach. First, the MPPT technique without speed control is used to generate the optimum electromagnetic torque. This torque was then utilized to determine the reference stator quadrature current. The entire system was simulated using MATLAB/Simulink software environment. The simulation results reveal a good tracking performance.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Mini-review on DC-DC Power Converters Used in V2X Battery Chargers
papers.abstract: The widespread adoption of Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) communication systems has significantly transformed the electric vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure landscape. As V2X Chargers are essential for facilitating bidirectional power flow amid electric vehicles and the grid, efficiency and reliability of the power conversion process become paramount. This mini-review provides a comprehensive overview of recent advancements in DC-DC converters utilized in V2X chargers, focusing on the existing topologies and their features.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Multi-objective Evolutionary Algorithms in Recommender Systems
papers.abstract: Recommender systems are beneficial in suggesting items to users by knowing their preferences and, therefore, effectively managing the vast amount of available information. Regarding the classical systems that focus on accuracy, the needs of their users have changed so much that many sometimes-conflicting performance measures now have to be taken into account. Recent research has enhanced the applicability of multi-objective evolutionary algorithms in recommender systems, balancing indicators such as accuracy with other essential ones. This survey provides a listing of recent works that applied MOEAs to the problem of recommender systems and pays special attention to critical areas, such as methodological approaches, goals, datasets, and evaluation strategies. This analysis, beyond the state-of-the-art synthesis, helps in the determination of the problems that are linked to the use of MOEAs and the prospects of the development of future research. The exploration targets aiding progress and innovation in this dynamic field.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: New Vigenere Method Surrounded by Two Genetic Crossovers For Color Image Encryption
papers.abstract: In the present work, we introduce an innovative approach to encrypting color images. Our method represents a significant advancement over the conventional Vigenere cipher by employing a significantly enhanced version, achieved through the fusion of a large pseudorandom replacement matrix derived from two largely used chaotic sequences in cryptography. The process commences with the con-version of the original image into a one-dimensional vector, followed by the computation of an initialization value. This value is of utmost importance as it alters the initial pixel value, thereby initiating the encryption process with a preliminary genetic crossover. Subsequently, a Vigenere cipher circuit is utilized, complemented by a secondary genetic crossover especially customized for image ciphering, implemented to ensure the resulting vector integrity. Extensive simulations have been carried out on diverse images with varying sizes and for-mats to assess the effectiveness of our approach against established attacks. These simulations serve to highlight the robustness of our method.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Nonlinear Control of a Bidirectional DC-DC Power Converter in V2H Applications
papers.abstract: The modelling and control of a reversible DC-to-DC energy converter for an electric vehicle-to-home (V2H) converter is the contribution of this work. The investigated power converter is a non-isolated single-stage structure connecting an electric vehicle's DC-driven high-voltage battery link. Initially, we developed a mathematical model for the system under study. Then, a controller that is not linear with double objectives is designed utilizing the backstepping technique: (i) adjusting the direct current (DC) link voltage as well as(ii) controlling the emptying of the battery (CC-CV controlling Ibat or SOC control). We have performed numerical simulations to evaluate the efficacy of the suggested command laws.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Optimizing Medium Access Protocols: A Comprehensive Node-Level Performance Analysis Exploring Network Density and Data Length
papers.abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) are made up of numerous small, low-power sensor nodes that work together to gather and transmit data from the environment. The successful operation of WSNs depends on robust communication protocols. Researchers have developed and evaluated numerous communication protocols and techniques aimed at offering reliable and energy-efficient communication in WSNs. Selecting an appropriate communication protocol and enhancing its performance for a certain application, on the other hand, remains a substantial challenge. In this study, we evaluate the performance of the IEEE 802.15.4 Carrier Sense Multiple Access with Collision Avoidance (CSMA/CA) protocol in a Contiki-based WSN application by measuring key Node-Level (NL) metrics like latency, Packet Delivery Ratio (PDR) and energy consumption under different network densities and with various data lengths. The results indicate the simulation-based network's usefulness in offering insights into the trade-offs between performance measures and different network setups. The findings help to understand WSN performance characteristics and may be used to drive the design and optimization of medium access protocols in real-world WSN deployments.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Particle Swarm Optimization with PID Controller for BLDC Motor Speed Control
papers.abstract: Brushless DC motors are becoming more and more common in the industry because of their high torque capability, quick speed, and low maintenance requirements. In this circumstance, the most effective solutions are typically in light of approaches that produce the parameters for the controller in every instance when a disturbance occurs, whether it is indoors or outdoors, adjusting the controller actions to nonlinear behavior. This paper focuses on a novel proposed strategy for PID speed regulation that is optimized using the Particle Swarm Optimization algorithm (PSO). It describes an intelligent algorithm for a BLDC motor created in MATLAB/SIMULINK. The simulation data confirmed the PSO controller's efficiency in terms of stability, quickness, precision, and torque variations.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Performance Improvement of Predictive Direct Power Control of Grid-Connected DC-AC Converter
papers.abstract: The growing need for renewable energies has resulted in their extensive integration into the grid. However, incorporating these systems comes with various challenges. These challenges encompass the preservation of grid stability, voltage regulation, ensuring grid protection, compliance with grid codes and standards and power control. This research study provides enhanced predictive direct power control (P-DPC) approach for grid-connected two-level inverter. In the suggested P-DPC, a novel sequence of voltage vectors is developed based on the angular position of the necessary inverter voltage vector instead of the grid voltage vector. As a result, low-order harmonics in AC currents are eliminated, and periodic oscillations in reactive power are alleviated with reduced switching losses. To assess the performance of the P-DPC approach, numerical simulations are carried out for steady-state and dynamic operation conditions where the improved P-DPC shows its superiority in terms of power oscillations, line current quality and switching losses when compared with the classical P-DPC.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Proteus ISIS Modeling and Experimental Validation of a Photovoltaic Tracer Based on Boost Converter
papers.abstract: The current-voltage tracer is helpful for analyzing and reproducing the characteristics of photovoltaic (PV) generators under various weather conditions. Therefore, it can be used in assessing the performance of PV panels and pinpointing any system issues. Several tracer topologies with inherent weaknesses exist in the literature, including increased circuit complexity and non-smooth characteristics. This paper suggests a straightforward approach to solve these problems by introducing an I-V tracer design using a boost converter, implemented using Proteus ISIS software. Afterward, experimental validation is conducted to confirm the simulation results. The key findings reveal a significant agreement between simulation and experimental results, demonstrating that the designed PV tracer enables smooth and accurate PV characteristics.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: RPL-Shield: A Deep Learning GNN-Based Approach for Protecting IoT Networks from RPL Routing Table Falsification Attacks
papers.abstract: Considering the growing threats within the domain of the Internet of Things (IoT), this research responds to the pressing need for protection measures that would help to prevent routing table falsification attacks. This research aims at improving IoT networks especially on the area of securing it against such prevalent attacks through a novel deep learning approach. The approach that has been developed is called SecureGuard and it uses the concept of Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) to provide dynamic analysis of evolving network structures. Thus, training SecureGuard on the labeled datasets containing both normal and attack scenarios shows its ability to effectively detect subtle signs of routing table falsification. The achievement of this proficiency sets up a new parameter for identifying, as early as possible, the possible threats on the way. The work provides an exhaustive demonstration of the performance and capabilities of SecureGuard during simulated attacks asserting on its capability of real-time identification and prompt countermeasure against various types of attacks, and proving the model’s high accuracy, precision and recall ratios for accurate distinction of abnormal network traffic and simulated attacks. It is, however, clear that SecureGuard could help to leverage deep learning technologies to improve the robustness of IoT network infrastructures in the face of new complex threats with proven applicability in real-world conditions are still yet to be validated). The results reported here clearly and immediately highlight the necessity of preparing early security measures in the context of IoT related issues, enabling a sound perception of the critical challenges of routing table falsification attacks and their extremely high potential to be detected and reacted to at a very brisk pace.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Steady-State Features of DFIM in Electrical Traction Systems
papers.abstract: This paper explores the potential integration of Doubly-Fed Induction Machines (DFIM) into a variable speed traction system by investigating the DFIM’s steady-state features and performances. The equivalent circuit is an indispensable tool for analyzing the steady-state characteristics of DFIM. Nevertheless, the existing steady-state model does not accurately analyze some special operation modes. To overcome the limitation, in this article, a complete DFIM drive model is developed under MATLAB environment using d-q dynamic model to verify the strength and significance of the proposed controller. Based on the synchronized mathematical model, analytic determination of the control laws is presented and illustrated by various figures to understand the effect of the applied rotor voltage on the torque and the efficiency. The motor performances with proposed rotor voltage control are compared to conventional asynchronous motor. This study shows that by combining the synchronous and asynchronous torques, the DFIM electromagnetic torque can be increased across a wide range of speeds and overload capabilities can be improved. This is an attractive and valuable feature for electric vehicle applications.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: The Digitalization of the Moroccan Administration and Its Impact on Performance: A Case Study of the Customs and Excise Administration
papers.abstract: This study analyzes how the Moroccan Customs and Excise Administration performs in light of digitization. This study intends to demonstrate how digitalization improves administrative procedures and service quality by looking at the integration of information and communication technologies (ICTs) and the ensuing structural changes. A substantial positive link between digitalization and the performance of the Customs and Excise Administration, which was demonstrated by structural changes, was discovered through an empirical analysis based on a sample of 212 people. These results highlight the vital role that ICTs play in enhancing administrative effectiveness and creating an atmosphere that is conducive to investment.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: The University and Territorial Entrepreneurial Ecosystem
papers.abstract: University education is receiving increasing attention in most developed and developing societies, as it is a strategic asset that nourishes society with all its human resources it needs to cope with the developmental burdens in various areas of life. Countries around the world are focusing on higher education institutions because their outcomes are the main driver of economic and social sectors. Training an educated person capable of participating effectively in the delivery of development programs is the path to global competition, especially with the increasing intensity of competition for the human capital-based knowledge economy. Many researchers have pointed out that entrepreneurship in higher education institutions in the modern era has become an urgent necessity due to its significant contribution to supporting and achieving sustainable competitive advantages. In this sense, the main objective of this intervention is to highlight the role of the university in creating real dynamism in order to advance the pace of business and create businesses. the proposed intervention will include an analytical and statistical study in order to identify the imbalances and problems that prevent the Moroccan university from playing its effective role in educating young people on the issue of the role of entrepreneurship in people's lives. Likewise, studying through a questionnaire will allow me to extract a set of ideas and opinions that can help create an atmosphere conducive to entrepreneurship.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: The Utilization of Artificial Intelligence and Deep Learning for Identifying Vehicle Wheels
papers.abstract: The technology using artificial intelligence (AI) allows, through the preprocessing of video and image data, their transformation into traffic information, the recognition in abusive conditions of elements, the specification of the different characteristics of the vehicle (type, brand, model, color), object detection and tracking, automatic calibration and positioning. Algorithms for performance evaluation can evaluate recognition quality and detect malfunctions due to external factors, reduced lighting conditions, or incorrect camera installation. This article discusses the application of AI and deep learning techniques to identify vehicle wheels. It highlights the preprocessing of image and video data to extract traffic information, detect vehicle attributes, and perform object tracking. The study focuses on understanding AI and convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for image processing, emphasizing the significance of feature extraction algorithms. The research outlines the methodology for creating a dataset of annotated wheel images, selecting the YOLO detection algorithm, and using the Darknet Github framework for training and testing CNNs. It discusses the training process, including configuration, data augmentation, and evaluation metrics like Mean Average Precision (MAP) and Intersection Over Union (IOU). Finally, the article concludes with insights into the practical applications of the developed wheel detection system, suggesting future improvements and potential integrations with other projects.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: UWB Slotted Rectangular Antenna Gain Enhancement Using AMC Metasurface Layer for Wireless Communication Devices
papers.abstract:
A compact slotted-Rectangular Shaped UWB antenna integrated AMC Metasurface has been discussed in this work. For the design, a slotted conventional patch antenna was printed on a low-cost Fr-4-epoxy material with the permittivity, 
                
                  
                
                $$\text{tan}\delta $$
                
               and thickness ‘h’ are 4.4, 0.02 and 1.6 mm respectively. The overall size of the proposed design is 24.39 × 22.64 mm2. AMC Metasurface unit cell was designed, and it provides two resonance frequencies at 4.91 GHz and 7.51 GHz. In addition, 3 × 3 AMC-MS layer was placed over the antenna in order to improve the radiation performance such as the radiation pattern and the gain. The studied metasurface integrated antenna shows an omnidirectional widespread radiation pattern, a wider bandwidth from 4 GHz to 8.75 GHz, a peak gain of 6.1dBi and a radiation efficiency of 82.34%. Based on those characteristics, the proposed structure validates its relevancy for wireless communication devices area.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Understanding the Attitude of Teacher Education Students Toward Utilizing ChatGPT as a Learning Tool: A Quantitative Analysis
papers.abstract: This study provides a comprehensive discussion regarding the use of ChatGPT as a learning tool among second-year students from the College of Teacher Education and will educate educational institutions, policymakers, and educators about how to effectively incorporate ChatGPT as a learning tool in CTE programs. In total of 225 respondents 183 (81.3%) females composed a large sample. The respondents of this study are pursuing courses in education, namely: Bachelor of Early Childhood Education (BECEd), Bachelor of Elementary Education (BEED), Bachelor of Secondary Education (BSED), Bachelor of Culture and Arts Education (BCAEd), and Bachelor of Special Needs Education (BSNEd). This study employed a quantitative research design. To ensure accurate data collection, the study adopted a research instrument [1] exhibiting a very good reliability score for interpretation of 0.93 Cronbach’s alpha. Furthermore, the collected data were subjected to independent sample T tests and Pearson correlation. The investigation yielded a moderately positive result regarding the use of ChatGPT as a learning tool in affective, cognitive, and behavioral components of the ATUC scale. Additionally, the study revealed that there is a significant relationship between the affective and cognitive component, cognitive and behavioral component, behavioral and affective component in the ATUC scale. Last, the results show that gender has a moderate agreement on the results and does not pose any significant difference. This study suggest that ChatGPT can be a valuable learning tool for teacher education students as it can positively impact their learning outcomes.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Understanding the Digital Frontier: Examining Privacy and Data Security in Desktop Web Browsers
papers.abstract: In an era where the privacy and security of web users’ data are paramount concerns for governments, practitioners, and cybersecurity researchers, the imperative need for fortified web browsers is evident. Serving as the primary interface for personal information entry, online transactions, and data exchange, web browsers play a pivotal role in the digital landscape. This paper conducts a comprehensive survey of privacy and security issues in desktop web browsers, alongside a detailed case study on their latest versions, to address three critical questions: (Q1) The security level of default browsers, (Q2) The extent of information extraction from user profiles during online browsing, and (Q3) Strategies for users to enhance online privacy and security. Additionally, the paper scrutinizes the performance of prominent desktop browsers - Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, Microsoft Edge, Opera, and Internet Explorer - against established privacy and security requirements. The case study’s findings reveal a consistent leakage of confidential user data in default browser versions. In response, users are urged to heighten security awareness, adhere to best practices for safe browsing, and augment default settings with additional security mechanisms to comprehensively shield their privacy.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Unraveling the Intersection of Artificial Intelligence and Idea Generation: A Systematic Literature Review
papers.abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) breakthroughs have revolutionized various fields. It is altering the ways businesses generate ideas by improving human creativity and problem-solving capabilities. This paper carries out an extensive literature review to explore the current state of the art of the most relevant trends addressing how artificial intelligence supports idea generation. The findings are split into two sections. The descriptive analysis section examines the evolution of AI-driven idea-generation research, the geographical distribution of research, key authors who have made significant contributions to the field, the top journals and conferences that publish in the issue, and the various research approaches used in the field. Furthermore, this review uses VOSviewer to map the keywords retrieved from the selected papers, revealing the interconnection and relationships between various research topics. The content analysis section, on the other hand, aims to provide a thorough comprehension of the literature. We identified three central themes: the role of semantic networks in AI-driven idea generation, the potential of AI in improving group discussions and brainstorming sessions, and the role of AI-based tools in idea generation (e.g. generative AI).
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Water Balance in Basins of the High Andean Zone of the Province of Cotopaxi
papers.abstract: To define the behavior and comprehensive management of water resources in the high Andean zone, it is necessary to understand their functioning from different points of view: hydrology, geology, hydrogeology, among others. This work presents a water balance as support for the comprehensive management of the Cutuchi sub-basin, in addition to the characterization of its main tributary the Pumancunchi where its main problem is incomplete information and the alteration of water dynamics due to anthropogenic activities such as agriculture, livestock, industrial and domestic areas. Applying surface-velocity, Penman Monteith and Infiltration methods, the results of flow rates, evapotranspiration, infiltration and surface runoff are presented. We found in the study area a flow rate of 3.1 m3/s in the dry season, evapotranspiration of 98.33 mm/year, infiltration of 58.1 mm/year, determining a water balance in the Cutuchi sub-basin, a surplus of 42.4 mm in the wet season, and a deficit in the dry period from 5.5 mm/month to 140.2 mm/month, identifying a variable water behavior where it is true that if the precipitation is less than the evapotranspiration, a deficit is obtained mainly in the months of May - October that belong to the dry season of the high Andean zone; which indicates a considerable increase in water deficit that would indicate droughts in the study area.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: A Comparative Study of Protocols’ Security Verification Tools: Avispa, Scyther, ProVerif, and Tamarin
papers.abstract: The proliferation of Internet and network-based services has experienced significant growth, leading to the creation of novel security protocols across many different systems. This includes communication protocols, authentication protocols, group key management schemes, etc. Nevertheless, the complicated structure of these protocols frequently poses challenges for humans to evaluate and validate them properly. To ensure the efficiency of these security protocols, it is crucial to examine and verify them thoroughly before deployment. Consequently, researchers have developed user-friendly automatic validation tools that generate easily comprehensible output. In this paper, we performed a comparative examination of the four most commonly used security analysis and verification tools in the literature: AVISPA, Scyther, ProVerif, and Tamarin. Gaining a comprehensive understanding of each tool’s distinct attributes, functionalities, domains of application, and constraints will enable researchers and professionals to make well-informed decisions when selecting the most suitable tool for their requirements.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: A Metaheuristic Algorithms for Optimization of Integrated Scheduling of Cranes and Storage Platforms at Automated Container Terminals
papers.abstract: This article focusses on the demanding situations that semi-automatic container terminals face, that specialize in APM Terminals in Tangier. We introduce our ‘System Health Check’ dashboard, that is a tool designed to display how properly the terminal’s devices are working. This is vital for fixing the problem of makespan (the time for handling a container), because it offers decision-makers stable statistics and real-time updates. This proactive method allows make operations smoother and decreases the time ships live withinside the port. We’ll additionally display how this dashboard suits with different studies geared toward enhancing global field terminals. This device is not only a one-off—it’s putting the degree for destiny enhancements. We’re making plans to apply a few algorithms to make it even better, so it could adapt to adjustments withinside the delivery enterprise and assist field ports run easily worldwide.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: A New Method for Identifying the Parameters of the Double Polarization Model of the Li-Ion Battery
papers.abstract: The article focuses on modeling the Li-ion cell and the requirements for identifying system parameters. A mathematical model is established representing the nonlinear system of our cell. A Double polarization (DP) model is adapted, which includes terms describing dynamic parameters such as state of charge (SOC), open-circuit voltage (Vocv), Rin, R1, C1, R2, and C2. Subsequently, appropriate battery discharge tests are conducted to identify the model parameters. Initially, the relationship between the SOC and the Vocv is established using the least squares method. The value of Rin is ascertained by cell discharge assays subsequent to the identification of this association. Next, R1, R2, C1, and C2 are determined by utilizing the least squares approach once more to determine the temporal constants τ1 and τ2. Once the different parameters are identified, the established battery model is verified through experimental tests. The obtained error indicates that the mean absolute error (MAE) for modeling is 0.0075 V, and the root mean square error (RMSE) is 0.0113 V. These results demonstrate the high accuracy of our cell model.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: A New Multi-objective Optimization Model for Optimal Configuration of CNNs
papers.abstract: In recent years, convolutional neural networks (CNNs) are compute-intensive learning models with growing applicability in a variety of real-world problems. However, determining an optimal CNN architecture is still unclear and needs mathematical development. Furthermore, most current CNN models are primarily crafted in a specific manner. This study introduces a novel multi-objective optimization model to find optimal hyperparameters related to CNN architecture. Our proposal simultaneously minimizes both cross-entropy loss and a related CNN complexity function. This model enables the fine control of each neuron’s contribution within the CNN’s fully connected layers through a set of associated decision variables. To solve the resulting model, we opt for the evolutionary multi-objective algorithm (NSGA-II). Extensive experiment on three benchmark datasets MNIST, SVHN, and NORB demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed approach.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Advanced Demand Forecasting and Pricing in Moroccan Auto Industry: A CNN-LSTM-Attention and Reinforcement Learning Approach
papers.abstract: This paper presents a novel forecasting model that combines Convolutional Neural Network-Long Short-Term Memory with an Attention mechanism (CNN-LSTM-Attention) and Reinforcement Learning (RL) to enhance impressive utilization in forecasting accuracy on demand for pricing optimization within auto parts industry in Morocco. The performance of the model was determined using Mean Squared Error (MSE) and R-squared (R2) as evaluation measures for prediction of demand accuracy while “Average Reward per Episode” was applied to measure how well pricing strategy works. The Application Programming Interface (API) utilized CNN-LSTM parameterization for direct spatial computation from images, Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) like sequential information processing and attended timestamps, which resulted in error rates below 1% while producing an R2 of around 90%. Regarding those metrics, it outperforms normal LSTMs or convolutional long short-term memory networks. By contrast, empirically Deep Q-Network (DQN) was more compatible than conventional Q-Learning when applied towards evolving pricing strategies over time leading up greater rewards and consistency amidst market volatilities. Market trend response was cores dependable, which resulted in significant readiness in supply chain operations. The present research work gives an example where smart predictive analysis can provide a comparative advantage.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Advanced State of Charge Estimation for Electric Vehicle Batteries Using Gradient Boosting and Random Forest Models
papers.abstract: The growing interest in artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) has increased the research and development of new methods to estimate the state of charge (SoC) of batteries in electrified vehicles. The accurate estimation of the state of charge (SoC) is essential to ensure the reliability and autonomy of electric vehicle (EV) batteries. In this article, a Random Forest algorithm and a Gradient Boosting algorithm have been used to estimate the SoC from experimental data obtained in the laboratory on a lithium-ion battery using a lithium-ion battery cell. This article presents a comparative study between the SoC measured experimentally and the SoC estimated considering the effect of temperature and various current profiles, so the battery can be faced with the diverse situations possible. Both models aim to demonstrate the ability of artificial intelligence to estimate the SoC. The performance of the Random Forest model appears to be excellent on the validation data, with a mean absolute error (MAE) of less than 0.3% and a coefficient of determination of 0.999. In contrast, the Gradient Boosting model gives a mean absolute error (MAE) value of 1% and a coefficient of determination of 0.997. The results demonstrate that the Random Forest model is the most accurate and reliable.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Agricultural Sector and Digital Transformation: An Explorative Research of Consumers and Producers Views of Authenticity
papers.abstract: The landscape of the business world is switching to the new normal post Covid 19 era. Therefore, the pandemic has accelerated the digital transformation and it has been added to the deep health crises in human history. In turn, it has disrupted the consumer-health relationship and it has reshaped the digital transformation practices. This research contributes to knowledge by exploring how digital transformation affects the practices in the agricultural sector. Regarding the key determinants of authenticity’s definition in post Covid 19, it considers the different and conflicting views from consumers and producers in the same time. This essay infers that the authenticity could represent a flexible, controversial and especially fluid concept. Findings underline that digital transformation of the agricultural sector needs to be shaped by a digital that emphasize the social reality of consumption. In this sense, a deep understanding of local environment provides a value for the technology and strategy for bridging the authenticity gap.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Application of Artificial Intelligence for Efficient Tumor Volume Segmentation of Lung Cancers in the 18FDG PET-CT Modality
papers.abstract: Broncho-pulmonary cancer (BPC) remains the primary cause of morbidity from malignant tumors in Tunisia. By being the first killer of all cancers, bronchial cancer bears witness to a disastrous and bleak prognosis that presents a major public health problem worldwide. The diagnosis of cancer is largely based on indicators reflecting the stage of the tumor. Various medical imaging modalities are indicated in the chest scan, which is an important diagnostic tool in 18F-FluoroDeoxy-Glucose Positron Emission Tomography with Computed Tomography (FDG PET-CT) imaging. A completely automated diagnostic system’s key means of detection is the accurate segmentation of cancer lesions in PET-CT images. The main objective of this work is to develop and test a deep learning algorithm (U-Net) for the detection of lung tumors of all stages in the FDG-PET/CT modality. The aim of this research is to develop an algorithm for the segmentation of malignant lung tumors using a convolutional neural network called U-Net. To enhance its efficiency, the transfer-learning technique is applied. The proposed network is distinguished by its efficient segmentation approach, which utilizes lightweight filtering to reduce computation and pointwise convolution to build additional features. In U-Net, skip connections were established with the “Relu” activation function to better model convergence, connecting the encoder layers to the decoder layers and allowing for the concatenation of feature maps with different resolutions. Additionally, the proposed model was trained and tested on the dataset obtained from the nuclear medicine department of the Institute of Salah Azaïez (ISA). The results showed an F-measure of 73.55%, accuracy of 99.99%, precision of 83.62%, and specificity of 99.99%. It is worth noting that the presented approach outperformed existing networks that require multiple phases of training and testing.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Application of IoT Technologies Oriented to a Vehicle Safety System
papers.abstract: This paper describes the development of a security and tracking system applied to vehicles, integrating technological advances based on the Internet of Things (IoT). The proposed solution employs low-cost and open source hardware, generating vehicle locking and unlocking functions. A dual approach is presented: control via radio frequency for immediate interventions and commands via a Telegram bot, the latter with Google Maps integration for precise location.
