Radio frequency guarantees responses of 2 to 3 s depending on the distance of action, while the use of Telegram offers an average range of 12.3 s with a location tracking accuracy of up to 2.4 m, being suitable for urban environments and large roads. Research shows that the effective range of RF control is up to 15 m, with signal reduction beyond this distance due to environmental and physical factors. Even so, the system remains effective and operational for theft prevention and emergency management in vehicles, standing out as an essential tool in contemporary vehicle security.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Artificial Neural Network for Enhancing Supply Chain Risk Management
papers.abstract: The importance of supply chain (SC) resilience and performance has been underscored in response to recent disruptions caused by pandemics and other crises. Furthermore, there is a growing recognition of the importance of developments in information processing techniques, notably artificial intelligence (AI), in supporting supply chain risk management (SCRM) as the SC continues to expand towards digitalization, integration, and globalization. Modern organizations need to handle risks within the SC effectively. By examining and utilizing this information, companies can pinpoint potential risks and opportunities for improvement. Consequently, an effective approach is necessary for managing SC data and providing precise predictions to support decision-making. This paper introduces an artificial neural network (ANN) as a predictive tool for risk management within the SC. The ANN model is trained using real-world data obtained from a global automotive manufacturer. Demonstrating strong performance on a dataset comprising 9200 samples, the model exhibits notable accuracy in risk prediction, as indicated by the low mean square error (MSE). The proposed model excels in identifying potential risks within the SC and provides excellent decision support by effectively recognizing potential dangers within the SC. These results significantly improve the SC's capacity for risk prediction, which provides invaluable support for business operations decision-making.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Artificial Neural Network for Extraction and Analysis of Consumer Opinions in Tourism
papers.abstract: Exploring opinions from written or orally expressed data in sentiment classification has become an important decision-making tool that helps companies improve their profits using various machine-learning methods. In this article, we introduce a new method, termed the combined approach, for performing sentiment analysis on consumer opinions it uses a hybrid Artificial Neuron Network ANN algorithm which combines PSO swarm particle optimization and PB backpropagation. The results obtained by the Artificial Neural Network model developed by the PSO-PBB algorithm show very good prediction, better performance, and convergence.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Bibliometric Analysis of Artificial Intelligence Applications in Asset Pricing
papers.abstract: The demand for advanced financial solutions has promoted the phenomenal growth of the Artificial Intelligence (AI)-driven finance industry. This article covers the progress of AI-based asset pricing. While conventional models created the framework for understanding asset risk and return, current advancements in AI and Machine Learning (ML) have made it feasible to build more complicated models that can evaluate enormous amounts of data, find trends, and improve investment methods. This work aims to perform a bibliometric study of asset pricing models powered by AI. This study analyzes 154 papers from the Scopus database using VOSviewer and shows a considerable rise in publishing during 2018. The study includes author impact, co-authorship networks, magazine participation, country contribution, and keyword co-occurrence. In terms of publications, the United States leads, demonstrating its strength in technology progress and financial area. The United Kingdom and China are closely following. To highlight authorship influence, we underscore the fact that a majority of authors contributed with only a single work. Co-citation research gives extra light on author cooperation practices. The keyword analysis shows the importance of “Machine learning” and “Financial markets,” showing the academic community’s strong desire to apply modern computing methods for financial data and market dynamics. The obtained insights are meant to serve as a guide for future study in the growing field of AI-driven asset pricing models, helping both scholars and practitioners.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Boosting Octree Color Quantization Descriptor for Content Based Image Retrieval: A Case Study
papers.abstract: Color quantization (CQ) plays a crucial role in image processing with reducing the number of colors in an image or of bins in a color histogram. This operation is particularly important in Content-Based Image Retrieval (CBIR) when using color histogram-based descriptors. Among various image processing techniques, the Octree tree data structure stands out as a powerful tool for performing CQ. By manipulating two key parameters, namely the number of colors K and the depth of the tree D, diverse outcomes can be achieved with the Octree. In this study, multiple experiments were conducted to determine the optimal values of K and D that yield the highest precision for the Color Octree Quantization Descriptor (COQD). COQD is a descriptor that utilizes the Octree data structure to quantize images and subsequently extracts features from them based on a color string coding (CSC). The experiments undertaken in this case study demonstrate enhancements in precision.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Central Bank Digital Currencies in the Global Economy: Lessons from China's Digital Yuan for the Development of the European Central Bank's Digital Euro
papers.abstract: The primary objective of this article is to provide a comprehensive examination of the Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC), its potential benefits and the risks it might pose to traditional banking systems. To fulfil this objective, the article offers a holistic analysis of Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC), examining risks inherent in traditional banking and the merits of CBDC adoption. It delves into the motivation behind increasing CBDC adoption by central banks and outlines various experimental programs. It mainly explores the digitalization of the Euro and the transformative potential of CBDCs on aspects like financial inclusion and monetary policy. Included within is an exploration of the wholesale CBDC with Bank of France followed by a practical example of the digital yuan within the Chinese economy. The research methodology encompasses a rigorous literature review, analysis of the research context, and retrieval of pertinent studies. The results and discussions section reveal critical findings and potential global financial impacts of CBDC. The article concludes with prospects and challenges of CBDC implementation, underlining its importance in financial systems’ evolution.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Combining Lean Tools and 4.0 Technologies: Application in a Logistic Warehouse
papers.abstract: Integrating Lean principles with Industry 4.0 technologies offers an inventive approach to boost Supply Chain performance by reducing waste and improving processes. This combination is bound to deliver significant benefits in terms of productivity and profitability. To ensure the approach’s success, a thorough deployment methodology is essential. This involves careful needs assessment, judicious selection of suitable technologies, strategic planning and a carefully coordinated pilot phase to validate outcomes and ensure a smooth transition to large-scale deployment. By combining these efforts, it is possible to make meaningful changes to supply chains, by aligning operational practice with modern industry requirements
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Communication in Virtual Teams: A Conceptual Model for Effective Use of Technology
papers.abstract: Communication is one of the most important challenges for virtual teams (VTs). Interaction and collaboration among these teams occur through information and communication technologies. Generation gap between members who are not digital natives and their young teammates, may lead to different perceptions regarding the use of technology. Moreover, selected technological tools and functionalities should be appropriate to the organizational context, team objectives, and tasks at hand. Based on a literature review of task-technology fit (TTF) and technology acceptance model (TAM), a conceptual model was elaborated for the context of VTs. This proposed model, which integrates TAM and TTF, aims to put forth a framework for improving the use of technological tools by online team members. This work contributes to the theoretical knowledge by highlighting the relationship between virtual collaboration context, team members’ attitudes and behaviors, and the performance impact in terms of technology adoption and use. It can also be used, in practice, to leverage the use of the technological tools within VTs. Additional studies are needed to test the proposed model in various virtual contexts.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Comparative Analysis of Multiclass Classification Machine Learning Models for Cybersecurity Intrusion Detection
papers.abstract: Recently, the use of the Internet and computer networks in general, has increased exponentially, leading to a high demand for cybersecurity to protect against all kinds of network attacks that are constantly evolving. Nowadays, Machine Learning (ML) is implemented in various cybersecurity tools, Intrusion Detection System-based ML brings more capabilities to improve the detection of cyber attacks. This study aims to perform a comparative analysis of a multiclass classification problem for cybersecurity attack detection. The comparative analysis is performed using six different machine learning algorithms: Naïve Bayes, Decision Tree, Random Forest, Support Vector Machine, eXtreme Gradient Boosting, and Multi-Layer Perceptron, which are applied to the full NSL-KDD dataset, and three other subsets of datasets to confirm and verify the findings in terms of precision, accuracy, training time, and testing time. In all dataset subsets we worked on in addition to the NSL-KDD dataset, eXtreme Gradient Boosting significantly beat the other algorithms. From all the experimental results, it is concluded that XGBoost is a plausible choice for an intrusion detection system in terms of all the metrics compared to the other ML algorithms discussed.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Comparative Analysis of U-Net with Transfer Learning and Attention Mechanism for Enhanced Medical Image Segmentation
papers.abstract: Medical image segmentation is an important component in computer-aided diagnosis and treatment. In this work, we study the application of the U-Net architecture to dermoscopy image segmentation, the former being a crucial stage in any algorithm attempting to detect and classify skin lesions. To enhance the segmentation task, we address two different approaches: we propose a novel method by integrating Attention gates inside the U-Net architecture (Attention U-Net), and we perform Transfer Learning for the encoder part of the U-Net using an array of pre-trained models leveraging ISIC-2017 dataset. The experimental configuration consists in a comparison between the common U-Net, the Attention U-Net and five Transfer Learning adaptations that each one uses one pre-trained model (VGG16, VGG19, MobileNetV2, ResNet50 and EfficientNetB0) in the encoder. The importance of Transfer Learning is highlighted through a thorough evaluation based on three key metrics: Loss, Intersection over Union (IoU), and Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC). The DSC scores demonstrate superior segmentation performance of the enhanced architectures across benchmark analyses compared to the baseline U-Net model. Compared to other Transfer Learning variants, U-Net+MobileNetV2 is the overall best performer with a high DSC of 89.75%. It outperforms the respect DSC values of U-Net+ResNet50 (85.15%), U-Net+VGG19 (84.56%), U-Net+VGG16 (83.71%), and U-Net+EfficientNetB0 (50.44%). Additionally, due to the Attention Mechanism added to U-Net architecture, the DSC increases to 52.46%. These results collectively underline the efficacy of Transfer Learning, particularly with MobileNetV2, in substantially improving segmentation accuracy, as measured by the DSC metric.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Contribution of the Evaluation of Infection Control Committees to the Development of a Computerized Infection Risk Management Tool
papers.abstract: This study evaluates the nosocomial infection control committee (NICC) with a view to new governance, organisational performance and hospital efficiency. To this end, we conducted action research in university hospital of Marrakesh with the objective of identifying the organisational shortcomings and deficiencies of the nosocomial infection control committee (NICC). A critical analysis of the results using the NVivo software revealed that the root of these dysfunctions lies mainly in the lack of management of information flows and coordination between the various players on the committee, as well as the under-use of information systems in decision-making. In anticipation of the results of this study, we have proposed the development of a computer tool for managing infectious risks in the operating theatre, given its high frequency as a preliminary step. To this end, we conducted a collective project, which was enriched by proposals and recommendations from the future medical and nursing team of the university hospital. This was done as part of a participatory and collegial governance approach, with the objective of establishing from the outset a comprehensive, functional and scalable software.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Digital Transformation in Morocco Public Health Sector: Survey of the Telemedicine in the Fez-Meknes Region
papers.abstract: As digital transformation has grown significantly globally, all sectors are affected. The health sector is also concerned by the use of several forms of digitalization. Among the forms of digitalization in the health sector, we find telemedicine procedures. Our article seeks to study the situation of telemedicine as a form of digital transformation in a specific region, that of Fez-Meknes in Morocco. This issue has not been previously addressed in the region, knowing that it is of great magnitude. The results show that telemedicine is increasingly being used and could help patients access care more easily.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Dynamic Computing of Discrete Tchebichef Polynomials
papers.abstract:
Discrete Tchebichef moments are shape descriptors used in approximation theory; they provide optimal approximation for certain types of functions, such as images and signals, and they are used in computer vision for image representation and pattern recognition. However, the high dimensions of images may limit the use of these moments because of their high computational cost. There is a need for advanced algorithms to calculate these moments for higher orders efficiently. In this paper, we discuss the time complexity of the recursive relation of Tchebichef polynomials and propose the use of memoization, which is a dynamic programming technique to speed up the computation by storing the results of Tchebichef function calls. These values can be later used when needed, reducing time and memory costs. Time complexity has been reduced from O
                
                  
                
                $$\left({2}^{n}\right)$$
                
               to linear time O
                
                  
                
                $$(n)$$
                
              , which is the best possible time complexity. Additionally, we presented various measurements, including a time cost comparison, the calculated nodes, the percentage of nodes calculated using the recursive and dynamic approaches, and finally the time cost comparison of the proposed dynamic approach and a state-of-the-art algorithm.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: E-learning and Augmented Reality: A Systematic Literature Review
papers.abstract: This comprehensive systematic literature review delves into exploring how augmented reality (AR) technologies can significantly enhancethe e-learning landscape. The objective is to understand the multifaceted impact of AR integration and scrutinize the current state of research, prevalent theories, and practical applications within e-learning. The study examines the dynamic interplay between AR and e-learning, spanning prevalent research trends and theoretical frameworks. The review seeksto shed light on the tangible benefits that AR brings to the e-learning environment. The results of this investigation underscore the emergence of augmented reality as an up-and-coming and impactful tool in the domain of e-learning. One of its key strengths lies in its capacity to amplify learner engagement. The culmination of the review involves providing valuable recommendations for future research endeavors. These recommendations guide scholars and researchers in exploring new dimensions, addressing potential gaps, and advancing the understanding of AR's role in e-learning.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Educational Assignment Sources: Data Collection Challenges
papers.abstract: Data collection is a crucial step in various fields, which helps to obtain valuable insights and make better decisions. However, finding an open dataset that matches a user’s needs is one of the biggest challenges, particularly with advances in artificial intelligence that depend on data for algorithms. In the educational field, assignments are essential in virtual and traditional environments. In this paper, we aim to address the lack of educational datasets, particularly for assignments, which on the one hand help teachers evaluate learners’ progress. On the other hand, they help learners apply and practice their understanding as an intermediate between theoretical knowledge and practical application. Furthermore, the data search process applied was based on keywords as a search style to achieve this objective.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Energy Sovereignty: What Role Can Digitalization Play?
papers.abstract: Digital technologies play a vital role for all businesses around the world, as digital (big database) and information enable better predictions and decisions. Here we are talking about a system that works autonomously and faster, with reduced costs and the possibility of connecting to networks in conjunction with supply chains. In this regard, you can imagine the effective role that digitalization will play in a new and sensitive area like that of renewable energies which has become the topic of conversation of the hour in the world through giant projects (On transmission networks and distribution, or small projects on the roofs of houses, shops and others). Digital transformation has broken through one of the widest doors in the renewable energy industry. For example, digitalization tools and platforms help build renewable energy plants with automated processes to make informed decisions such as remote control of photovoltaic and wind farms. Additionally, digitalization reduces downtime and provides alerts based on predictive maintenance and anticipation of facility maintenance. In this sense, the proposed intervention will try to analyze the role of digitalization in the field of renewable energies. In addition, an axis will be dedicated to an empirical study via a questionnaire on Google forms, and using SPSS software to enrich this work and give reliability to the results and findings mentioned.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Enhancing Battery State of Charge Estimation Through a Combination of Extended Kalman Filter and Artificial Neural Network
papers.abstract: In the field of battery management and state estimation, accurately determining the initial state of charge (SOC) is a necessary yet difficult task. The Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) is an effective technique for estimating the battery state of charge; nevertheless, its accuracy can be considerably impacted by the initial battery charge state (initSOC), especially if it differs significantly from the real state of charge. This disparity has the potential to cause problems such as sluggish convergence or, in more extreme cases, full divergence in the estimation process. To solve this, we propose an innovative approach in this paper that uses 1D Convolutional Neural Networks (1D CNN) to improve the EKF’s initial state estimate.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Enhancing Data Security Through Hybrid Cryptographic Techniques: XOR and RSA Integration for RGB Image Encryption
papers.abstract: A popular solution is cryptography, which is a reliable and secure data protection technique due to the rise in information access terminals and telecommunications. XOR and RSA cryptosystems are used in this study to present a data protection tool that is popular. In this method, an XOR function is executed between a randomly generated mask and the four channels of an RGB image (alpha, red, green, and blue), then RSA encryption is utilized. The results prove that the method is effective.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Factors Influencing Healthcare Professionals’ Satisfaction with the Use of Teleconsultation: The Case of Al Haouz Provincial Hospital
papers.abstract: E-health, including teleconsultation, offers opportunities to improve patient care in terms of accessibility, efficiency and quality. The aim of this study was to determine the factors influencing healthcare professionals’ satisfaction with the use of teleconsultation. To this end, a questionnaire was distributed online to healthcare professionals participating in teleconsultation at the Al Haouz Hospital. Analysis of the variables quality of service, quality of information and quality of the system, showed that 47.7% of respondents expressed a high level of satisfaction, while 53.3% of respondents expressed an average level of satisfaction with the use of teleconsultation. However, there was no significant influence of healthcare professionals’ characteristics on their satisfaction with teleconsultation.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Fixed-Time Disturbance Observer-Based Adaptive Super Twisting Control for MPPT Tracking of Wind Energy Conversion System
papers.abstract: In this paper, a control scheme based on adaptive super twisting controller (ASTC) combined with a fixed-time disturbance observer (FTDO) is proposed for a wind energy conversion system (WECS) based on a permanent magnet synchronous generator (PMSG). The main objective of this controller is to track the maximum power point (MPPT) and ensure that the system operates with good performance despite the presence of known and unknown external disturbances. Firstly, the ASTC control is designed to perform optimal speed tracking of WECS based on PMSG without a priori information on the upper limit of system disturbances and uncertainties. Secondly, a fixed-time torque observer is designed to estimate aerodynamic torque without any additional sensors. Finally, simulation in the MATLAB/Simulink environment is carried out to validate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed controller. Simulation results indicate that the ASTC-FTDO control offers a good tradeoff between extreme robustness, MPPT operation accuracy and low chattering effect.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: From Data to Decision: Exploring Machine Learning’s Impact on Shaping Smart Cities
papers.abstract: In today’s era of rapid urbanization, smart cities are emerging as a beacon of efficiency and sustainability. At the heart of this evolution is the integration of Machine Learning (ML), which can transform vast streams of data into informed decisions. This article examines through a literature review the transformative potential of machine learning in urban environments, highlighting advances and challenges in the key smart city domains: Infrastructure, Environment, Mobility and Transport. The review highlights both the promising breakthroughs achieved and the challenges ahead in this interdisciplinary field. Our analysis aims to provide readers with a comprehensive understanding of how machine learning not only improves data processing capabilities, but also leads to actionable and impactful decisions in the urban context. By tracing the path from raw data to informed urban solutions, this paper highlights the central role of machine learning in shaping the smart cities of the future.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Image Denoising: A Comparative Study of Convolutional Neural Networks
papers.abstract: Image processing is a vigorous area of study that utilizes various algorithms to manipulate, analyze, and enhance digital images. Image denoising is one of the crucial applications of image processing. Still, the occurrence of image noise is inevitable due to various sources, including low light conditions, high ISO settings, and transmission artifacts, necessitating the availability of denoising techniques to significantly improve visual image quality. This is particularly important in fields such as computer vision, medical imaging and remote sensing. Not only does it facilitate image analysis by retaining important details, but it also optimizes the performance of compression algorithms, improves storyteller detection. In this project, we propose an in-depth study of image denoising, focusing on the use of convolutional neural networks (CNNs). The problem of Gaussian noise will be treated by applying different levels of σ (low σ = 15, medium σ = 25, and high σ = 50). During this project, a full comparative analysis will be made with the three main CNN architectures: DnCNN, RIDNet, and IRCNN, illustrative of the quantitative and qualitative experimental results obtained by these different approaches. In fact, these approaches have shown impressive performance in image processing tasks, including image denoising, since they used different techniques that can be adopted in CNN, such as regularization methods, batch normalization, and residual learning.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Impacts of Digitalization on Human Resources Health in the Banking Sector in Morocco
papers.abstract: Digitalization is being used more and more in the service industry, which is leading the way in the digital revolution. Among the major participants in this shift, financial institutions are notable for their extensive use of technology. Nevertheless, the risks of computer malfunctions are greater in credit institutions than in other industries, even with all the benefits these instruments have to offer. This study looks at how digitization has affected the state of human resources in Morocco's banking industry. A quantitative strategy was used for this investigation, emphasizing detailed descriptive statistical analyses. There were 345 questionnaires distributed in total; 228 of those were collected and used in the study. The findings demonstrate that banking personnel are at risk for health issues due to excessive technology use. Moreover, important technological hazards in Moroccan banks include system failures, slow networks, and disruptions in connectivity, all of which have a substantial negative impact on the mental health of human resources at their workplace. This study emphasizes how important it is for banking organizations to create practical plans to control these hazards and protect the health of their staff members.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Improved YOLOv5 with Backbone Replacement to MobileNetv3 for Weapons Detection: Application for Smart Video Surveillance
papers.abstract: In the field of computer vision technology recent advancements have made improvements, in object detection impacting domains such as transportation and security surveillance. One important use case involves the real time identification of weapons for law enforcement and monitoring purposes. This study focuses on comparing the performance of two object detection models; YOLOv5s and MobileNetv3 YOLOv5s, based on factors like accuracy, computational efficiency and parameter count. Our models were trained using the Sohas Weapon Dataset and Gun Movies Dataset to address orientations and lighting conditions. The main objective was to optimize the models for efficiency while maintaining an Average Precision (mAP). The results indicate a reduction in parameters, weight and GFLOPs; however, there was also a decrease, in mAP and recall metrics.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Innovations in the Control of an Intelligent Electronic Scoreboard Using IoT
papers.abstract: The present work is focused on the development of an application for the control of electronic scoreboards in sporting events within an educational institution using IoT technology based on low-cost Arduino and ESP8266 devices. The goal is to solve the identified drawbacks of traditional systems such as intermittent keypad connections and lack of remote control. A Telegram bot is implemented that facilitates remote control of the scoreboard from anywhere with Internet access, thus having significant advantages in terms of ease of use, configuration and the possibility of receiving real-time notifications. System response times of between 4.83 and 5.00 s are obtained depending on the Internet connection and the command executed. It is highlighted that this solution provides significant benefits in efficiency, cost savings and the possibility of remote diagnostics, which highlights its effectiveness and practicality for the modernization of educational and sports facilities.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Is Artificial Intelligence a “Learning Material” in Higher Education? Students Perception on Using Artificial Intelligence on Language Learning
papers.abstract: In the expansion of artificial intelligence technology, a few is shed to light of its use for learning of a language in the Philippine setting. Push-Pull Mooring Habit (PPMH) Theoretical Framework was adapted to analyze the perception of students in using chatbots in higher education. This study used a descriptive-quantitative research design with a 12-item survey questionnaire consisting of three different aspects to measure the perception of students in terms of Performance Expectancy, Effort Expectancy, and Social Influence. Artificial intelligence is being used in the educational field as well as by organizations and governments. However, there is not much known research on artificial intelligence usage in higher education for language learning. The result suggest that the students are ready to adapt artificial intelligence in their English language learning.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: MOERID: Design and Implementation of a GPT-3-Based Generative Chatbot for Personalized Open Educational Resource Recommendations in Teaching
papers.abstract: The integration of technology into education has produced major changes in teaching and learning. Open Educational Resources (OER) have played a role in this transformation enabling educators to enhance course development by utilizing existing educational materials. However the main challenge lies in locating OERs that meet the users’ educational needs and goals. To tackle this issue we introduce MOERID, a chatbot powered by GPT 3 that offers recommendations for OERs. MOERID integrates GPT 3 for its natural language processing (NLP) abilities utilizing deep learning algorithms to comprehend context and produce text with minimal adjustments. Key features of MOERID include an interface, customized recommendation systems, scalability and continuous learning mechanisms to ensure performance and user satisfaction when delivering tailored OER suggestions. This chatbot is designed to assist educators in streamlining the process of searching for resources so they can focus on enhancing the learning journey. The study investigates the context and challenges of OER use, gives an overview of Language Models (LLMs), and describes the MOERID implementation, with a focus on integrating NLP and machine learning technologies. This research contributes to advance AI-powered educational tools by providing actionable recommendations for future research.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Maximizing Wind Energy Efficiency Through ACO and ANN-Based MPPT Algorithms
papers.abstract: The wind energy sector is experiencing continuous improvement aimed at increasing the exploitation of this type of energy by optimizing its energy efficiency as much as possible. The energy efficiency of wind power systems is study by evaluating two MPPT algorithms is presented. The objective focuses on two approaches: The use of artificial neural networks (ANN) and the ant colony optimization (ACO) algorithm. The adaptability and learning capabilities inherent in these methods and their characteristics is explained. This study aims to provide a performance analysis of these various MPPT algorithms and evaluate their accuracy in tracking the maximum power point, their effectiveness in reducing energy losses and their stability as well as improving energy efficiency.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Optimization of Acoustic Wave Transmission in a One-Dimensional System Using the Parallelepiped-Shaped Local Copper Patch Resonator
papers.abstract: The transfer matrix method (TMM) is used to study the transmission of acoustic waves in a one-dimensional system, such as a guide tube of length d1. To improve the performance of this system, a local parallelepiped resonator (patch resonator) of dimensions L × h is added to the outer surface of the tube. By using a copper patch, we’ve created a resonant environment that effectively absorbs sound waves at specific frequencies. The main objective was to obtain frequency stop modes for a specific frequency. They also show how various changes can impact the position of modes in the relation between patch height and length. Specifically, we found that increasing patch length causes these modes to move to lower frequencies, while the opposite effect is observed as patch height increases, also, the material of the patch plays an important role. This work has numerous applications in many fields, such as industrial noise control, which offers the possibility of developing more effective attenuation methods. For communication devices, the study helps improve sound quality, facilitating clearer voice communication.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Optimization of Power System Operation Using Battery Energy Storage Systems
papers.abstract: This paper presents an optimization model to investigate the effects of incorporating Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) into power system operation. The proposed optimization model solves the power economic dispatch problem, minimizing power system operational costs; the model considers the operation of BESS, network constraints, transmission losses, and other factors, and is based on AC power flows. To evaluate the performance of the proposed optimization model, the 14-bus IEEE test system was used. The model was solved using the software General Algebraic Modeling System (GAMS), considering different load demand scenarios and a 24-h period. The simulation results indicate that when BESS with capacities of 80 MW y 50 MW were incorporated into the test system, the system operational costs diminished from USD 196,675.53 to USD 188,656.58 (which represents savings of USD 8,018.95 during a day), and that BESS operation lasted on average 9 h. Furthermore, the results indicate that incorporating BESS into power systems is feasible, and that they can enhance power system operation.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Optimization of a Grid-Connected PV System for a Moroccan Industrial Unit Using PVsyst
papers.abstract: This paper investigates the feasibility of integrating a grid-connected PV system for an industrial factory to provide clean energy using PVsyst software. The PV system aims to reduce the use of the distribution grid when load demand reaches its peak value. The system’s sizing is done based on theoretical and simulation methods. Results show that the proposed system can cover 80% of the daily consumed energy. The performance ratio of the system is 0.84 while the energy injected into the grid is 362 MWh per year.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Optimizing Wind Farm Performance in Northern Morocco with Weibull Distribution
papers.abstract: To optimize wind farms in northern Morocco, a validated approach based on the two-parameter Weibull distribution is used to assess the wind potential of a wind farm in TETOUAN over one year. With a total capacity of 120 MW and 40 turbines of 3 MW each, the maximum likelihood method is chosen to estimate the Weibull parameters, utilizing wind measurements taken every 10 min. Recalculated power densities, compared to actual measurements, reduce forecast errors, highlighting significant deficiencies in turbines WTG 30 and WTG 33 due to orientation issues. In conclusion, the use of Weibull and rigorous statistical analysis optimizes the performance of wind farms, crucial for the sustainable exploitation of wind energy in the region.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Overview of Electric Vehicles Charging Technologies
papers.abstract: The paper discusses the various electric vehicle (EV) charging technologies in the context of smart cities. It begins with a representation of battery swap stations (BSS), where exhausted batteries are exchanged with fully or partially charged ones. However, this technique has its limits, which must be addressed by looking for other alternative solutions. One of them is the second method discussed, conductive charging, employed in plug-in vehicle applications, relying on a physical connection between the vehicle and the charging equipment. This method can deliver either AC or DC current based on the charger topology, i.e., on-board or off-board charger. The evolution of smart mobility has given rise to wireless charging, with inductive charging being another choice in EV applications, facilitating energy transfer through electromagnetic fields. The paper also provides an overview of standards and guidelines for managing both conductive and inductive charging. These measures ensure the safety, interoperability, and efficiency of the charging process. Taking into consideration the advantages and disadvantages of each charging method, it highlights how crucial it is for manufacturers and authorities to follow standards in order to ensure that EV users have a smooth charging experience.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Overview of Smart Parking: Types, Sensors Technologies and Energy Efficiency Strategies
papers.abstract: As we realize today, especially in metropolitan cities, locating a proper parking space for a car is the biggest hassle. The time wasted searching for a parking space amplifies the already existing problems of congestion and leads to consumption of an unnecessary amount of fuel. This problem can be solved by using advanced communication technology known as smart parking systems. These systems use sophisticated sensing techniques to inform drivers, in real terms, of the availability of parking spaces. This paper presents a comprehensive study of various smart parking elements with a focus on the sensing side of smart parking systems that helps in detecting the presence of vehicles in various parking situations, it also clearly defines the classification of parking spots by differentiating between open-air and enclosed facilities, and highlight methods to reduce sensor energy consumption in order to ensure the smart parking infrastructure operates sustainably and effectively.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Pedagogical Synergy: Effective Integration of ICT in the Flipped Classroom
papers.abstract: The use of information and communication technologies (ICT) in the flipped classroom is covered in the article, with an emphasis on how it improves student performance and engagement. More flexibility is provided by encouraging students to participate actively in distance learning activities. According to the findings, the flipped classroom approach outperforms conventional techniques in terms of performance, primarily because it makes use of customized instructional videos. The essay highlights the requirement of tailoring instruction to each student's needs and calls for more study in the area of ICT and education to enhance teaching methods and student outcomes.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Pre-service Elementary Teachers’ Attitudes Toward the Use of Mobile Learning in Elementary Science Education
papers.abstract: The present study aimed to examine the pre-service elementary teachers’ attitudes in Morocco toward integrating mobile learning in science education. Convenience sampling was the method used to select the research sample. Data collected through a 15-item questionnaire was distributed to 340 pre-service teachers of the Bachelor of Education Elementary Specialty (BEES). The data were analyzed with statistical techniques including mean scores and Mann-Whitney tests. The results indicated that the pre-service teachers had positive attitudes toward the use of mobile learning in science education and that these attitudes were not dependent on the teachers’ gender, educational level, or pre-university academic background. The results also show that Moroccan pre-service elementary teachers would feel uncertain about the ability of their future children to use mobile devices in science learning.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: RETRACTED CHAPTER: Artificial Intelligence and Supply Chain Management: Implications for the Smart Cities of Tomorrow
papers.abstract: This article explores the concept of smart cities by examining the literature review, research methodology, and associated findings and discussions. The literature review sheds light on the emergence of smart cities in response to the challenges posed by rapid urbanization and global competition. The methodology adopted is based on convenience sampling, mobilizing 267 professionals involved in smart cities in Morocco. The results of the multinomial regression reveal that the age variable plays a significant role in the efficiency of smart cities, highlighting the importance of designing urban environments that are inclusive and adapted to the specific needs of each generation. The practical implications of these findings suggest that understanding age-specific influences is crucial for a successful and balanced implementation of smart city initiatives, thus guiding targeted interventions in areas such as health, education, and marketing.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Relationship Between Arabic Reading Skills and Phonological Awareness in the First Level of Primary School
papers.abstract: This study examines the educational effects of developing phonological awareness at an early stage among children learning the Arabic language and their subsequent implications on the quality of the reading act. The study sample consisted of 83 learners at the first level of primary school from three educational institutions affiliated with the Regional Directorate of Sefrou, whose ages ranged from 66 to 76 months, with an average of 70 months, and a standard deviation of 2.75. The study relied on the descriptive, correlational approach in a longitudinal follow-up study. The results of the study showed that children who have developed phonological awareness do not have major difficulties recognizing letters, words, and sentences. While children who have difficulties in representing and manipulating the sounds of spoken language are more likely to encounter difficulties in learning to read in Arabic.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Retraction Note to: Artificial Intelligence and Supply Chain Management: Implications for the Smart Cities of Tomorrow
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Robust Attitude Control of a Disturbed Multi-rotor Aircraft: Experimental Comparisons
papers.abstract: In this work, a robust nonlinear attitude control is developed for a perturbed quadrotor aircraft. The proposed control scheme combines a finite-time sliding mode control with a smooth-reaching law to achieve the stability of the closed-loop system. The suggested method can provide precise tracking of the predefined trajectories while compensating for the impact of wind disturbances. Experimental comparisons with other recent controllers are conducted to highlight the outperformance of the suggested method in terms of trajectory tracking, robustness, and chattering alleviation.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Securing Visual Data: A Fresh Approach with Arnold Cat Map and Chebyshev Map Encryption
papers.abstract: In this paper, we introduce a new image encryption algorithm to encrypt gray-scale images. We will use the Arnold Cat map in the confusion part to change the position of each pixel from the original gray-scale image, then we use an external secret key of 160 bits and a double one-dimension (1D) chaotic maps (Chebyshev map), those maps generate two images with identical dimensions as the original gray-scale image, after that, we apply the XOR operation to all generated images. Experiments and security analysis show the value and quality of the proposed approach in terms of accuracy and strength against several attacks.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: SmartEval: A New Hybrid Quality Assessment System (Online/Paper-and-Pencil)
papers.abstract: This study is carried out within the project objectives of CORETEV in collaboration with ANEAQ and four Moroccan universities. The project is geared towards integrating technologies in teaching and learning assessment. For this reason, a hybrid platform, namely SmartEval, has been put together, which combines the two modalities of the evaluation: online and paper-based. Using the CGQTS Methodology by J-L Gilles, this platform wants to develop a modern way of evaluation and tackle exam management issues, especially post-COVID-19. The first aim of this platform is to increase quality learning and teaching outcomes in Moroccan universities. The integration of ICT with CGQTS shows a strategic and innovative movement in tackling the contemporary education challenges in Morocco.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: The Impact of Foreign Direct Investments on the Renewable Energy Sector in Morocco: An Empirical Analysis of Their Role as a Lever to Enhance Attractiveness
papers.abstract: In a context notable of an environmental crisis and aggravated geopolitical tensions relative to fossil resources, the question of energy transition emerges as a primordial and paradoxical challenge. The application of ecological norms can simultaneously act as a discouraging factor for investments associated with pollutant emissions, also drawing on alternative investment options. In this context, Morocco has incorporated the advancement of renewable energies by positioning them as a catalyst to attract foreign investments. The purpose of this article is examine the current relationship between the consumption of renewable energies and foreign direct investments in Morocco. In order to test the proposition of creating a connection between FDI and renewable energies, our analysis is grounded in an econometric study. We analyze the practical case of Morocco over the period from 1995 to 2020, depending on the Vector Autoregressive model as the methodological framework.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Towards a Methodological Approach for Intelligent IS/IT Project Portfolio Dashboard
papers.abstract:
With the advent of digital transformation, organizations have become more aware of sustaining and valuing their Portfolio of Information System (IS) and Information Technology (IT) initiatives in pursuit of strategic objectives. Thus, the IS/IT Project Management Office (PMO) should be equipped with IS/IT Project Portfolio Dashboards, to monitor the up-to-date performance of the IS/IT Project Portfolio and consequently carry out appropriate actions. Nevertheless, this performance is often compromised by uncertainty. In this paper, a methodological approach for intelligent IS/IT Project Portfolio Dashboard is designed based on three Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) which are Strategic Portfolio Value (
                
                  
                
                $$SPV$$
                
              ), IS/IT Project Portfolio ranking, and Overall Portfolio Risk (
                
                  
                
                $$OPR$$
                
              ). The proposed methodological approach measures the two first KPIs taking into consideration uncertainty to model respectively variability of strategic objectives in 
                
                  
                
                $$SPV$$
                
               and subjectivity of IS/IT PMO experts in IS/IT Project Portfolio ranking. Besides strategic interpretability, as an IS/IT PMO experts’ knowledge, is incorporated in 
                
                  
                
                $$OPR$$
                
              . An IS/IT PMO Project Portfolio case study demonstrates the new methodological approach applicability in which the 
                
                  
                
                $$SPV$$
                
               equals 0.68, the computed ranking emphasizes two highly-priority scored IS/IT projects while ensuring decision-making reliability via a Consistency Ratio (
                
                  
                
                $$CR$$
                
              ) lower than 0.1, and the predicted 
                
                  
                
                $$OPR$$
                
               is obtained with a satisfactory Root Mean Square Error (
                
                  
                
                $$RMSE$$
                
              ) accuracy of 0.108. The new methodological approach advances the current body of knowledge on IS/IT Project Portfolio performance measurement under uncertainty forms and enables IS/IT PMOs to support data-driven decision-making and consequently carry out managerial actions appropriately.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Underwater Image Enhancement by Modified Dark Channel Prior
papers.abstract: The study of underwater image analysis has generated a lot of interest and demonstrated significant improvements. Images taken underwater suffer from unbalanced colors and low contrast. DCP is one of the algorithms used in image enhancement. In this study, an algorithm, Modified Dark Channel Prior (MDCP), was proposed to improve underwater images based on the Dark Channel Prior algorithm and process the illumination component Y using the sigmoid function through the YIQ color space. Using non-reference quality measures Average Gradient (AG), CF, and Natural Image Quality Evaluator (NIQE), the proposed method was compared with other methods (UCM, RSU, RSAP, and MSRCR). The results showed the success of the proposed method in improving underwater images and they have good values.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Unpacking Adaptive Online Learning: A Detailed Literature Review
papers.abstract: Learning is an endless process that takes place at every level of existence. People constantly seek out new information, evolve with the times, and use a variety of tools and platforms to improve their learning process. This literature review explores the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) through adaptive online learning (AOL). It examines several adaptive online learning systems to identify methods and practices for creating comprehensive and scalable systems. We provide valuable information about the strategies used by adaptive online learning systems and their effectiveness in achieving desired outcomes. This paper is structured into sections that provide a general description of adaptive online learning, the essential modules that enable an adaptive online learning system to perform, the challenges and issues facing this field, and a review of existing solutions. We conclude by outlining the prospects for future work.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: A Comparative Review of Microcontroller Architectures in Embedded Machine Learning: The Raspberry Pi Pico (RP2040) and Arduino Nano 33 IoT (SAMD21)
papers.abstract: This paper presents a systematic review of microcontroller architectures with a focus on their application in embedded machine learning, comparing the Raspberry Pi Pico (RP2040) and the Arduino Nano 33 IoT (SAMD21). Embedded Machine Learning (EML) is becoming increasingly crucial in low-power, cost-effective computing platforms, necessary for advancing smart, battery-powered devices with built-in learning capabilities. We explore the architectures, performance metrics, and ecosystem support for both microcontrollers to understand their suitability for various EML applications. The RP2040 microcontroller of the Raspberry Pi Pico is evaluated for its high performance and flexibility, supporting complex computational tasks with lower power consumption. In contrast, the Arduino Nano 33 IoT is analyzed for its accessibility, ease of use, and robust community support, making it ideal for simpler and cost-sensitive projects. Comparative analyses indicate that the choice between these platforms should be guided by specific project needs concerning computational intensity, operational environment, and developer expertise. This study aims to aid developers in selecting the appropriate microcontroller for EML projects by providing a clear comparison based on system architecture, energy efficiency, and overall performance.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: A Comparison Between Two Radio Sources Observation of 3C 348 and 3C 433 Using BURT
papers.abstract: This study is focused to hold a comparison for the stability of the recorded radio data. This data is obtained by the observations of the two radio sources (3C 348 and 3C 433) using Baghdad University Radio Telescope (BURT). The coordinates positions of the observed sources 3C 433 and 3C 348 are (RA: 21h 23m 44.534s, DE: 250 04m 11.88s), (RA: 16h 51m 08.147s, DE: 040 59m 33.32s) respectively. These positions are suitable for radio observation from Baghdad city by BURT. The observations were spent approximately two months (January, June) from (2023/01/10 until 2023/06/01), with different time periods. The results showed that the more stable of the maximum recorded signal is obtained with the long time period of the observations. A curve fitting is implemented on the result to investigate the behavior of stability of the maximum signal with time observation. Statistical calculations are done to found the best behavior of the recorded signal stability. Finally, the behavior stability of the recorded maximum signal is found to be a linear.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: A Model-Driven Approach for Developing Smart Tourism Web Applications
papers.abstract: The Model-driven architecture (MDA) represents a software engineering approach designed to enhance the productivity and quality of software development by using models and transformations. In this paper, we propose a new MDA technic for developing smart tourism web applications, which are applications that provide personalized and interactive services to tourists. This approach consists of three main steps: (1) defining a platform-independent model (PIM) that captures the functional requirements and the domain model of smart tourism, based on the 6 As of tourism; (2) defining a platform-specific model (PSM) that specifies the architectural design of the web application, based on PHP as the programming language and clean architecture as the architectural style; and (3) applying model transformations to generate the source code of the web application from the PSM, using XSLT as the transformation language. We showcase the feasibility and effectiveness of this work by applying it to a case study of a smart tourism web application that allows tourists to explore and book various attractions and activities in a city. We also discuss the benefits and challenges of this approach, as well as some directions for future work.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: A New Model Based on CNN for the Detection and Classification of Some Tumors
papers.abstract: This work introduces a new model based on convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for the detection and classification of five types of tumors: breast cancer, cervical cancer, lung and colon cancer, kidney cancer, and lymphoma. The proposed method was evaluated using three independent datasets corresponding to the first three types of tumors. Comparative analysis against three existing models from the literature revealed comparable performance, with accuracies of 98.80%, 97.47%, and 93.68%, respectively. Our model was applied to the remaining two diseases using two additional independent datasets, yielding promising results with accuracies of 99.87% and 95.33%, respectively.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: A New Model for Digital Business Ecosystem in Complex View
papers.abstract: The analysis of a complex digital business ecosystem operating in a complex environment is problematic given the difficulties of studying a complex phenomenon. The dysfunction of the e-commerce ecosystem in the Tunisian context emerges the problem of analyzing the complexity of this digital ecosystem. This problem is confused by the obscuration of facts, the tangle of interactions and the uncertainty of the environment. This type of problem exposes difficulties in understanding and explaining a complex reality, especially since the aim is to understand this complexity without obscuring the facts of reality and without fragmenting the problem by reducing it to a few of his characteristics. This research aims to analyze the e-commerce ecosystem in complex view and proposes a new model for analyzing this ecosystem. A first partial explanation will be followed by a holistic explanation then by a complex explanation by considering the e-commerce ecosystem first as an elementary unit, then as a global unit and finally as a complex unit. The mobilization of the complexity approach promotes qualitative research while ensuring the diversity of the units of analysis. To analyze, it is useful to jointly design the entire ecosystem and its different parts as well as its environment and to highlight the dialogic of order and disorder in a way that is at once antagonistic, complementary and competitive. This research contributes to reaching a deep understanding of the reality of the Tunisian e-commerce ecosystem, but without claiming to have a complete vision of this reality.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Advancements in the Development of Diverse Hole Transport Materials for Perovskite Solar Cells: A Review
papers.abstract: Perovskite solar cells have shown a significant improvement in power conversion efficiency (PCE) from 3.8% to 22% over a period of 5 years. However, these cells lose efficiency over their operation; they degrade over time. Until this issue is resolved, they cannot compete with traditional silicon solar cells. In this present review article, we look into the different organic and inorganic P-type semiconductor materials, are used for the materials for the hole transport layers employed in the perovskite solar cells that are available and analyze their performance. The efficiency and stability of PSCs strongly depend on the morphology and type of materials selected within the cell, such as the HTL in the device. Additionally, we explore how digital technologies, such as computational modeling and data analytics, are accelerating the discovery and optimization of novel HTMs, thereby driving the evolution of PSCs towards commercial viability.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Advancing Wind Energy: Implementation of a Robust Nonlinear Control for DFIG Turbines
papers.abstract: The purpose of this work is to propose a new methodology for achieving nonlinear regulation within wind turbines. The realization is based on the nonlinear sliding mode approach. to control the power flow of a doubly fed induction generator connected to the grid with two converters: one on the machine side, and the other on the grid side. The efficacy of the proposed model is evidenced through all-encompassing tracking, robustness, and validation experiments using actual wind speed data. The control algorithm's MATLAB implementation the remarkable speed and resilience of Simulink serve as proof of the suggested model's efficacy. Particularly, the results show a remarkably low Total Harmonic Distortion of 1.07, underscoring the model's strength. The quality of power generated by the wind turbine system is enhanced largly by using the sliding mode controller model.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: American Sign Language Recognition with Convolutional Neural Networks: A Gateway to Enhanced Inclusivity
papers.abstract: In the realm of higher education, where the right to inclusive education is fundamental, it is essential to address the distinctive challenges faced by students with disabilities, particularly those who are deaf. This study explores inadequacies in accommodating the needs of deaf students within a university setting through the implementation of a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for sign language recognition. We conducted thorough testing of our model using three optimizers Adaptive Moment Estimation (ADAM), Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD), and Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD) with Nesterov’s Momentum. Notably, the highest test accuracy of 96% was attained when employing SGD with Nesterov momentum on the Sign MNIST dataset. These findings underscore that our proposed approach outperforms other state-of-the-art methods.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: An Investigation on Stability and Maneuverability of Motorcycle Using Multibody Approach
papers.abstract: The aim of this study is to investigate how motorcycle riders actively respond to the movements of their motorcycles. We used a multibody modeling approach to explore the biomechanical aspects and dynamics that lead to the wobble mode experienced by riders. Additionally, we sought to identify the critical factors that impact the occurrence and intensity of wobbling, and to develop strategies for minimizing its effects. Our findings highlight the significant impact of arm stiffness and damping parameters on motorcycle stability, revealing a nuanced effect that restricts the range of self-stability.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Are Preservice Teachers “Totally PACKaged”? A Quantitative Study of Pre-service Teachers’ Knowledge and Skills to Ethically Integrate Artificial Intelligence (AI)-Based Tools into Education
papers.abstract: Advancements in modern technologies have resulted in the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) in the field of education. It is imperative that educators are knowledgeable and skilled with various AI-based tools. However, little is known about the extent of the knowledge and skills of preservice teachers in utilizing AI-based tools in education or about the extent of their ethical considerations when using AI-based tools. Preservice teachers are the vanguard of 21st century teaching and it is important that they have the capacity to incorporate technology into pedagogy. This study employed a descriptive-quantitative-correlational approach to determine the extent of knowledge and skills of 212 preservice teachers in using AI-based tools, as well as the extent of the respondents’ ethical considerations in utilizing AI-based tools in education, with the use of the Intelligent-TPACK survey form adapted from Celik (2023). The data gathered were analyzed through descriptive analysis, namely mean, standard deviation and percentage, as well as inferential statistics of Pearson bivariate correlation to determine if there was a significant interrelationship among technological knowledge (TK), technological pedagogical knowledge (TPK), technological content knowledge (TCK) and technological pedagogical and content knowledge (TPACK). The results revealed that preservice teachers possess high levels of TK, TPK, TCK, and TPACK. Furthermore, the participants are capable of skillfully utilizing technology into teaching, which indicates that preservice teachers are up to date with the trends in artificial intelligence. In addition, the participants were revealed to have high levels of ethical consideration in utilizing AI-based tools, where they can discern which tools will be best in improving the students’ works and give fair feedback in assessing the works of the students. Finally, significant interrelationships among TK, TPK, TCK, and TPACK were also found, in which competency in one component also equates to competency in other components.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Artificial Intelligence Techniques for Medical Image Segmentation: A Technical Overview and Introduction to Advanced Applications
papers.abstract: Medical image segmentation plays a crucial role in enhancing diagnostic, treatment, and research applications within the medical field, increasingly relying on sophisticated artificial intelligence (AI) technologies. This survey explores various AI architectures employed in medical image segmentation, including Deep Neural Networks, Encoder-Decoder Networks, Attention-Based Networks, and hybrid models that integrate features from multiple architectures. It methodically assesses these models’ performances, focusing on their effectiveness across different medical imaging types. The analysis delves into each technique’s specific advantages and limitations, offering valuable insights into their practical applications in medical diagnostics and treatment planning. The comparative study highlights the differences in accuracy, processing time, and the ability to manage complex image structures among the methods reviewed. It also explores the synergistic benefits of hybrid models, which are shown to achieve superior segmentation results. By providing a comprehensive comparison of AI-driven segmentation techniques, the paper significantly enhances our understanding of how these technologies can be optimized and implemented effectively in medical image analysis. The ultimate goal is to guide future research and the practical application of AI in medical image segmentation, aiming to develop more efficient and precise diagnostic tools.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Artificial Intelligence and Supply Chain Management: Implications for the Smart Cities of Tomorrow
papers.abstract: This article explores the concept of smart cities by examining the literature review, research methodology, and associated findings and discussions. The literature review sheds light on the emergence of smart cities in response to the challenges posed by rapid urbanization and global competition. The methodology adopted is based on convenience sampling, mobilizing 267 professionals involved in smart cities in Morocco. The results of the multinomial regression reveal that the age variable plays a significant role in the efficiency of smart cities, highlighting the importance of designing urban environments that are inclusive and adapted to the specific needs of each generation. The practical implications of these findings suggest that understanding age-specific influences is crucial for a successful and balanced implementation of smart city initiatives, thus guiding targeted interventions in areas such as health, education, and marketing.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Assessing the Experience of Distance Teaching During Confinement: A Study on Behaviors and Attitudes of Higher Education Professors
papers.abstract: The paper aims to investigate and explain the involvement level of Moroccan university teachers in distance teaching during the COVID-19 containment period using the social representation theory framework and econometric model based on demographic and institutional variables. Overall, the results reveal that the selected variables exert a limited influence on teachers’ involvement in distance education. However, factors such as institutional support, ICT proficiency, availability of suitable workspace, gender and teachers’ rank contribute significantly to explaining their low to average involvement in remote teaching during COVID-19 confinement period. Furthermore, institutional support and ICT proficiency emerged as the most influential factors, highlighting the crucial role of organizational support and technological proficiency in facilitating effective distance education. These findings underscore the need for targeted interventions and support mechanisms to strengthen faculty involvement in distance education initiatives, which ultimately improves the quality of distance educational experiences for students.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Assessment of Machine Learning Algorithms for Predicting Potential Solar and Wind Energy Locations
papers.abstract: Reaching sustainability and tackling climate change need switching to renewable energy. Still, the erratic character of these energy sources poses problems for energy management. Precise forecasting can facilitate the more efficient integration of these sources, increasing system efficiency and energy production. Predictions of solar irradiance, wind speed, and load have all shown promise for accuracy improvement using machine learning (ML) methods. This study investigates the use of ML techniques, specifically Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP), for predicting potential solar and wind energy sites in Morocco. The performance of these ML algorithms was assessed using metrics including sensitivity (Se), specificity (SP), Positive Predictive Value (Po), and kappa coefficient (k ∗). Results showed that the RF algorithm demonstrated superior accuracy in classifying the geospatial dataset related to the wind farm, achieving Se and Sp of 0.97 and 0.90, respectively. Following this, the MLP algorithm achieves Se and Sp values of 0.77 and 0.80, respectively. Lastly, the SVM algorithm presented Se and Sp values of 0.68 and 0.74, respectively. The highest overall precision in predicting suitable locations of power plants was achieved using the RF classifier with 90% and 87% for wind and solar, respectively, followed by the MLP classifier with 78% and 71% for wind and solar, respectively. In contrast, the SVM classifier provided the lowest Po, with 72% and 70%, for wind and solar, respectively. The k* indices for wind and solar modeling using the RF algorithm were 0.78 and 0.77 for wind and solar, respectively, highlighting its potential for predicting solar and wind farm sites. The main findings of the study are that the RF algorithm outperformed the other ML algorithms in predicting potential solar and wind energy sites.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Big Data and Management Control in Tourist Destinations
papers.abstract: Big Data has become a determining factor in the day-to-day and strategic management of organizations. It is a new technology that offers many advantages to businesses. It enables patterns and connections in data to be analyzed. These analyses enable good decisions to be made based on exhaustive information and data. In this way, Big Data enables the analyses used by organizations to optimize their business processes and, as a result, become more efficient. Thus, management control is associated with the role of supporting data, which constitutes its solid foundation, its relevance, its reliability and can influence the main objectives of management control. This paper aims to show whether Big Data can influence management control so that it functions well and achieves the main objectives of the destination while improving its performance. The study also shows that Big Data is used at all levels; strategic and operational; of the destination for the determination of tourism strategies and the operational management of the tourism activity.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Comparative Analysis of Encoding Methods in Regression Predicting Models of Bachelor's Final Marks
papers.abstract: This study assesses the impact of One-Hot and Target Encoding techniques on the accuracy of predicting bachelor's degree final marks in economics and management. Employing regression models across six machine learning algorithms -Linear Regression, Support Vector Machine, Decision Tree, Random Forest, XGBoost, and Neural Network- we analyze a comprehensive dataset from Hassan II University of Casablanca. This dataset includes both demographic and academic performance data of students. We focus on the application of encoding methods to categorical variables and evaluate model performances based on Mean Squared Error (MSE) and Mean Absolute Error (MAE). Our findings highlight the differential effectiveness of encoding techniques in enhancing the precision of predictive models for academic outcomes.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Comparative Study: The Emerging of Quantum Cryptography
papers.abstract:
Data secrecy concern has always been
 and still is a crucial matter for supper secret information. The transport of this kind of information was, in the early ages, done by a trustworthy prophet to accomplish the process. It was either to alert or for safety reasons. The same cycle still existed within or even between many different countries. Which prepared the right causes to improve the cryptography field. Lately, many industries have included secrecy settings to protect user’s private information and have contributed to promoting it. First, it started with transposition and shifting the message’s letter in a direction, then it jumped to a cryptosystem founded on mathematical equations, especially the complex and the computational-power consumer. Although, it worked for decades. However, quantum mechanics has altered this belief and the dependency on classical and modern cryptography. Quantum physics’ properties have given an unpredictable opportunity to achieve both the full confidentiality and reliability of quantum encryption. An example of the BB84 protocol, which was proposed in 1984 by Bernhard and Bennett, the Quantum Secret Sharing (QSS) in early 2000, the Twin-Field Quantum Key Distribution (TF-QKD) in late 2010, and more. Those protocols have proved the predictability of supposed eavesdropping, for some, by calculating a certain QBER quantum bit error rate. The objectives of this article are to state cryptography eras as well as the recent works done by researchers and their contribution to the quantum field.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Contribution of Artificial Intelligence in Entrepreneurship: A Systematic Literature Review
