papers.abstract: Increasing digitization and advances in artificial intelligence (AI) are bringing new jobs or business models. There is a gap in current research on the impact of digitalization on performance. This systematic literature review (SLR) seeks to enhance our understanding of this field and provides a logical evaluation of existing contributions. It aims to review research on the way artificial intelligence impacts performance. The findings show that artificial intelligence has a significant impact on business, especially a positive impact on entrepreneurs. The present study provides policy signal makers and entrepreneurs with a comprehensive view of key concepts, enabling them to understand the current state of artificial intelligence in the industry.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Correction to: Exploring Moroccan Medical Students’ Perception of Artificial Intelligence
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Mobile Robots: Overview and Issues
papers.abstract: This study offers a comprehensive overview of the integration and impact of Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) in the field of mobile robotics. Focusing on the period from 2000 to 2023, The evolution and influence of DRL are analyzed through a detailed citation analysis, leveraging data from major scholarly databases. The core concepts of Reinforcement Learning (RL) are explored, emphasizing its application in mobile robotics for balancing exploration and exploitation, a critical aspect of autonomous decision-making processes. The paper delves into various applications of DRL in mobile robotics, including autonomous navigation, path planning, and target tracking, highlighting the advancements that have significantly enhanced robotic capabilities. A review of recent developments is also included in the analysis, such as Successor Representations and latent imagination learning strategies, illustrating their contribution to the exploration-exploitation trade-off. Additionally, the advantages of DRL, such as adaptability and learning from experience, alongside challenges like hardware limitations and dynamic environment adaptability. The paper concludes by identifying key future research directions, notably in generalization and transfer learning, robustness to uncertain environments, and the potential role of Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) in further advancing RL for mobile robotics. This overview aims to provide a clear understanding of the current state and future potential of DRL in enhancing the efficiency and intelligence of mobile robotic systems.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Detecting Credit Card Fraud Using 1D Convolutional Neural Network: An Efficient Approach for Enhanced Security
papers.abstract: The COVID-19 pandemic promotes the increasing tendency of internet buying even more. Credit cards are frequently utilized in Internet shopping. Credit card fraud detection is becoming more pressing due to the growing damages caused by fraudulent activity, necessitating sophisticated detection approaches. This study uses the one-dimensional Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) to identify fraudulent credit card transactions. Our approach requires careful preparation of transactional data and the development of a specific 1D-CNN architecture designed to identify complex temporal patterns that indicate fraudulent activity. By conducting rigorous experiments on two real-world datasets, our technique significantly improves accuracy for detecting fraud, obtaining 99.77% and 98.89%, respectively. Furthermore, the model demonstrates interpretability and computational efficiency, making it well-suited for easy integration into fraud detection systems. This study presents an adaptable and robust approach utilizing advanced deep learning methods, particularly 1D-CNN, to enhance the security of credit card transactions, effectively solving a critical issue in financial protection.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Detection and Identification of Voltage Dips for the Control of a Photovoltaic Energy Generator Connected to the Distributed Electrical Network
papers.abstract: This paper presents a procedure to implement a voltage dip detector to be applied in the control strategy of a photovoltaic energy generator connected to a distribution grid. The most common way to mitigate the number of incidents and conserve nature is to introduce renewable energy sources to the electrical grid, such as photovoltaic systems. However, when these systems are connected to the network, mutual impacts have been introduced, and a thorough investigation of their effects on the influence of the distribution networks/and their potential effects on the effects of photovoltaic systems experienced in network disturbances and steady-state. The connection of photovoltaic sources can have a great impact to power quality such as harmonic injection, short-circuit current, phase imbalances as well as to voltage and protection. It is essential to consider the increase in energy losses and fault currents, particularly during short-circuits. Overview Provides an overview of the types of incidents that impact the service, approach that brieflly explains how a system can detect different dip types based on their simulations Checked, this will help to understand how to detect the different styles voltage dips. It also presents a new technique for encoding the gathered information.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Digital Adoption by Teachers in Moroccan Public Institutions: An Analysis of Key Adoption Factors
papers.abstract: In todays changing world driven by the digital revolution Moroccan public institutions face a challenge; effectively incorporating digital technologies into the education system. This shift not offers ways for students to learn by rethinking traditional teaching methods but also underscores the significance of promoting engagement, interaction and personalized learning experiences. However, within these opportunities are obstacles to overcome. This article explores the factors influencing the adoption of tools among educators in Moroccan public institutions. Through this research we aim to make a contribution by unraveling the dynamics of technology integration in this context. Our objective is to identify the drivers and barriers shaping this adoption process thus enhancing teachers preparedness for the age and creating an environment that better meets the needs of Moroccan students in the 21st century. Additionally this study seeks to initiate discussions and initiatives focused on improving education practices in Moroccan public schools. By conducting data analysis and involving stakeholders we aspire to instigate transformations, in Moroccos educational landscape.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Digital Public Communication as a Key Factor for Promoting Sustainable Development Based on Innovation in Developing Countries – Comparative Study Between the Municipalities of Spain and Regional Investment Centers in Morocco
papers.abstract: There is widespread consensus regarding the imperative to enhance global governance, considering it a fundamental objective and a prerequisite for realizing the Sustainable Development Goals. Underpinning this perspective is a fundamental principle of good governance, which underscores the significance of a participatory approach involving a broader array of public actors and local communities aiming at fostering trust in public institutions encouraging investment and innovation, and contributing to social well-being. The proposed method is to assess the impact of digital public communication strategies implemented by public entities, thereby contributing to achieving the Sustainable Development Goals. Through a parallel analysis of two digital communication processes deployed by two government institutions, one in Spain and the other in Morocco, this comparative study highlights the importance of digital applications in government institutions, facilitating the achievement of sustainable development objectives at the level of public governance and responding more easily to the needs of citizens.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Digitization of Moroccan Companies: Influence on Organizational Change and Performance. An Exploratory Study
papers.abstract: This study investigates the digital transformation of businesses in Morocco and its effectiveness in the renewal of management and organization practices. This study, which takes a qualitative approach, collects data from semi-structured interviews that have been conducted with five Moroccan companies that have digitally transitioned and adopted new management and organizational methods. The findings show that the use of NMMO can enhance the performances of the companies as they have been supported by the digitization in Morocco. Flexibility, change management, agile approaches, corporate culture, innovation and productivity are some new methods to help in transitioning. Still, Moroccan companies can leverage agile & digital principles in order to better capture the co-evolving demands of customers, foster innovation, and ultimately, improve productivity, through faster, adaptive capabilities. The practical implications of the research offer relevant indicators for managers of Moroccan companies, allowing them to adopt NMMO in the era of digitization in order to meet the requirements and performance of companies. The originality of the study lies in its innovative contribution to both theory and practice. It sheds new light on the benefits of digital transformation and its effect on organizational and managerial change and business performance, offering valuable insights for research and managerial practice.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Efficient 2D and 3D Image Classification and Compression Using Dual-Hahn Moments
papers.abstract:
This paper focuses on improving the Dual-Hahn moments to boost the efficiency and accuracy in tasks such as compressing and classifying high-quality 3D and 2D images. 
The proposed method, which uses the Deep Neural Network algorithm to classify images based on Dual-Hahn moments as features in first layer, seeks to precisely characterize the image by representing it as a vector called the descriptor vector of moments. This vector is subsequently utilized to reconstruct the image for each order. The simulation and analysis of the results illustrate the superior quality and efficiency of the proposed DHM-DNN method for image compression, and classification, as well as its exceptional accuracy in both noisy and noise-free images.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Emerging Research of Artificial Intelligence in Education: A Literature Survey
papers.abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) has become an integral part of human daily lives, leaving no area of life without affecting it in one way or another. Whether it is sport, health, renewable energies, business, transport, and the field of education has not been spared by this revolution. AI can revolutionize the education system's way of working and learning, opening new possibilities for learners and simplifying various educational tasks, ultimately making the learning process simple and hassle-free. Today's artificial intelligence enhances the ability to advance education by providing personalized learning experiences for each student based on their unique learning styles, interests, and skills. This paper discusses specifically the most prominent emerging applications in the field of education in the era of artificial intelligence, such as predicting academic guidance, detecting learning styles, anti-cheating in exams, predicting students’ performance, intelligent tutoring systems, etc. This research aims to present the education system's current state under AI and shed light on future applications and challenges from both a theoretical and a practical perspective.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Empirical Investigation of Real Exchange Rate Misalignments in Morocco: A Machine Learning Approach
papers.abstract: Machine learning techniques such as Random Forests and LASSO have become increasingly popular for forecasting time series dynamics. These methods have demonstrated superior predictive accuracy, particularly when dealing with a large number of explanatory variables. The primary goal of this study is to evaluate the effectiveness of these techniques in forecasting Real Exchange Rate misalignments, which refer to the disparity between the Real Effective Exchange Rate and the equilibrium or steady-state Real Exchange Rate. Our dataset consists of around one hundred variables encompassing economic, financial, social, and global environmental indicators. The application of these methods indicates that the predictive performance is satisfactory and aligns with findings from the International Monetary Fund, particularly those generated by the LASSO algorithm. Projections suggest an overvaluation in the second quarter and throughout 2021, with additional forecasts for 2022 indicating a 0.22% overvaluation of the REER.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Enhancing Road Safety: A Novel Benchmarking Framework with Case Study
papers.abstract: Road accidents are a major global issue that requires collective efforts to address effectively. As many countries ramp up initiatives to improve road safety, close collaboration is necessary to tackle common challenges and leverage best practices in this area. In this context, international benchmarking appears to be an essential tool for advancing road safety management. This study introduces a new six-step framework for road safety benchmarking. The proposed framework provides a structured approach to evaluate road safety initiatives in a designated country, through comparative analysis with other selected countries. The main goal is to identify areas for improvement and discover best practices that can be implemented in the country under study. To demonstrate the framework’s effectiveness and practicality, we applied it in real-world contexts. Through a practical case study, we showcase how the framework can guide decision-makers in implementing targeted initiatives to enhance road safety in the country being evaluated. In essence, this benchmarking framework offers a systematic way to learn from other countries’ successes and shortcomings, ultimately helping to improve road safety.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Estimate the Parameters of the Polynomial Open Circuit Voltage (OCV) Function LiFePO4 Battery Using the Genetic Algorithm Method
papers.abstract: One important factor that affects the driving range and battery life of an electric vehicle is the state of charge (SOC) estimation accuracy. Since the SOC is a hidden state, we are unable to measure it directly. However, by creating a battery-equivalent circuit model, this state can be estimated using the model-based approach. The SOC's performance is directly impacted by the battery model's accuracy. As a result, a suitable equivalent circuit model needs to be found. The SOC-OCV relationship is one of these factors. This paper focuses on using the genetic algorithm (GA) method to estimate the polynomial open-circuit voltage (OCV) parameters function of the LiFePO4 battery. The proposed method is compared to the least squares method. The results show that the GA method can well estimate the parameters of the polynomial function in various degrees, with root mean square error (RMSE) within 1.7% and maximum absolute error within 11% in high degree.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Evaluation on State of Charge Estimation of Li-Ion Battery with Extended Kalman Filter Compared to Random Forest and Gradient Boosting Models
papers.abstract: Using an estimator is essential to obtain information about the state of charge (SoC) of the battery at any time because, in direct measurement, the battery capacity of an electric car is unattainable. In this paper, we have set up our real data acquisition circuit of the battery, and we chose the first-order Thevenin model to model the battery and identify the parameters of the model, then we did a discharge and a charge of the model of the battery with a current profile for verifying the performance of an extended Kalman filter (EKF) to estimate the state of charge. The estimation error does not exceed 5%. We have a good estimate of the state of charge despite the errors due to the linearization of the nonlinear function of the model and the errors accumulated during the integration of the current. We also make a comparison of the state of charge and the estimation error with the “Random Forest” and “Gradient Boosting” methods. We performed a battery discharge with a constant current profile. It was noted that the error in estimating the state of charge by EKF does not exceed 2%. On the other hand, we have seen that the estimation error by Random Forest does not exceed 4%, and the estimation error of the state of charge by Gradient Boosting does not exceed 5%. This indicates that the extended Kalman filter (EKF) allows good prediction of the state of charge and error rejection.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Examining the Relationship Between Idiosyncratic Risk and Stock Returns: Insights from the Moroccan Stock Market
papers.abstract: This paper investigates the trade-off between indiosyncratic risk and expected stock return in the Moroccan market using a novel approach. Departing from the notion that systematic risk alone may not fully explain stock return variation, we analyze the cross-sectional relationship between expected stock return, idiosyncratic volatility, and expected individual volatility on the Casablanca Stock Exchange (CSE). Employing EGARCH frameworks alongside the Fama-French Three-Factor model and Fama-Macbeth cross-section technique, we find a significant negative impact of idiosyncratic volatility on the Moroccan stock market, while the relation between stock performance and expected individual volatility is insignificant. This suggests unique market characteristics in Morocco, distinguishing it from more mature financial markets.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Exploring Moroccan Medical Students’ Perception of Artificial Intelligence
papers.abstract: This study evaluates students’ perception of artificial intelligence (AI) and compares their vision with other studies from the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) found in the literature. The present study showed that most students had a positive perception of AI but that they had only a limited understanding of how it works. Additionally, the study suggests that there is a need for public education efforts to raise awareness about the potential implications of AI and develop strategies to improve understanding in this area. The main purpose of this study was to explore students’ attitudes towards AI and their degree of knowledge about this technology. We also compared the perspective of Moroccan students with other students from the MENA region. Most participants acknowledged the part AI plays in the medical field but were uncertain about how AI would deal with ethical and social challenges. It is also interesting to note that the majority believed that including AI in training is imperative so they can be prepared for their future practice. Furthermore, this paper indirectly highlight the importance of understanding regional and cultural differences in AI awareness, which should be taken into account when developing educational programmes and resources.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Fog-Based IoP Application Monitoring and Failure Detection
papers.abstract: Due to the increase of IoT (Internet of Things) applications and connected devices, for the purpose of solving daily human activity, systems complexity is increasing. And system failures are inevitable, some errors will always occur. Additionally, system failure detection in IoP (Internet of Plant) applications is essential and crucial for maintaining system reliability and ensuring optimal system functionalities. This paper presents monitoring and automating failure detection in fog computing microservices for IoP applications. It employs tools and techniques to proactively identify and notify about system issues before they impact irrigation systems and disrupt critical crop growing conditions. The proposed approach significantly reduces system complexity by 20% compared to traditional methods. The proposed method effectively identifies and mitigates errors such as overloading, crashes, and timeouts. Furthermore, implementing this approach resulted in improved system reliability by 23%. We assessed four microservices patterns in precision irrigation, evaluating query response times, hardware efficiency, and system health.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Hardware-Based Blockchain Architecture with Physical Unclonable Function for Securing IoT Systems
papers.abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) represents a new era of interconnected devices that seamlessly communicate and share information through internet technology, eliminating the need for external intervention. As the exchanged information can often be of a confidential nature, ensuring its security is paramount. Traditional security solutions prove inadequate for safeguarding IoT systems, prompting the exploration of alternative measures. Blockchain technology has emerged as a viable solution, gaining traction for its adaptability to address the security challenges inherent in IoT deployments. However, the integration of Blockchain with IoT systems presents challenges, particularly in incorporating hardware accelerators effectively. This paper introduces a novel approach to enhancing security in IoT systems by proposing a hardware-based Blockchain architecture leveraging a hardware security primitive known as the Physical Unclonable Function (PUF). The inclusion of PUFs is advocated for their unique advantages in bolstering security.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: How GPUs Kill Threads in Neural Network Training
papers.abstract: The optimization and exploration of computing resources continue significant challenges in challenges in the field of neural network training. This paper present an analysis of the effectiveness of several computational architectures, including Graphics Processing Units (GPUs), Central Processing Units (CPUs), and CPUs using threaded techniques, in supporting neural network training. The research highlights the significant differences in training times across different platforms, demonstrating the effectiveness of GPU-based techniques. In order to guarantee a thorough assessment, the research uses two separate datasets: MNIST(Modified National Institute of Standards and Technology), which has 60,000 photos, and USPS(United States Postal Service), which has 7,291 images. These datasets provide the basis of our empirical work, representing a range of dimensions and complexity. The investigation's findings demonstrate the superiority of GPUs in accelerating neural network training, also provide insightful information on how best to use compute resources for these kinds of jobs. This study offers a thorough understanding of the dynamics involved in neural network training by analyzing the minute variations in performance across various computational architectures. Additionally, it lays the groundwork for future investigations into how to make these training procedures more effective, which might result in faster progress in the field of artificial intelligence.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Implementation of Industry 4.0 Case Study of Moroccan Companies
papers.abstract: Industry 4.0 (I4.0) is revolutionizing the world, especially the manufacturing companies which face challenges to integrate new technologies in technical and non-technical services. The I4.0 is driven by the digitization, rise of data and connectivity, analytics, and human-machine interaction, therefore its implementation is a complex process. Morocco and many countries are currently implementing policies to facilitate companies’ transformation within the I4.0. The primary objective of this pilot study is to provide a first insight about the implementation of I4.0 in Moroccan companies. This paper presents a case study of a company that have implemented the I4.0 technologies within 15 Moroccan companies. The collected data is analysed to provide insight into the status of implementation of the I4.0 technologies, with a focus on identifying barriers.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Influence of Space Limitations on Optimal Sizing of Grid-Connected Hybrid Renewable Energy Generation
papers.abstract: The study explores the underexplored issue of space limitations in the optimal design of renewable energy systems. Previous literature frequently neglects these constraints due to simplifications and tool limitations. To address this gap, Pareto Front Multi-Objective Optimization in Matlab is used to examine the impact of space constraints on grid-connected hybrid renewable energy systems. The analysis focuses on power generation and key optimization metrics, including net present cost, levelized cost of energy, and greenhouse gas emissions. A mathematical model that integrates renewable energy generation, grid electricity supply, energy storage, and multi-objective optimization is developed. Simulations are conducted for both one-way (grid import) and two-way (grid import and export) scenarios, revealing distinct trends. In the one-way grid setup, power generation increases with space until it reaches a threshold, while in the two-way grid setup, it continues to grow indefinitely. Economic parameters follow unique trajectories, with net present cost stabilizing after reaching thresholds in both scenarios and levelized cost of energy decreasing within certain ranges. Greenhouse gas emissions are notably affected by space constraints, with storage costs influencing the preference for energy storage or export. Emissions decrease with increased space allocation, reaching their lowest levels in scenarios that include energy storage. The findings highlight the critical role of space allocation in the optimal design of renewable energy systems, paving the way for more efficient and effective renewable energy sizing.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Intelligent Approaches for Optimal Irrigation Management: A Comprehensive Review
papers.abstract: The complexity of irrigation management necessitates the use of professional experience for effective decision-making. Expert systems (ES) are specifically engineered to mimic human expertise efficiently, allowing cognitive mechanisms to be replicated at the irrigation level. The value of combining human knowledge with cutting-edge technologies such as fog computing, cloud computing, blockchain, IoT, and machine learning has recently come to be recognized more and more. The objective is to create an integrated platform that increases irrigation systems’ resilience and adaptability while also increasing their accuracy, efficiency, and capacity for adaptive decision-making in light of agricultural environments’ changing conditions. With a focus on ES, fuzzy logic, and machine learning algorithms, this paper examines the research trends and applicability of intelligent approaches for optimal irrigation management scenarios. It also provides a thorough analysis of how these approaches contribute to the overall success of smart irrigation management.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Low-Cost rPPG Application for Real-Time Heart Rate Monitoring: Robustness Analysis of Signal Processing Techniques
papers.abstract: A new application has been developed for remote PhotoPlethysmoGraphy (rPPG) tests in real-time using a low-cost laptop or smartphone cameras. This offers a non-contact and convenient solution for heart rate (HR) monitoring, which doctors can use to view all recorded rPPG signals. The application uses a new heart rate estimation method developed from facial videos. It employs skin pixel semantic segmentation and signal processing techniques, including a deep learning model to detect skin pixels from non-skin pixels. The spatial RGB means of the segmented skin pixels are used to extract the rPPG signal, which is then post-processed to remove outliers and calculate the heart rate. The proposed rPPG-based heart rate estimation method has achieved a mean absolute error of ±2.5 bpm, comparable to HR sensors. This low-cost and convenient solution for heart rate monitoring holds promise in medical and biometric applications, providing a new approach to health monitoring.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Online Search Attention, Social Media Sentiment and Sustainable Investment Performance
papers.abstract: Digitalization is increasing the volume of online searches and people’s interaction in social media. This might reflect people’s attention and sentiment toward topical issues such as sustainability. This paper aims to investigate the connectedness between online search attention, social media sentiment, and the Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) index returns in Morocco. Using weekly data and the quantile connectedness approach, the results show that the connectedness between online search attention, social media sentiment and ESG index returns is time-varying and event dependent, especially during the COVID-19 pandemic. This implies that the pandemic has heightened investor sensitivity to sustainability, which might affect ESG index returns. Moreover, online search attention and social media sentiment sometimes act as net receivers and sometimes as net senders. The results also illustrate that spillover effects are mainly driven by the short-term component, except for the COVID-19 outbreak. This research has practical implications for investors, companies, and policymakers.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Optimization of Meixner Parameters Using the AOA Algorithm for Signal and Image Analysis
papers.abstract: In this paper, we introduce a novel approach to solving the problem of choosing the parameters of Meixner moments, recognized as robust descriptors for signal and image analysis. Our method is based on the application of the arithmetic optimization algorithm (AOA) to refine the local parameter values of the Meixner polynomials for optimal performance when calculating the corresponding moments. Our algorithm is evaluated on a set of signals of significant dimensions based on quantitative criteria such as mean square error and maximum signal-to-noise ratio. Our simulation results highlight the efficiency of the proposed algorithm, both in signal analysis and in the analysis of two- and three-dimensional images, compared to conventional methods. Therefore, this approach shows great potential in enhancing the precision and efficiency of extracting information from intricate data, thereby introducing fresh opportunities in the realm of signal and image analysis.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Predicting Loquat Quality Using Visible, Near Infrared Spectroscopy and Artificial Neural Network
papers.abstract: The main objective of this study is using Visible and Near-Infrared Spectroscopy (Vis/NIRS) as a simple, fast and non-destructive method, combined with Artificial Neural Network (ANN) in order to build three ANN models for predicting fructose, glucose and pH levels in loquat. A huge amount of spectral data characterizing each sample was extracted. In addition, by applying destructive procedures, soluble sugar content (glucose and fructose) and pH were determined. Feature selection was employed to select the most suitable wavelengths from the raw spectra. Afterwards, ANN was applied to quantify the three physico-chemical properties of loquat. Different feature selection algorithms were tested, namely Least Absolute Shrinkage and Selection Operator (LASSO), Interval Random Frog (iRF), Variable Iterative Space Shrinkage Approach (VISSA) and Interval VISSA (iVISSA). As a result, ANN models based on feature selection (VISSA) gave the best results compared to those obtained using full spectra. The performance of the ANN models in quantifying the three loquat properties were: a prediction determination coefficient (R2p = 0.97), (R2p = 0.98), (R2p = 0.98) and a standard error of prediction (SEPpH = 0.08), (SEPglucose = 0.30°Bx) and (SEPfructose = 0.34°Bx) for pH, glucose and fructose prediction respectively. The results indicate the power and efficiency of combining Vis/NIRS and ANNs to predict loquat quality. Furthermore, this research can be extended to other food products.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Review on Techniques for Evaluating the Degradation of Lithium-Ion Batteries Based on Artificial Intelligence: Algorithms, Implementations, Problems and Prospects
papers.abstract: The degradation of lithium-ion batteries is a key area of research, as these batteries are widely used in many sectors, including electric vehicles, electronic devices, and renewable energies. Deep learning (DL) based evaluation systems have become widely adopted because of their capacity to process complex data and deliver accurate predictions. Lithium-ion batteries are subject to degradation over time, limiting their ability to store and deliver energy reliably. To monitor and manage the degradation of Li-ion cells effectively, research is now focusing on the use of evaluation systems based on DL algorithms like recurrent neural networks (RNN), convolutional neural networks (CNN), and hybrid models. These approaches make it possible to predict the remaining useful life (RUL) and the state of health (SoH) of the battery and to optimize its use to prolong its lifespan. This paper presents several techniques that can be used to predict RUL and SoH to evaluate battery degradation. It also provides an overview of the data collection process, as well as the challenges faced and solutions proposed.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Selective Filter System Based on Multi-Defectives Resonators Inserted in 1D Acoustic Periodic Star Waveguides
papers.abstract: The spectre of acoustic wave transmission through a one-dimensional phononic comb-like structure is derived using the Green Function Method. The devised system comprises a finite series of cells, where each cell consists of a segment of length d1 connected at its end by resonators of lengths d2. The defective resonators are positioned at two distinct sites, each having lengths d02 and d03, which differ from those of the ideal resonators. The perfect phononic structure exhibits the presence of pass bands and band gaps where the propagation of acoustic waves is prohibited. To facilitate comparison, we initially introduce a single defect resonator situated at one site, thereby inducing two defect modes inside the band gaps. These defect modes exhibit noticeable transmissions and high-quality factors, enabling our proposed system to function as a single narrow selective filter. When introducing two defect resonators of varying lengths at different sites, multiple localized modes emerge in the band gaps. Consequently, the proposed system operates as highly narrow multichannel filters with the defect modes shifting towards lower frequencies as the lengths of the defect resonators increase. The obtained results underscore the dependency on the frequencies of defect modes, the lengths of defect resonators and their positions in the structure.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Sentiment Analysis for Standard and Dialectal Arabic Using the Lexicon-Based Approach
papers.abstract: Sentiment analysis offers a substantial opportunity to extract valuable information by studying trends and discovering of new insights. However, conducting such an analysis based on opinions expressed in Arabic proves to be challenging task due to the nature of this language and the strong presence of dialectal expressions. This work focuses on sentiment analysis of Arabic opinions using the lexicon approach. Thus, we propose a new sentiment analysis lexicon that covers modern standard Arabic and Moroccan dialect. First, we present the construction process of this lexicon according to a manual step and automatic expansion step. Then, we will go through the steps of implementing it in order to classify sentiments. We evaluate the performance of the lexicon through two Arabic corpora collected from Facebook and Twitter. The results obtained showed good scores, since the classification performance was above 80% in the Fscore for both corpora. When it comes to lexicon coverage, the score was above 90% for the same tested corpora.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Smart Grid Integration: PID-Controlled Offboard Electric Vehicle Charger for V2G and G2V Interactions
papers.abstract: Offboard electric vehicle (EV) chargers are becoming more prevalent worldwide as a result of the growth in EV usage. The most complex and challenging domain in the Smart Grid is vehicle-to-grid (V2G) and grid-to-vehicle (G2V) interactions. To accurately manage the supply quality of the Smart Grid, it is necessary to control the power delivered to and received from EVs. This paper presents a method of controlling an offboard Electric Vehicle charger, which facilitates both Opportunities from network to car and car to the network. The development approach depend on the PID controller method to simplify the design and achieve desired values with minimal error. The controller's performance is assessed in two modes: discharging and charging, with reactive power compensation. There are two simultaneous phases in the battery charger: firstly, AC to DC, and secondly, DC to DC. An LCL filter is sized to compensate for reactive power. A MATLAB Simulink simulation, informed by a study, is showcased for a 15 KVA EV charger.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Smart Water Management: What Implications for Morocco?
papers.abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive study of the water situation in Morocco and the challenges facing the management of this sector. Despite facing a decrease in freshwater availability due to droughts, population growth, and climate change, Morocco has implemented various measures to transcend these issues. These include the construction of large and small dams, the mobilization of conventional and non-conventional water resources and efforts to expand access to potable water. However, in such a context marked by the rise of new technologies and increasing levels of water stress, moving towards smart water management is no longer an option. Water loss in Water Distribution Networks (WDN) is one of the problems facing Morocco. Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools are used in detecting leaks in these networks, which is why it is so important to present smart water management to optimize the management of this natural resource. This article aims to provide researchers with the water situation in Morocco, the importance of smart water management, and the AI solutions available to optimize water management and reduce losses in Morocco’s water distribution networks.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: State of Charge Estimation for 18650 Lithium-Ion Batteries: A Comparative Analysis Using Technical Specifications from Three Leading Manufacturers
papers.abstract: This paper addresses the crucial challenge of optimizing battery management for electric Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs), with a specific focus on 18650 lithium-ion (Li-Ion) batteries. Drawing upon extensive knowledge and insights gained from experimental tests and real-world missions, we aim to establish a foundational understanding of their application in UAVs and address inventory issues related to State of Charge (SoC) battery estimation. To enhance our analysis, we delve into the technical specifications of leading Li-Ion cell manufacturers—LG, Samsung, and Panasonic—to extract discharge behavior data, forming our training dataset. Employing the Four Parameter Logistic (4PL) regression model, we then introduce three distinct models, each tailored to one of the three manufacturers’ cells, demonstrating their capability to accurately estimate the SoC. To validate our approach, we apply these models to a real-world flight log, achieving satisfactory results.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: The Role of Digitalization in Entrepreneurship
papers.abstract: Since the appearance of the Internet for the general public around 1995, our habits, approach to consumption, modes of production in a global manner have evolved significantly, which has pushed companies to begin their digitalization. The concept of digital entrepreneurship has become widespread during this last period, because it expresses the implementation of new projects, or the transformation of existing projects using digital technologies. The current business environment has also seen a major shift towards digital environments. Today, digital is profoundly changing the managerial function, customer relations are becoming digital, new technologies are appearing, and models based on sharing and collaboration are emerging. The arrival of new competitors also threatens the profitability of organizations; Generally, we are more and more connected, the objects around us are also connected, this technological evolution has been accompanied by new behaviors of Internet users, who have new expectations, access information more easily, as a result they need increasingly volatile and ephemeral Instant information, as well as extensive and constantly renewing digital services. Managerial action can be strengthened by the digital revolution; and the company can take digital as a central core of decisions and strategic positioning. This article has tried to enrich this stream of research through the treatment of 3 axes: the first axis was devoted to a theoretical model to better approach entrepreneurship and the managerial function within organizations the second axis mentioned the role of the digital revolution for the smooth running of the managerial function and finally the third axis tried to emerge the role of digital at the business level in periods of crises such as the case of covid 19. This work will be enriched by a questionnaire on Google forms and the results will be processed by SPSS software.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: The Role of Top Management Commitment in Enhancing Energy Efficiency Through Artificial Intelligence
papers.abstract: In the quest for the integration of Artificial Intelligence technologies to enhance energy efficiency within companies, understanding the role of top management commitment gains in importance. This perspective article explores the pivotal effect of top management commitment in in promoting AI technologies and advancing energy efficiency within companies. Through a review of recent studies, we conclude the importance of top management commitment in producing a chain of positive changes, leading ultimately to an optimum level of energy efficiency within companies through the implementation of AI solutions. This perspective article stresses that the path to companies’ energy efficient use is intimately linked to top management commitment and leadership.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Trading Strategies Within a Low Interest Rates Environment
papers.abstract: In this work, we propose to construct a trading strategy and simulate its future payoffs in the order to study its behavior within a low-interest rate environment. Indeed, today interest rates are historically low and investors tend to take more risks for yield enhancement. Before constructing the strategy, we explain the basic option strategies of the markets, as they are the main yield enhancement components of almost all strategies. Then we expand the different aspects of structured products and, more precisely, Autocallable (AC: Auto callable) products. In the order to understand the pricing models implemented, the proposed study explains the Black-Scoles Model and the Monte Carlo pricing method. The objective of this study is to investigate the field of the trading strategies within the context of low-interest rates. A genuine AC strategy was constructed from scratch, and the results are quite intriguing. To realize that, we had to implement our AC strategy on python and analyze the results. We conclude that, while the price is attractive, the strategy is highly sensitive to the interest rate when getting low and represents many risks.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Unlocking Corporate Energy Efficiency Potential: The Role of Top Management and the Digital Transformation
papers.abstract: In today's ever-changing business environment, the call for energy efficiency has never been more urgent. Companies worldwide are under growing pressure to optimize resource utilization, reduce environmental impact, and improve operational performance. However, despite increasing awareness of the benefits of sustainable practices, many organizations struggle to turn their intentions into concrete actions. This perspective article delves into the intricate connections between top management, digital transformation, and energy efficiency within companies, aiming to address this challenge. Through an analysis of pertinent literature and empirical evidence, we examine the role of top management and digital transformation in driving energy efficiency initiatives and explore the mechanisms through which organizations can optimize energy efficiency. By capturing these interconnections, this perspective article offers policy recommendations for advancing top management in the service of digital transformation, ultimately bridging the gap between intention and action in implementing energy efficiency initiatives within organizations.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs): from Evolution to Simulation, A Comprehensive Exploration
papers.abstract: This research provides an overview about how Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) or drones a market that is evolving even at the present time, their history, applications and some of the challenges that are affecting in different parts of the world. The report explores the vantage points of next-level UAVs, the potential academic ramifications, and the ever-changing global commercial drone industry. The course covers UAV simulators in great detail, which are crucial in R&D. This study is a comparison of two software, DJI Drone Simulator and SimPro Drone Simulator, and features and uses of both of the software has been elaborated in this study. It also discusses several open-source simulators such AirSim, Ramos’s airship simulator, SIMSAT, and Thompson’s aircraft/control system simulation facility for testing and training of autonomous vehicles.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: A Business-Centric Approach to Automated Microservices Identification
papers.abstract:
Microservice-oriented architectures are becoming more and more popular in the academic and industrial domains as opposed to monolithic systems. With the help of microservices, huge, monolithic apps may be divided into more manageable, highly coherent, loosely linked pieces. Benefits like enhanced maintainability and scalability are provided by this. 
However, determining the optimal boundaries for these microservices continues to be a challenging task that might hinder the effective adoption of this design. This article presents an approach for automatically identifying microservices from Business Process (BP) using Natural Language Processing (NLP) methods and semantic analysis. The approach makes use of NLP to extract important data from business operations, providing insights into the connections between various tasks. As a result, discrete microservices are formed from activities that have comparable semantic properties, offering a data-driven method of breaking down the system. A case study centred on a bicycle rental system is carried out to exemplify and illustrate the approach.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: A Comparative Study of PSO and MILP Optimization Algorithms for Economic Dispatch in Grid-Tied Microgrids
papers.abstract: The study contributes to addressing the challenge of economic load dispatching in microgrids, offering insights into efficient energy management techniques. In an energy management system, the economic dispatch (ED) strategy aims to minimize operating energy costs while ensuring a reliable power supply. The implementation of the ED strategy relies on the use of various optimization methods. This paper presents a comparative study of two optimization algorithms to investigate their performance in solving economic dispatch problems. The two algorithms, which are mixed integer linear programming (MILP) and particle swarm optimization (PSO), were implemented in the Matlab environment and compared in terms of the computational capability and optimization results. By comparing PSO and MILP algorithms, the study aims to identify the most effective approach for optimizing power dispatch in grid-connected microgrids. To validate the proposed approach, a case study of the Solar Decathlon Africa Village project in Ben Guerir, Morocco, is conducted. This real-world application serves to evaluate and verify the efficacy of the methodology in practical settings.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: A Comprehensive Exploration of Cryptographic Solutions for Securing Medical Images
papers.abstract: The sensitivity of medical images (MI) requires them to be protected in order to preserve patients’ privacy and guarantee a reliable and correct diagnosis. To prevent unwanted access and unauthorized manipulation of this crucial data, MI encryption (MIE) is considered as one of the most effective ways to protect medical data. For this purpose, an overview of the principles, requirements, evaluation standards and typical attacks associated with MIE is provided in this article. Various encryption techniques, such as post-quantum cryptography (PQC), elliptic curve cryptography (ECC), chaotic systems (CS), and DNA-based encryption are presented in this paper describing their advantages and disadvantages. Additionally, it covers some features of each MIE technique and compares them to each other as it suggests future avenues of research for further studies and development in this rapidly developing sector while addressing the complexities of transmitting and safeguarding MIs information online.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: A Deep Learning Approach to Potato Late Blight Detection: Developing and Evaluating a Lightweight Single-Stage Detection Model
papers.abstract: Detecting and identifying crop diseases in their early stages remains a challenge in modern agricultural practices, affecting yield and food security worldwide. Potato late blight, caused by Phytophthora infestans is a serious threat to potato crops requiring fast and accurate detection methods to minimize losses. A new approach using deep learning was suggested in this study to spot this disease at an early stage. The study focused on developing and training a YOLOv8n model with a dataset of 14,150 images and annotations from Berkane, Morocco. This dataset was carefully chosen to cover a diverse range of symptoms and stages of potato blight under different environmental conditions to ensure the model’s reliability, robustness, and generalizability. The model is known for its suitable design for real-time decisions in agricultural settings due to its lightweight size. However, this experiment shows that YOLOv8n has a modest detection performance on complex and diverse datasets, making it an interesting opportunity for enhancing research towards early intervention against potato late blight using lightweight models - the potential of deep learning to improve farming methods.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: A Deep Learning-Powered TinyML Model for Gesture-Based Air Handwriting Simple Arabic Letters Recognition
papers.abstract: This paper offers a novel deep learning (DL)-powered Tiny machine learning (TinyML) model meticulously customized to recognize Arabic hand gestures (AHGs) executed in mid-air. The principal focus of this study lies in the intricate task of precisely classifying Arabic letters through these gestures. The paper provides a comprehensive exposition of the complex dataflow architecture, encompassing the processing of gyroscope and accelerometer data to derive precise 2D gesture coordinates. The pivotal role of convolutional neural networks in the DL model is elucidated, emphasizing their outstanding performance in reaching a level of accuracy of 94.2% in classifying diverse AHDs. This accuracy showcases the model’s efficacy and robustness, underscoring its potential for real-time, practical deployments in scenarios of gesture recognition. The implications of this research extend beyond the immediate domain of Arabic letter recognition, contributing to the progress of TinyML applications in real-world gesture recognition apps.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: A Geo-AI Approach for Electric Vehicle Charging Infrastructure Allocation: Case Studies from Marrakech-Safi Region, Morocco, and Andalucia Region, Spain
papers.abstract: The rise of electric vehicles (EVs) in Morocco’s Marrakesh-Safi region and Spain’s Andalucia demands a well-developed network of charging stations. This network would make charging EVs convenient, leading to more people choosing sustainable transportation. This study uses a Geo-Spatial Artificial Intelligence (GEO-AI) approach to pinpoint the best places to put these stations. We considered both technical factors and social and economic ones. To identify promising areas, we used clustering algorithms along with the Elbow Method. Then, we used a Random Forest algorithm to choose the exact spots within those areas. Our research shows that the best places for charging stations are cities, including Marrakesh, Benguerir, Safi, Essaouira, and Kelaat Seraghna in Marrakesh-Safi, and Seville, Malaga, and Cádiz in Andalucia. Model evaluation demonstrates the efficacy of the Random Forest model in placement prediction. The outcomes results highlight how RF-based prediction models can improve e-mobility infrastructure not only in the cities studied but also in other African and European urban areas.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: An Advanced Control Law Combining Sliding Mode and Fractional Calculus for Wind Energy Conversion Systems
papers.abstract: This paper presents an improved nonlinear control law that combines the sliding mode control and the fractional-order control. The utility of the sliding mode control returns to its power in terms of stability against parameter variation, robustness against modeling uncertainties, and ease of implementation. The utility of the fractional order returns to its better model of the dynamic behavior of a studied system to enhance the performance of various controllers. The main purpose of this advanced law applied to the mechanical part based on the two-mass model of the three-blade horizontal axis variable speed wind turbine is to minimize the effect of the chatter phenomenon (which is the major disadvantage of the controllers due to its high-frequency oscillations that damage the considered system) and reduce the value of the peak of the electromagnetic torque. The novel law conserves the same benefits of the other controllers such as accurate tracking and robust stability, as proved by Lyapunov’s theory. The effectiveness of the developed control law is compared with other controllers and is shown by simulation results and numerical results using MATLAB.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Analysis Customers’ Factors of Switching Intention from Using Conventional Bank Services to Digital Bank in Indonesia
papers.abstract: Nowadays, the development of information and communication technology has had a huge impact on all sectors, especially banking industries. Currently many companies are competing to acquire conventional banks in Indonesia to become digital banks. This research will use a quantitative approach by conducting surveys to conventional bank users that have intention to use digital bank in various regions of Indonesia. This research aims to what extent are users of conventional banking services in Indonesia influenced to migrate to digital bank. The collected data is processed using SmartPLS to identify factors that influence the intention to switch to digital bank. The results of this study are expected to provide more insight to the Bank, namely in developing marketing strategies and improving digital bank services and the customer also helped with the other customer’s experience. By using this research, the Bank can also optimize the results of this research as a business strategy in the future. This research showed that 5 factors have influence and 1 factor that have no influence to customer switching intention.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Analysis and Design of Power Management System for Wireless Power Transfer Circuits
papers.abstract: The advent of wireless technology has revolutionized handheld and wearable devices. When comparing battery technology to other aspects of portable device technology, it becomes evident that it has been a bottleneck in the innovation of miniaturized electronic devices. In this paper, a wireless power harvesting system was designed and analyzed. The Wireless Power Transfer (WPT) chip was produced using 2.5 V devices in a 90 nm CMOS process. The target performance was to wirelessly power a 10 mA load with 1.8 V. The objective of the WPT system implemented in this work can be achieved with a system efficiency greater than 50%. The inductive link power transfer efficiency for strong and moderate coupling was reasonably good, yielding a measured WPT system efficiency of 46.1%, considering the rectifier only as the Power Management System (PMS).
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Analysis of Public Electric Vehicles Charging Impact on the Grid: Morocco Case Study
papers.abstract: Electric vehicles charging infrastructures can have a significant impact on the power grid. Statistical analysis of the charging behavior of PEVs is necessary for planning and operation of public charging stations to evaluate the impact of the rapid deployment of PEVs and charging infrastructure on the power grid and to evaluate their potential benefits for future microgrids, especially in regulated (non-liberalized) electricity markets. This paper evaluates the impact of plug-in electric vehicles load and V2G (Vehicle to Grid) on the Moroccan distribution network at different EVs penetration scenarios. Simulation is carried out in MATLAB/SIMULINK. Results shows that the high penetration of EVs (Electric Vehicles) in Morocco might have a significant negative impact on the grid stability, but V2G might be an effective solution for energy dependance in the Kingdom. Results will also enable Moroccan utility company ONEE (National Office of Electricity and Water) anticipate the charging demand of PEV customers, as well as the impact on the electric grid as PEV penetration rates increase.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Applications of Machine and Deep Learning in Funding Decision: A Review
papers.abstract: In corporate finance, a number of conventional techniques are being replaced by machine and deep learning algorithms as a result of artificial intelligence advancements. These tools of artificial intelligence will continue to revolutionize society and various aspects of the economy, including corporate finance. This paper is dedicated to a review of the applications of machine and deep learning algorithms in corporate finance decisions throughout the funding process. These algorithms can be used to assess current or future funding needs, explore the various funding options available to the company, choose the most appropriate funding method, prepare the funding file, and manage the risks inherent in funding. These modern technological tools are indispensable for navigating an increasingly complex and competitive financial environment. Their strategic use helps optimize financial decisions and maximize growth opportunities, from the beginning stages of planning to continuous risk management. By using them, decision-making is enhanced, resource allocation is optimized, and profitable growth is guaranteed.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Approach to Real-Time Simulation and Hardware-In-The-Loop for Microgrid Battery Management Systems
papers.abstract: Real-time energy management is vital to address the intermittency of renewable energy resources. Assuming that the state of charge (SOC) availability of the battery is favorable, this paper will focus on achieving optimal integrated control to accomplish the utilization of solar power generation, battery energy storage, and grid interaction. This paper presents the offline modeling in MATLAB/Simulink study and the model validation through HIL (Hardware-in-the-Loop) on real-time simulation using OPAL-RT. Furthermore, the methodology is tested with residential datasets measured in Green & Smart Building Park (GSBP) in Ben-Guerir, Morocco. The analysis and evaluation of battery management systems (BMS), this research aims to contribute insights into enhancing system performance and reliability. This work seeks to understand how to improve system performance and reliability. The collected results show the performance of the battery energy management system and the contribution to the penetration of renewable resources through the Moroccan energy mix. This study is vital to developing renewable energy and advancing the global energy transition to cleaner energy sources.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Bearing Fault Diagnosis with a Hybrid CWT-ResNet-LSTM Model
papers.abstract: Bearings’ reliability is very important for the seamless operation of rotating machinery, as their failures can cause much downtime and expenses. In applications of bearing fault diagnosis (BFD), where deep learning (DL) has performed well, problems remain in making sure that the model remains robust under different operating conditions. This study introduces a novel hybrid model which integrates both Continuous Wavelet Transform (CWT) and Residual Networks (ResNet) together with Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to improve fault detection. The process begins with CWT that transforms time signals into 2-D time-frequency (TF) images, which are used by ResNets to extract spatial features. Temporal dynamics of these features are analyzed using an LSTM network in order to enhance predictive accuracy and adaptability to different operational scenarios. Tested against the famous CWRU dataset, our hybrid model demonstrates superior diagnostic performance, outperforming existing methods in accuracy.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Blockchain Applications in Emergency Logistics: A Bibliometric Study and Future Research Horizons
papers.abstract: Improving emergency logistics in order to effectively respond to disasters is becoming vital, with the constant rise of disasters frequency. Conventional supply chains often face challenges in different phases of a disaster, such as the lack of transparency or coordination issues. To examine this potential, the proposed study sheds light on the crucial role of emerging technologies, mainly Blockchain Technology in optimising emergency supply chain management. Blockchain technology has the ability to transform logistics operations deployment during a disaster, through its decentralised and secure features. This paper uses a systematic approach based on science mapping and performance analysis, it offers insights into relevant literature, key scientific works, leading authors, journals, and countries in this topic. The study considers 405 articles from Scopus database and deploys VOSViewer software for visual analysis. The findings highlight the significant contributions of authors like Ivanov. AND Cherrafi, A. Sustainability Switzerland Journal, setting the standard followed by International Journal of Production Research. Regarding countries analysis, the publications are originally from 74 different country with India, China and the United States taking the lead in scientific production. In conclusion, the proposed paper suggests a deeper exploration of potential research opportunities, mainly in the integration of Blockchain technology with other emerging technologies, and the deployment of dynamic modelling techniques in disaster management.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: COV19Sim Morocco: A Web-Based Application for Simulating COVID-19 Dynamics
papers.abstract: Infectious diseases present significant threats to humanity, with the COVID-19 pandemic wreaking havoc globally. Even though there are considerable educational resources to learn the fundamentals of epidemic modeling, a notable disparity exists between the relatively simple models employed for theoretical exposition and those utilized in practical applications to model COVID-19 dynamics. Comprehending these models demands substantial prerequisite knowledge and can be daunting for individuals new to epidemiological modeling and governing bodies. This study introduces COV19Sim Morocco, a web application designed to simulate COVID-19 dynamics using compartmental models. Through an interactive and user-friendly interface, the application presents a versatile tool for exploring COVID-19 data, simulating the spread of the virus, fitting models to available data, and generating predictions.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Constraints and Precautions of the Experimental Implementation of the PWM Control of a Three-Phase Asynchronous Machine Using the dSPACE 1104 Board
papers.abstract: This paper presents the experimental implementation of pulse width modulation (PWM) using the dSPACE 1104 board for a three-phase asynchronous machine (ASM). The PWM technique is commonly employed to control the speed and torque of motors by comparing a modulating signal with a triangular carrier waveform and generating a numeric signal (0 and 1) to regulate the machine’s voltage. However, the real implementation of this control presents several challenges. Thus, the objective of this article is to outline the necessary steps and precautions required to successfully implement PWM in real-time using the DSPACE 1104 board, and to analyze the system behavior in various test scenarios. To achieve these objectives, two tests were conducted: one with PWM control and another without it (direct startup). Additionally, a comparative study was performed to assess the performance and effects of each starting technique on the machine’s behavior. The results presented in this study were obtained using the Control Desk software. These results clearly demonstrate the performance of the PWM, validating the implementation technique used in this study.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Context of Culture and Social Relationship Management in E-learning Context
papers.abstract: The e-learning offers the opportunity to people from different cultures to interact on line in order to change their points of view and achieve a personal development. However, there is a risk of misunderstanding associated to on line interpersonal relationships. These misunderstandings have some effects on the harmony between people, the good functioning and as a consequence the learning results. This study uses Hall's theory to explore the relationship between culture and the social practices of the learners on line. Through a netnographic study of two international online learner communities, involving secondary data, Moodle communications, observation notes, and online interviews, the analysis revealed how cultural differences influence learners’ social experiences. The study emphasizes the significant impact of communication context on social orientation, personal engagement with the group, relationships with the tutor, conflict management, and communication styles. It highlights the importance of considering cultural differences to enhance the effectiveness of e-learning environments.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Deep Learning Approaches for Understanding Adverse Drug Reaction: Short Literature Review
papers.abstract: An adverse drug reaction (ADR) is a harmful disarray that occurs as a result of taking a drug at doses commonly used in humans for the prevention, diagnosis, treatment of disease, or to change physiological function. The detection of ADRs is critical to our understanding of risk-benefit profiles. Reporting ADRs has become an essential part of the monitoring and evaluation activities carried out in hospitals. Spontaneous reporting of ADRs has long been the standard method of reporting. However, this approach is known to have high underreporting rates, a problem that limits pharmacovigilance efforts. Deep Learning (DL) techniques are now widely used for drawing useful information from unstructured textual data. The review begins with an explanation of ADRs. It also underscores how traditional methods are inadequate to deal with the large volume of unstructured information. The following parts describe major methods of DL applied in ADR detection.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Deep Neural Networks for Automated Metadata Extraction
papers.abstract: Metadata extraction is essential for the discovery of scientific information. However, it can be tedious and requires a lot of time and effort, given that the number of scientific publications is increasing rapidly. In this work, we developed a solution based on deep learning to automatically extract information relating to metadata from scientific articles. The architecture based on this solution permits segmenting and labeling the main areas of scientific documents, such as title, abstract, sections, image, etc., while simultaneously recognizing the text of the detected areas. The proposed solution is based on object detection models, specifically Faster Region-based Convolutional Neural Networks (Faster R-CNN), Mask Region-based Convolutional Neural Networks (Mask R-CNN), and Cascade Mask Region-based Convolutional Neural Networks (Cascade Mask R-CNN), in order to detect the layout. The areas of interest found are then passed through an OCR (Optical Character Recognition) for text recognition. The model was trained using annotated data from the SciBank dataset. During this study, we had the opportunity to test three different models and compare the results obtained. The results from the proposed solution show promise.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Design, and Optimization Using Genetic Algorithms of a Dual-Band Microstrip Antenna Based on Defective Ground Structure for Wireless Power Transmission Applications
papers.abstract: This paper investigates an improved microstrip patch antenna array (MPAA) design using the defective ground structure (DGS) technique operating at a dual-band (at 2.45 GHz and 5.8 GHz) in the ISM (Industrial, Scientific, and Medical) band. This improved MPAA based on DGS contains two identical and symmetrical microstrip rectangular patches fed by an inset feed technique using a single microstrip feed line of each patch. The dimensions of each layer (radiating element, substrate, and ground) are calculated using the transmission line model coded in MATLAB software. Then, these dimensions are optimized based on the genetic algorithm (GA) technique which is available in CST MSW (computer simulation technology microwave studio). This novel MPAA design printed on a Rogers RT/Duroid 5880 substrate. The effectiveness of this optimized MPAA using GA is proved by the results obtained by CST software and confirmed by another software called HFSS (high-frequency structure simulator) in terms of its high performance: coefficient reflection, voltage standing wave ratio, directivity, gain, efficiency, and dual-band functionality. The MPAA conception is effective in various wireless power transmission applications to supply different systems ecologically.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Digital Healthcare Transformation and Artificial Intelligence: Zizekian Perspective
papers.abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is an emerging technology that transforms healthcare management. AI solutions reduce time and make patient-doctor interactions efficient. This paper explores a compelling intersection between digital transformation, Artificial Intelligence, and Zizek’s philosophical insights. Psychoanalytic mapping offers an innovative tool for decision-making in AI applications. This new tool emerges from an authentic act and develops more ethical ways to use AI.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Digital Innovations for Good: A Bibliometric Study of Social Entrepreneurship Digitalization
papers.abstract: As social enterprises seek out new tools to broaden the scope of their activities, digitisation is emerging as an important topic of interest to practitioners and researchers alike. Despite this interest, a gap persists in terms of bibliometric reviews concerning this research topic. This article aims to fill this gap by conducting a bibliometric review of the literature via semantic analysis, keyword-occurrences and co-authorship networks analysis in order to identify the research dynamics, the most cited articles and journals and the collaboration networks in the research field studied. The results show a remarkable increase in the number of articles published between 2011 and 2023, with the United States ranking first among the countries with the highest number of contributions. Thus, emerging markets case studies represents the most productive journal in terms of publications. Research trends include are digital platforms, e.g., crowdfunding; sustainable business models; and managerial resilience in times of crisis. This research will enable researchers and stakeholders in the social entrepreneurship ecosystem to better understand the dynamics of research on the subject of the digitalisation of social entrepreneurship. Future research should further study aspects of inclusive entrepreneurship; social impact assessment of digital initiatives and ethical considerations regarding the use of technological innovation, e.g., Artificial intelligence in social entrepreneurship.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Digital Revolution and Emerging Opportunities of the Blockchain in Finance
papers.abstract: This article delves into the revolutionary impact of blockchain on the financial sector, tracing its evolution from conceptual origins in the 1980s to practical implementation with Bitcoin in the 2000s. The literature review meticulously analyzes the advantages of blockchain, specifically focusing on its implications within the Moroccan financial landscape. Employing a systematic review methodology, the study deeply explores 21 relevant articles. The ensuing discussion revolves around four key pillars of blockchain: the reimagining of financial trust, a cost revolution in finance, the disruption of Fintech, and the redefinition of intermediary roles. Throughout, blockchain emerges as a transformative force, offering innovative solutions to the challenges faced by the financial sector, encompassing decentralization, enhanced transparency, and the automation of processes. This comprehensive examination positions blockchain as a dynamic catalyst for positive change in the financial industry.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications
