The findings showed that as more layers of PCM tubes are used, more heat is stored at the time of sunshine, which significantly reduces the temperature of the chamber at day time, but when it comes to releasing this energy, the 2 layers showed the best performance. They release the energy in the same way as 3 layers, while for one layer, it barely releases anything. Regarding the inclination of these tubes, there is not much difference between vertical and inclined tubes, where the latter outperforms the vertical ones by about 1.6° at sun time.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Evaluating IoT-Based Healthcare Architecture with Quality Factors
papers.abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) has become a major technological revolution. Evaluating any IoT advancements comprehensively is critical to understand the conditions under which they can be more useful, as well as to assess the robustness and efficiency of IoT systems to validate them before their deployment in real life. The compliance of IoT architecture to quality is paramount to achieving users’ satisfaction. Currently, we do not have a comprehensive set of guidelines to appraise and select the most suitable IoT platform architectures that meet relevant criteria. Considering the challenges associated with developing IoT healthcare applications. This paper proposes a reference platform as a quality guideline to help both researchers and practitioners who are developing and maintaining IoT platforms. We focus on the suggestion of quality factors to evaluate IoT-Based Healthcare Architecture. We adopted the design science research approach to develop a novel evaluation.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Exploration of Indoor Energy Harvesting
papers.abstract: An energy harvester is the best way to avoid having to use primary and secondary batteries. Thermal, vibration, solar, and wind energy are among the energy collection methods being investigated by researchers. Indoor light, which is a mix of artificial light and scattered sunlight, can be collected using solar cells. Despite the minimal power (in microwatts) generated, the energy can be used to power wireless sensor nodes. This research looks at a number of energy harvesting approaches and explores the need for indoor energy harvesting as well as the challenges that it presents..
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: FOC-DPC Hybrid Structure of a Wind Energy Conversion System Based on PMSG
papers.abstract: Everywhere in the world, the improvement of the electrical energy production as well as the improvement of the electrical network quality has now become a crucial necessity to meet the expected requirements in terms of the energy produced quality. In addition, the shift towards renewable energies such as solar or Wind Power Systems (WPS) to generate electricity has become a current trend for many countries around the world. In this context, we are interested in the Permanent Magnet Synchronous Generator (PMSG) which remains a relevant solution in Wind Energy Conversion Systems (WECS). This machine has many advantages over other machines of the same power such as control of machine power and operation with less noise thanks to the absence of a gearbox in most cases of installation. The complete chain of WECS uses connection stages which serve to connect the generator with the network thanks to power converters mounted back to back. The power control is related to both the PMSG and the control algorithms applied to the Machine Side Converter (MSC) and the Grid Side Converter (GSC). In this present work, we will proceed to the analysis of Field Oriented Control (FOC) applied to MSC and Direct Power Control (DPC) applied to GSC. The simulation results of such controls are provided through the Matlab\Simulink framework.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Factors Influencing Students’ Satisfaction in Learning with Virtual Platforms: Case of Higher Education in Morocco
papers.abstract: E-learning provides many technological and pedagogical advantages thanks to the merger result of education sciences, telecommunication, and the internet. It is a solution adopted at the moment of the changes imposed by the Coronavirus pandemic to achieve distance learning. This paper sheds light on the impact of online education and the students’ satisfaction identifying problems encountered and solutions adopted, which represents a solid basis for better strengthening distance education through either the resolution of the problem or the proposal of remedies. Findings show that Moroccan students’ in engineering schools are not satisfied due to many issues related to connectivity and technical problems, but they proposed many suggestions that can help to build a hybrid educational model.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Fault Diagnosis of the Bearing Outer Ring of an Induction Motor Under DTC Control by Using Hilbert Filter
papers.abstract: Motor Current Signal Analysis (MCSA) has been the most requested method by researchers in recent years for the diagnosis of faults in three-phase Induction Motors (IM). While this method must always involve another field of research for it to be effective, it faces challenges imposed by the evolution of the controls. In this article, it is proposed to adopt this method of detecting Bearing Defects (BD) when the machine is controlled by Direct Torque Control (DTC). Spectral analysis of line current in the case of a direct start, then by the DTC using the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) alone and with the Hilbert Transform (HT), which is an efficient and preferred tool for the extraction of the signal envelope, The extracted signals will clearly illustrate the interest of HT. This diagnostic system is simulated in MATLAB/ Simulink.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Fighting Fake News Propagation Using Blockchain in Journalism: A Systematic Literature Review
papers.abstract: The challenge facing journalism these days in the Internet environment is greater than ever. The public no longer believes in traditional media in light of fake news and infected news. This is possibly due to the emergence of many diverse sources of conflicting information via social media. The central intention of this article is to present a review of previously conducted studies related to the use of the blockchain to fight against the proliferation of fake news to acquire knowledge on the results obtained in this context. The literature review includes articles published between 2017 and 2021. This review shows that despite the published research opportunities, there is not a standard deployable framework in the journalism ecosystem.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Fire Safety System Implementation for Controlling and Monitoring a Siren in Smart Farm Using Gas Sensor and Flame Sensor
papers.abstract: This paper proposes designing and realising a Fire Safety System for Controlling and Monitoring a Siren using a new Embedded Board to solve fire control and extinguishing at the Smart Farm level. The new embedded fire safety system is developed by designing and modelling an embedded board called F3SF (Fire Safety System Smart Farm) based on the Atmega328p Microcontroller. It ensures the monitoring of the Smart Farm in the event of a fire by using specific sensors that detect the fire linked to the proposed board, which automatically controls and monitors (automatic maintenance of the siren and the sprinklers) the siren and sprinklers used to extinguish the fire. The proposed Fire Safety System implemented in the new embedded board F3SF-0X using a voltage sensor, an H-bridge, an ATMega328p microcontroller, a Flame Sensor, an MQ-135 gas sensor, a Water sprinkler, and an electronic relay. The new system is simulated in the EasyEDA web software tool.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Forecasting Heating and Cooling Energy Consumption by Seasonal ARIMA Models
papers.abstract: The main purpose of this paper is to predict energy consumption of heating/cooling for the building’s sector based on Seasonal Auto Regressive Integrated Moving Average SARIMA model for a tertial building located in Errachidia city in Morocco. The data of heating and cooling consumed energy have been collected during the last five years from January 2016 to December 2020 by using ECOTECT ANALYSIS software, considering the climate conditions plus the occupants’ behaviors. After fitting and testing SARIMA model, the analysis of the Mean Absolute Error MAE, Mean Square Error MSE, Root Mean Square Error RMSE and the Scatter Index (SI) show that the seasonal ARIMA approach gives optimal results for both cooling and heating energy consumption forecasting. Finally, this developed time series SARIMA model can be applied as an efficient tool to forecast the energy needs to ensure a long-term development and energy management in the building sector.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: From Sketch to Simulation in Virtual Laboratory Using Deep Learning
papers.abstract: Virtual labs are simulated learning environment softwares that allow students to complete laboratory experiments online or offline to explore concepts and theories without stepping into a physical science lab. Developing such a software code is a long process and a hard task to achieve that requires a skilled expert developer. Human free-hand sketches have been studied in various fields including sketch recognition, synthesis, sketch-based image retrieval, etc. This paper will present a new sketch paradigm to generate a simulation from a Hand sketch. We propose a novel method that uses Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN) combined with a Laboratory as a Service (LaaS) in order to transform a user-drawing sketch into a simulation code. Our approach is based on the use of both SSD (Single Shut Detector) and Mobile-Net as feature extractions for object identification in the sketch, called corresponding web services, which eventually regenerate a simulation software. Our method has been trained and evaluated using the “Quick, Draw” dataset. Experimental results have demonstrated that the approach has achieved over 80% of accuracy.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Generation and Analysis of Residential Microgrid Database
papers.abstract: Nowadays, the high level of solar PV rooftop penetration leads to the increase of residential microgrids and brings new challenges mainly to the Energy Management Systems (EMS). To investigate such issues, a Microgrid Platform (MGP) has been developed in the National Engineers School of Tunis (ENIT). As all residential microgrids, MGP provides a large amount of data from various types of equipment and devices. Each of these data has its format and sampling period and is accessible via its dedicated portal requiring authentication. Hence, the analysis and exploitation of such microgrid data are tedious. In this paper, we propose the design of a centralized database that can be used for Microgrid analysis and EMS development. The data formats are standardized according to the chosen database management system (DBMS), namely MySQL. Depending on the user’s needs, unified sampling periods for all the microgrid data are implemented. In addition, access to the proposed database is designed to be possible from a unique portal.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Government, Digital Society and Industry 4.0: Connective Action Against Covid-19 Fake News
papers.abstract: This article explores social media coverage of news related to the infodemic as a threat to panic and peace in society during the current global crisis due to Covid-19. The infodemic phenomenon has distorted scientific work and reduced public trust in state authorities. This research has identified and analyzed the government’s response to fake news attacks on social media. The method used is qualitative-exploratory with data analysis used of Nvivo 12 Pro. The research results showed that the quality of people’s digital literacy, public communication performance, and the effectiveness of government regulations are part of the challenges in mitigating the infodemic. The Indonesian people have been trying to mitigate the Covid-19 infodemic in Indonesia, both in groups and individually. The presence of the digital society and media movement to mitigate the Covid-19 infodemic that is rife in industry 4.0 within digital public spaces in Indonesia. The presence of fact-checking in the form of a report website to face hoaxes and disinformation is a form of the Indonesian digital 4.0 mitigation movement. In addition to the efforts of the Government of Indonesia, which has three strategic approaches, namely 1) Upstream, digital literacy, and capacity; (2) middle flow, direct action to remove harmful information through technology and collaboration with social media platforms; and (3) downstream, direct action to provide technical assistance in monitoring the spread of malicious information.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: HSIL: Hybrid Semantic Infused Learning Approach for Course Recommendation
papers.abstract: Course recommendation is essential to enable learners to choose the best course among many available courses. So, as a result, a semantically driven course recommendation system is required as the data over the world wide web is increasing, and the web is also tending towards Semantic Web 3.0. In this approach, the initial term pool is created based on the existing user learner profile and the current clicks of the user to determine the exact needs of the user for the term enrichment is done by incorporating ontologies of courses and metadata generation approach is proposed where the metadata is initially classified using XGBoost algorithm where the top 25% is used. Then, the core dataset is also classified using the XGBoost algorithm based on the enriched user terms. And then, the semantic similarity is computed using concept similarity under the genetic algorithm and is recommended to the user. Overall, the accuracy of 96.3% is achieved.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Improved IOT/SDN Architecture with the Concept of NFV
papers.abstract: For a standard software-defined Network (SDN), the controller class the flux gotten for the switch to using it in the network virtualization platform (NFV). we wanted to create a new IoT architecture forced with SDN while adding NFV to the equation. And then we see that the IoT is taking the development between the internet and the new connected devices in our everyday life to another level. Therefore, to be more effective and secure, it needs new approaches and new architecture designs to keep a good level of performance and security. Our paper presents a developed IoT architecture design with the use of SDN technology and also possibilities to use NFV and how it’s going to all work!
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Improvement of DTC Performance of Three Level Inverter Fed IM Drive with High Gain Flux Observer
papers.abstract: The present paper proposes an enhanced direct torque control (DTC) strategy for induction machine (IM) drive associated with a three-level neutral clamped (NPC) inverter. To ensure high-performance of the control scheme, accurate knowledge of flux, current, and speed values is crucial. However, some state variables, namely stator fluxes, are not accessible for measurement. To solve this problem, the high gain observer (HGO) has been integrated. This observer allows estimating the stator flux based on directly measured quantities (stator voltage and current) and also provides a robust control against machine parameter variation. The global control algorithm of the DTC with HGO has been designed and investigated via numerical simulations with Matlab/Simulink software.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Inner Differential Current Suppression in Modular Multilevel Converter
papers.abstract: The applicability of the modular multilevel converter is expanding in diverse industrial applications. The presence of inner differential current can affect the performances and efficiency of this converter, increase the conduction losses, and add more ripples in capacitor voltages. This paper compares two control methods to suppress the AC component of the inner differential currents in each phase. The first uses a controller based on a DQ synchronous reference frame, while the second is based upon a moving average filter (MAF). The inverter structure of the MMC is modeled and simulated in the PLECS environment to evaluate the impact of inner differential current on capacitor voltage ripples and arm currents.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Innovation Activities of Ethiopian Enterprises: Current Issues and Recommendations
papers.abstract: This article examines the findings of Ethiopia’s second national innovation survey, which took place in 2019. The conclusions of this study analyze the status of technological and non-technology innovations in Ethiopian firms, as well as indicate the hurdles to innovation. The findings of this study demonstrate that only 19.4% of enterprises were innovative, based on a list of 300,000 legally registered business-oriented corporations gathered from various government ministries and organizations. Product innovations were the most common followed by process innovations; around % of innovative businesses introduced technology improvements (product and/or process innovations). The inventive enterprises introduced 686 inventions, with 56% being product advances and 78.6% being technology improvements.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: IntelliLegalRec: An RDF Based Metadata Driven Semantically Compliant Recommendation System for Socio-legal Judicial Documents
papers.abstract: Pertinent socio-legal judicial documents are a necessity as backing for similar judicial cases. However, incomplete and irrelevant particulars while reviewing potentially related judicial documents pertaining to a case may prove unfavourable to its direction. This paper proposes a Semantically inclined recommendation system IntelliLegalRec for the recommendation of socio-legal judicial documents, complemented by the generation of metadata and the Resource Description Framework. The approach is user query centric and uses the Latent Dirichlet Allocation model for topic modelling. Furthermore, two stages of semantic similarity computation with the help of semantic measures like Concept Similarity, Normalized Pointwise Mutual Information and Normalized Google Distance, is carried out and further optimized using the Golden Eagle Optimization algorithm. As a result, IntelliLegalRec achieves best in class accuracy of 94.60%, making it an efficient and semantically compliant system for the recommendation of judicial documents.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Intelligent IoT Platform for Precocious Detection of Late Blight and TYLCV Tomato Disease in Morocco
papers.abstract: The agricultural sector is a key backbone of Morocco’s economy. Agriculture has played a major role in the economy through agricultural exports and job creation, thus improving and maintaining good farming practices is critical in ensuring great agricultural yields. Tomatoes are a major product in Moroccan agricultural exports (51%). However, tomato plants are prone to diseases that can significantly reduce production volumes. The two main diseases to which tomato plants in Morocco are exposed are late blight and TYLCV (Tomato yellow leaf curl virus). The objective of this paper is to present an intelligent IoT platform that uses convolutional neural networks to ensure precocious detection of these two diseases, and thus limit the damage they could cause. The proposed convolutional neural networks model allows a 98% accuracy.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Inventory Management in a Refugee Crisis: Paving the Way Towards an Effective New Inventory Model
papers.abstract: This paper aims to prepare a set of the most important assumptions to be considered when modeling the inventory management of a large humanitarian refugee organization. For this purpose, our paper attempts to link the results of conceptual works regarding refugee crisis management to the principles of inventory management in the context of the humanitarian supply chain. As a result, the paper paves the way for researchers to develop an efficient mathematical model to take under consideration the various parameters, such as the big demand fluctuations, the complexity of the situation of refugees, the choices of shipments, the budget, the optimal partitioning, and finally to the imperatives concerning the subsequent solution approaches.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Lightweight Hardware Architecture of EKF-SLAM and Its FPGA Implementation
papers.abstract: Simultaneous localization and mapping (SLAM) estimate the position of a mobile robot and the locations of its landmarks from noisy measurements. EKF-SLAM is a very important class of algorithms that uses the extended Kalman filter (EKF) for SLAM. It is among the most widely used algorithms in the field of mobile robotics for localization and mapping missions for its capability to treat noise and its very high level of accuracy. In light of the computational complexity of SLAM algorithms, the major focus of research to meet real-time requirements is on reducing the computational complexity to develop embedded systems based on lower resource and computationally complex platforms. This paper presents the design of the hardware architecture of EKF-SLAM and its implementation on FPGA. The whole design is implemented through Cyclone 2 FPGA; it can achieve 114 MHz and uses 21512 LUTs, which leads to lightweight hardware architecture.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Literature Review Around: Sustainable Urban Logistics
papers.abstract: Currently, we observe large numbers associated with internal urban migration exacerbating the environmental urban scenario, which is the outcome of various prior circumstances, and this is why urbanization is becoming more widespread. This is true of internet shopping, globalization, and damaging industrial and human resources, to name a few examples. The key solutions are primarily involved with urban mobility, the involvement of the relevant actors, trip optimization, the usage of lanes and roads, and vehicle selection. One of the most significant foundations of the notion of “urban logistics” is sustainability, which is one of the main parts of flow management. Indeed, the ultimate goal extends beyond the delivery of commodities, promoting the environment as a vital component of urban logistics. We will attempt to solve the following problem: Where are we in terms of urban logistics? To address this issue, we used the following strategy: Presentation of the fundamental principles, selection of articles to be used in the study, identification of the major axes, actors, and clarification of the most important axis.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: MASSTagger: Metadata Aware Semantic Strategy for Automatic Image Tagging
papers.abstract: As the social media usage is expanding and people are using more and more platforms to share their stories through pictures, the use of image tags is becoming more relevant in the social media world. On social media. It is often seen that the option of tagging the images that are uploaded. Since image tagging is optional to the users, many images are posted untagged. Now the problem which arises is without image tags these images are hard to find. To overcome this issue many automatic tagging tools have been proposed. But image tagging requires precision and accuracy so that the images are tagged automatically based on the content in them. The proposed methodology uses Structural Topic Modelling and semantic similarity to tag the images and classify them into their respective categories.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Model Reduction in Takagi Sugeno Systems: An LMI Method
papers.abstract: The article is interested MR problem for continuous T-S systems. Sufficient conditions are then established for the error system (ES) are asymptotically stable (AS) has L2-gain performance. An example is provided to check effectiveness of the derived results.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Model Reference Adaptive System Based DPC-SVM Control for Permanent Magnet Synchronous Generator
papers.abstract: This paper presents a new optimization direct power control strategy based on the space vector modulation (DPC-SVM) applied to a PMSG-wind power system. The main purpose of this study is to overcome certain drawbacks of conventional direct power control on the wind power systems, such as variable switching frequency and high currents and power ripples. Hence, the system performance will be improved. In addition, to increase the control performance, a speed observer based on a Model Reference Adaptive System (MRAS) was used to overcome the problems caused by the use of the rotor sensor. To verify the effectiveness and the robustness of the proposed DPC-SVM with MRAS, the whole system was tested under a variable wind speed in the MATLAB/Simulink environment. Furthermore, a comparative analysis was performed between the proposed control DPC-SVM and conventional DPC to highlight the performance of the proposed approach.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: New ETL Process for a Smart Approach of Data Migration from Relational System to MongoDB System
papers.abstract: Today, data is experiencing a real explosion in terms of quantity and nature. They also play a very important role in reading the past, managing the present, and planning for the future. To this end, organizations consider them a treasure and are always looking for a veritable way to manage and exploit them. And since the old data management system has some weaknesses compared to Bigdata, and designers have designed systems called NoSQL to overcome these weaknesses, there is a critical need for migrating data to the new NoSQL system, to keep their old data and take advantage of the power of NoSQL systems. To meet this need, several approaches have been developed by researchers to ensure this migration. The problem is that these approaches transform data, structures, or both. These approaches at best mimic a relational database with its limitations and constraints in another NoSQL environment, which loses much of its efficiency when applying relational processing in the migration result database. In this article, we will develop a smart approach that aims to migrate the three essential parts of relational databases: the first is data, the second is structure, and the third is the semantic component, using an ETL to be defined. The solution respects the analytical part of the relational systems that we have developed and the transformations of the structure and semantics of the relational system towards the NoSQL system, to develop our model of our ETL, which consists of three phases: data extraction, transforming this data, and uploading it to the destination system.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: New Experimental Approach for MAC Layer Misbehavior of the IEEE 802.11 Protocol
papers.abstract: This paper is dedicated to the description of the procedure to be followed in the experimental validation of the fairness of the BEB (Binary Exponential Backoff) algorithm as well as the case of the presence of the bad behavior of this algorithm. We set up all the necessary tools for the experimentation, the generation and the analysis of communication traffic between the stations of our experimental bench. This work is conditioned by the availability of network cards that can support modifications to the protocols, including the BEB. The solution for this modification currently lies in the ATHEROS network cards (those which can support one of the three drivers: MADWIFI, ATH5K or ATH9K). The proposed experimental network consists of at least three stations (laptops) including a receiver and a cheat with integrated wireless cards. Laptops operate under IEEE 802.11. We put all the devices inside the lab to make sure they are under the same channel conditions and the only difference between legitimate and misbehaved nodes is the Back-off timer. JPerf, the commonly adopted network test tool, will be used to create traffic on the network, as well as generate User Datagram Protocol (UDP) streams that can fill the transmit queue on each device, putting all devices in full mode. Wireshark is employed to display the captured data and the Perl language to analyze the corresponding trace files.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: New Printable Concrete Formulation Based on Fibers from Doum Palm Leaflets for 3D Printing of Energy-Efficient Buildings
papers.abstract: Numerous countries throughout the world, including Morocco, are devoted to the process of sustainable construction in order to construct high-energy-efficiency structures capable of significantly reducing energy consumption during the various stages of construction. Among the innovative solutions for achieving sustainability in the field of construction is 3D printing technology, which allows for raw material savings, cost reductions on construction sites, and a significant reduction in CO2 emissions. The 3D printing of buildings, or additive manufacturing, is carried out by the injection of concrete, layer by layer, with a specific formulation and properties. This formulation could be developed by integrating ecological materials, which will contribute to the thermal inertia of buildings thanks to their low thermal conductivity. Several insulating materials have been proposed in this regard, including minerals such as natural basalt fiber, pozzolana, and hemp, and biological fibers such as flax, rapeseed, and palm leaflets. The objective of this article is to propose a new formulation of injectable concrete for buildings made by the 3D technique, which must be fluid to be used in the printer and firm to hold the times printed. This is done by using aggregates characterized by their low thermal conductivity and reinforcing fibers based on natural materials, providing thermal comfort to users. These fibers are, in particular, the leaflets of the doum palm, which are very abundant in Morocco and in the Mediterranean region, and their characteristics will be discussed in this article.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: OSIBR: Ontology Focused Semantic Intelligence Approach for Book Recommendation
papers.abstract: With the dawn of digital books on the web, finding reliable book suggestions has become a complicated question, and several significant machine learning algorithms were developed to address such requests. Also, there exists a problem of the prevailing recommendation system not presenting a well fit to the semantically designed nature of the world wide web. Moreover, due to the vast digitization of books, a plethora of the old book discovery strategic approaches is intended for physical selection only. Therefore, research interest is increasing in the innovative approach to search, browse, and use the recommendation engine. In this paper, an ontology focused semantic intelligence approach for the book recommendation is proposed using Jaccard similarity, NPMI, and Kullback Leibler divergence with the optimization done by the Krill Herd algorithm. The proposed system OSIBR reaches an overall Precision and Accuracy of 94.16% and 95.55% respectively and with the highest Normalized Discounted Cumulative Gain (nDCG) of 0.96.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: OntoRecipe: An Ontology Focussed Semantic Strategy for Recipe Recommendation
papers.abstract: In present-day times there is a need for a recipe recommendation system to cater to the demanding culinary needs of several users around the globe. This paper proposes an ontology focused recipe recommendation approach by incorporating semantic models. Its deep learning infused using RNN classification. The dataset uses here is from Food.com Recipes and Interaction which contains 280 k+ recipes and 700 k+ recipe reviews which covers 18 years of user interaction and uploads. The dataset based on it increases a good number of auxiliary knowledge and uses pair of approaches like SemantoSim and Kullback-Leibler divergence under the Shuffled Frog-Leaping algorithm a knowledge-centric recipe is recommended. It shows that the proposed structure performs better than the other baseline approaches in terms of the F-Measure and False Discovery Rate having values 95.03 and 0.06.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Operating Modeling of Medium Range Protocols IoT
papers.abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) is a hot topic. It considers a system of billions of physical objects, industrial machines, and billions of humans where these objects connected to the Internet. The Internet of Things based on existing communication techniques and protocols. The diversity of protocols responds to the multiplicity of billions of connected objects. There are different technologies, each of which has its own particularities in terms of a set of criteria. However, the absence of connectivity protocol results in a lack of interoperability between IoT objects and applications. In this article we answer the question of protocol operation, we will present the operating models of medium-range network protocols. These models ensure a deep and flexible conception of how network protocols work using UML modeling. We used the activity diagram.it permit to graphically represent the behavior of these protocols. In this paper, we focus on medium-range are Z-wave, Wi-Fi, and Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE).
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Optimal Design of Standalone Hybrid Renewable Energy Systems Using HOMER
papers.abstract: Hybrid systems can compensate the intermittent character of renewable energy sources and offer a higher overall energy efficiency. Their main interest is the possibility of energy autonomy that they permit since they do not depend on a single source. This paper discusses a new approach for designing a stand-alone hybrid system (photovoltaic, wind and battery) combining three objectives: power loss probability, life cycle cost and embodied energy for an optimal design. The simulation results are presented using Matlab/Simulink software and discussed with a comparison of developed methodology and an existing commercial tool (HOMER software).
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Optimization of Meixner Moments by the Firefly Algorithm for Image Analysis
papers.abstract: Discrete orthogonal polynomials are widely used for image processing, these polynomials are characterized by parameters. However, it was very important to optimize these parameters to obtain a good result, in this context; this study suggests a new approach to optimize Meixner polynomial (MPs) parameters (β, u) based on the Firefly Algorithm (FA), and we use them to reconstruct 2D images. Using the FA algorithm determines the optimal parameters of the MPs, which improves the quality of the reconstruction. The simulation results, on the one hand, suggest that the proposed method based on the FA algorithm offers a high image reconstruction quality. On other hand, the comparison carried out with other algorithms indicates the advantage of the proposed method.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Optimization of P&O Algorithm for Load Variation Scenario Through PV System Physical Model
papers.abstract: To face global warming and fossils fuels depletion, the use of renewable energy is becoming mandatory to satisfy the energy demand that increases further each year. For instance, photovoltaic systems are widely used thanks to their high performance and small cost. The photovoltaic (PV) systems can be connected to the grid for night supply or standalone systems with storage devices like batteries. To study and analyze the power produced by PV systems in different weather conditions, several mathematical models have been used in the MATLAB environment. In this paper, the modeling of each element of the photovoltaic system has been done including a PV module, a Boost Converter, a load, and a MPPT to operate the PV system in high efficiency in different weather conditions and scenarios. Moreover, the maximum power point tracking (MPPT) was investigated in this work using the Perturb and Observe (P&O) approach. The newly optimized P&O has been compared to an optimized P&O in several cases. The results show that our P&O protocol is very close to the optimized P&O for the solar irradiance variation scenario but more suitable and efficient for the load variation scenario.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Parametric Identification by Improved Levenberg-Marquardt Method of Solar Cell’s Double Diode Model
papers.abstract: The study of a photovoltaic module response is ensured by analyzing the behavior of its solar cells. To do this, the identification of the structural parameters of the solar cells is primordial. In literature, we distinguish several electrical models of solar cells which we consider the single and the double diode models. In this paper, we use an improved method “Levenberg-Marquardt combined with simulated annealing” to determine the five and the seven electrical parameters of the considered models. The performance of the proposed method is evaluated by comparing its identification accuracy with other methods discussed in the literature. A comparative study shows the better accuracy provided by the double diode model compared to the single diode model.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Performance Evaluation of Pilot-Based Channel Estimation for Conventional, Cell-Free, and Small-Cell Massive MIMO Networks
papers.abstract: Massive multiple-input multiple-output (M-MIMO) and net- work design are two contemporary technologies that have received a lot of attention. Moreover, an innovative network design known as cell-free (CF) has attracted considerable interest. Thus, addressing and assessing the channel estimation (CE) operation in a CF M-MIMO network is an appealing topic. Consequently, this paper focuses on the M-MIMO technology, in particular, the CE operation for three differents three-time division duplex-enabled M-MIMO networks, namely, the conventional M- MIMO network, the CF M-MIMO network, and the small-cell M-MIMO network. Furthermore, for each network design, we apply the minimum mean square error (MMSE) estimator to evaluate and determine the CE operation, including the expression of the large-scale fading model. On the other hand, we established the normalized mean square error formula for the MMSE estimator regarding each network configuration. Addition- ally, a pilot assignment scheme is proposed for CF M-MIMO network. Numerical results are displayed to confirm our theoretical expressions.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Photovoltaic Water Pumping System: Modeling and Simulation of Characteristics for Direct Coupling
papers.abstract: Significant progress has been made recently in the field of photovoltaic (PV) generator conversion, with an emphasis on the use of multiple approaches. This has largely focused attention on irrigation fields, prompting several researchers to investigate ways to make photovoltaic installations more cost-effective and affordable. Additionally, several strategies for improving photovoltaic cell efficiency are considered, which are modeled at the level of increasing cell lifetime or extracting maximum power from the cells. With this in mind, the current study analyzes the various parameters that affect the manufacturing of GPVs, including their electrical characteristics. Additionally, this analysis is numerically based. A direct coupling is established between the GPV, which is composed of REC 330NP photovoltaic panels, and a solar water pumping system powered by a SQF 0–6-2 motor pump. The numerical results of the proposed analysis are presented to emphasize the critical nature of a matching converter that maximizes the extraction of power generated by the GPV.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Predicting the Residential Energy Consumption in Morocco Based on Time Series Forecasting Models
papers.abstract: Forecasting the electricity demand is crucial for efficient planning, management, and conservation towards intelligent power systems. This study investigates the yearly electricity consumption of the Moroccan residential sector from 1990 to 2020. Two different time series forecasting methods are implemented and the best one is selected to predict the electricity consumption for the coming years. The performance of the Holts Linear Trend (HLT) method is compared to those of the Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average (ARIMA) Model. The outcome analysis shows that the similarities in prediction were high; meanwhile, the HLT performs better than ARIMA and predicts the data with more accuracy; lowest Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE), Root Mean Square Error (RMSE), and Mean Square Error (MSE) compared to ARIMA model. The forecasted results based on HLT model show that the Moroccan residential electricity consumption will remain increasing by an average annual rate of 2.4% in the following 10 years. In 2030, Morocco will consume around 1253 Ktoe of electricity in the residential sector. This paper has policy implications that can be deployed for optimal planning in the electricity sector.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Prediction of Water Quality Using Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Statistical Approach
papers.abstract: Water is a valuable resource on the earth. To be consumed safely, it must be treated. But the increasing pollution of the reserves makes this operation more and more delicate. Accordingly, water quality modeling and prediction have become critical in tackling pollution. The goal of this study is to use an advanced artificial intelligence (AI) system to estimate the water quality index (WQI) for the surface. We aim through this research to generate a non-supervised machine learning Model that can predict the value of target variable inferred from available data (training data), as a soft computing technique: Decision Trees for Classification was used to predict the potability of water. It provides a good performance in this study. A big dataset, collected monthly over a period of 5 years, was employed in the modeling process. The data were splited into sets, one for model building (training dataset) and the other for model validation (testing dataset) to avoid over-learn. Statistical assessment indicators and visual evaluation Metrics estimated the prediction capacity of the developed model. The outcome demonstrates that DTC was opportune for the forecast of the potability indice and would be functional for water purification organizations in the management of divers water supply systems (R2 = 72%, RMSE = 0.41). The models developed in this study could serve as a foundation for decision-making that will aid in the maintenance and improvement of water supply system management.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Prediction of the Dust Accumulation Rate on a Parabolic Trough Mirror by CFD Method
papers.abstract: In Industry 4.0, the development of intelligent power systems will contribute to providing people with better quality of electrical energy. The power generation system with solar power plants in Industry 4.0 is also the focus of development. The areas with high irradiation density have suffered from a high concentration of aerosol particles and thus, these technologies have experienced losses due to soiling. So, in this study, the behavior of dust particles with a cylindro-parabolic mirror has been carried out. For wind flow analysis, we have used the computational fluid dynamics (CFD) method, for the prediction in a 2D domaine, the simulation has been carried out based on the Shear Stress Transport k-w turbulence model and a discrete phase model for particle motion prediction. The results revealed that, unlike the large particle, the trajectory of the small ones creates vortices behind the heliostat, as the particle size increases, it can be noticed that the vortices start to dissipate. The maximum deposition rate due to gravity could be as high as 10% for 100 µm dust particles, which shows the important role played by gravity on the dust deposition rate for larger dust particles. The decrease in deposition after the size exceeds 100 µm is only because these particles do not reach the heliostat, they leave the area through the ground before reaching the mirror.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Predictive Torque and Direct Torque Controls for Doubly Fed Induction Motor: A Comparative Study
papers.abstract: The aim of the study presented in this paper is to improve the performance of the direct torque control (DTC) applied to doubly fed induction motors (DFIM). The DTC is characterized by good speed tracking and high robustness against parametric variations of the motor and external disturbances. However, there are significant drawbacks to this method as well. On the one hand, the switching frequency is not controlled, with a high ripple in the stator flux and the torque. Therefore, a new approach is developed to improve the performance of DTC. Predictive torque control (PTC) is a technique that determines the appropriate voltage vector to apply in the next sampling period by constructing a unique cost function from a linear combination of objective functions. The torque and flux errors are accounted for utilising weight factors in a cost function. Finally, the proposed control is compared to DTC by simulation in the Matlab\Simulink environment.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Ranking Task in RAS: A Comparative Study of Learning to Rank Algorithms and Interleaving Methods
papers.abstract: Relational Aggregated Search (RAS) is a paradigm that refers to a set of techniques for retrieving and aggregating information nuggets based on their relationships. The performance of a relational aggregated search is dedicated to how to retrieve an information nugget, and, how to present this information to users to fulfill their information need. Recent RAS research has focused only on information retrieval strategies. As consequence, there is a critical need to research strategies for aggregating and ranking information nuggets. In this paper, we focus on the recent issue, namely answering the question of how information is shown to the users. In addition, what are the most effective approaches for the ranking task? We use the ranked results from RAS. These ranked results include different content types (images, videos, news, maps, blogs, groups, and books, etc.). Thus, the focal challenge in the vertical results aggregation task is how to rank these heterogeneous results to obtain pertinent information. Our experimental results of the application of learning to rank algorithms and interleaving methods are shown in this study. From the evaluation results, we can affirm that LambdaMART proves its performance compared with the other; LambdaRank, MART, ListNet, and RankNet. Finally, we apply the interleaving approaches to satisfy the user's need by providing appropriate, organized, and most relevant results. Our experimental study proves that VI-TDA performs better than TDA in all steps of our tests.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Road Traffic Anomaly Detection: A Survey
papers.abstract: Lately the number of vehicles circulating on roads had been widely increased especially in the big cities, leading instantly to congestion and consequently the accidents rate increasement. In order to reduce traffic issues and enhance road safety, so many researchers focus their vision on this era problem trying to look for solutions by developing algorithms and embedded applications based on image processing and artificial intelligence, therefore we aim to automate the traditional detecting systems used to spot traffic violations such as: speeding detection; crossing a red light; driving on a prohibited lane, etc. this offenses considered among the principal factors of road accidents, for this main reason the built of those embedded systems play a very important role for traffic accident reduction through monitoring the road safety and automating the penalty detection. In this article, we are trying to study the most recent techniques based on computer vision to monitor and detect traffic offenses, lightning the strengths and weaknesses of these techniques, additionally we provide research a comparison and study basis to facilitate their future work also improve robust approaches to detect road violations.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Robust Control of a Wind Conversion System Based on a Doubly Fed Induction Generator: A Comparison Between Adaptive Backstepping and Integral Sliding Mode Controllers
papers.abstract: The voltage sag, caused by the short-circuit faults in transmission and subtransmission system of the wind energy conversion system (WECS), produces a transient stator flux (DC component) in the stator windings of the doubly fed induction generator (DFIG).This phenomenon damaging the fragile inverter and the system gearbox. In fact, the dynamic equations of the wind turbine are strongly nonlinear as are the ones of the DFIG. In addition, most of the WECS parameters are uncertain, and this system is always suffering from disturbance sources such the harmonics and the mechanical vibrations. To address these problems, robust power control strategies must be implemented. In this paper, two controllers are investigated. These strategies contribute to the elimination of the high peak currents and stabilize the active power at its desired value. First, an Adaptive Backstepping (AB) controller is adopted. Then, an integral sliding mode controller (ISMC) is designed. The performance of the two controllers is tested and compared under Matlab/Simulink environment. It has been proved that the Integral Sliding mode controller is more robust to minimizes the currents fluctuations and the parameters uncertainty.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Scenarios for ADAS Testing: Modeling and Design
papers.abstract: The aim of this paper is to offer a novel way of creating a collection of automated scenario-based tests to evaluate the safety of autonomous cars, especially those that mix simulation and real-world testing. Our methodology is built on formal methods, which combine a mathematical model to describe a scenario, define a relevant metric, and generate a set of test scenarios for characterization of test scenarios with a formal situation description. However, any advanced driving system (ADS) may encounter an infinite number of situations and scenarios that influence its interaction. As a result, such scenarios can be characterized as an extensive collection of characteristics and parameters that define all potential scenarios and are investigated using a scenario-based method.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Sensors Deployment in IoT Environment
papers.abstract: The rapid growth of wireless sensor networks (WSNs) demand creates numerous Internet of Things (IoT) applications applied in several domains. On account of its wide-ranging application, WSNs that can monitor specific sensing range is the most adopted IoT platform. Therefore, the search for preferable placements and the optimal number of sensor node deployments can reduce computation, energy, cost, and communication overhead, which are the most factors that directly impact the coverage network. To address the problem, we propose a deployment and connectivity model bonded to sensor networks to ensure sensor deployment on a grid for target coverage while allowing connectivity. The model focuses on the k-coverage problem, which necessitates selecting a minimum subset of nodes such that at least k sensor nodes cover each point of a field of interest. The simulation results proved the proposed deployment model’s efficacy and capability to reach maximum coverage with a minimum number of sensors.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Simulation and Forecasting the Covid-19 Pandemic in the Rabat Region
papers.abstract: There have been 19 pandemics since December 2019 in Wuhan, China. It then spread to all of the world’s major cities and capitals. As a result, the World Health Organization declared it a worldwide pandemic. It also impacts some industries such as industry, trade, the economy, and public transit. In this study, we utilized the SIR (Susceptible, Infectious, Recovered) model to forecast the status of Covid-19 in the Rabat region, and the model obtained was compared with the current epidemic situation in the region. Furthermore, we have simulated the propagation of the Covid-19 virus by the Multi-Agent System Gama-Platform.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Study of a Photovoltaic Pumping System Application for Irrigation the Palm in Beni Mellal City
papers.abstract: This paper presents a simple standalone photovoltaic pumping system that achieved satisfaction through the reliability installation, depending on the quantity of water needed and the solar energy deposit in the place concerned. This paper aims to design, size, and simulate the system that irrigates an area of 8823 m2 of palm trees in Beni Mellal city located in the Middle Atlas of Morocco, which needs 60 m3/day. The appropriate pump is selected according to the water required during the irrigation period, the total dynamic head, and the necessary hydraulic power, which is used to calculate the electric power to determine the required photovoltaic model and system elements. Finally, the PVWPS simulation is performed in the PVsyst software. Then, the photovoltaic pumping system scheme is validated using the MATLAB Simulink environment to discuss the obtained results.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: TS-Fuzzy Model Based Predictive Controller for a Variable Speed Wind Turbine
papers.abstract: The purpose of this paper is to look into the design and implementation of a wind turbine system. Because the wind turbine system's linearized model is required for control synthesis, the Takagi-Suengo fuzzy technique is used to generate a robust linear model, which is then used to design the continuous-time model predictive controller (CMPC). The simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the suggested approach for variable speed wind turbine control.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Technological and Entrepreneurial Factors as Drivers of Private Equity Funding in Morocco
papers.abstract: This paper investigates technological and entrepreneurial factors as drivers of private equity fundraising in Morocco. Based on ARDL analysis, the results of this research indicate that, in the short run, only productivity has a significant and negative effect on fundraising. Then, patents have no significant impact in the long run, while the number of new businesses, productivity, research, and development has a considerable effect. Finally, these findings demonstrated that the government must enhance the entrepreneurial ecosystem to promote private equity investments.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Terahertz Band Advances and Applications
papers.abstract: The terahertz band, which is located between the microwave and the infrared bands, has shown countless assets that have allowed researchers to make huge advances in different sectors. In this paper, we try to present an overview of terahertz digital and analog applications in some areas such as communication, material characterization, and imaging. Terahertz communication systems deliver data rates of the order of TBps, allowing data-intensive applications to operate more quickly. Terahertz spectroscopy can detect chemicals or liquids that are difficult to identify with other techniques, using FTS (Fourier transform spectroscopy). Terahertz rays offer very high image resolution thanks to their wavelength. Applications include medical imaging, protein detection, the effects of radiation on biological processes, disease diagnosis and the analysis of the optical properties of human tissues. In the field of security, the detection of illicit or dangerous objects is done by short pulses lasers. Finally, TD non-destructive spectroscopy makes it possible to observe and preserve cultural heritage through short laser pulses. In the field of terahertz communication systems, we will present the simulation results of an active bandpass filter at the frequency 129 GHz. This filter has the particularity of being tunable over a 300 MHz band.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: The Integration of Industry 4.0 in Operations Management: Toward Smart Lean Six Sigma
papers.abstract: Recently, Industry 4.0 “I4.0” has been identified as a priority research area and has attracted the interest of many researchers, universities and companies worldwide. I4.0 is now revolutionizing manufacturing operations management among other areas through the increased availability and accessibility of data. The capability of digital technologies for speed data collection and analysis promises a new age of continuous improvement and process performance. Lean six sigma “LSS”, the widely best continuous improvement concept used in manufacturing will be affected by I4.0 technologies. However, the relationship between the two paradigms is not well developed in the literature and remains to be studied. To fill this gap, this paper aims to address what has already been described in the literature and analyze the scientific research trends related to the combination of I4 and LSS in manufacturing context through a structured literature review. The findings confirm that Lean Six Sigma and Industry 4.0 is a new emerging research field that has attracted a great scientific interest over the last decade. Our study will relevant to both academics and practitioners, as it describes the main lines of research in the field. Further studies are needed to validate these results.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Towards an Approach for Efficient Energy Management in WSN
papers.abstract:
Since they are endowed with small batteries and limited energy, it is expensive and difficult to change or charge the batteries of the sensors, especially if they are deployed in large numbers. The exhaustion of the sensor nodes’ energy most often implies the malfunction of the entire network. Therefore, at the time of design, communication protocols for WSN should take into account the degree of energy consumption to provide optimal management of this resource. An effective model of conservation and Energy will be presented. In this work, our contribution focuses on the implementation of an integrated, efficient solution based on the integration of the following system: A new scheme for the management of energy consumption, based priority mechanism, using the AODV protocol, is proposed in this contribution. Most of the existing energy models only analyze the energy state of the communication module in the absence of studies of global energy consumption from the view of nodes.
 However, by modeling the energy consumption related to different node components in different modes of operation and state transitions, our work proposes a new energy management model, based on the improvement of the reactive routing protocol AODV, under the NS simulator, providing energy management and load balancing mechanism for the entire network, applying a technique of prioritizing nodes with more energy resources and event triggering. This model showed good performance in terms of energy consumption, average time, and flow.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Towards the Implementation of a Digital Twin for Induction Motors
papers.abstract: Energy Efficiency (EE) is one of the main pillars of Industry 4.0. The road map of this new industrial revolution has imposed a certain degree of accuracy for conditions monitoring of industrial machines, where Induction Motors (IMs) represent the most important machines in terms of energy consumption (42.2% of the global energy demand). Achieving an accurate EE monitoring for IMs imposes a good Stray Load Loss (SLL) estimation by taking into consideration the In-situ aspect. In this paper, the five-dimension digital twin (DT) model is used to propose a new approach to integrate the EE for DT. The proposed method is the first step toward DT implementation for IM. Three state-of-the-art SLL models for IM are compared and the most accurate expression to integrate into the EE model for the proposed DT is then selected. The selected model takes into consideration all SLL sources in IM represented in the Electric Equivalent Circuit (EEC) by the stator and the rotor leakage inductances and the magnetizing inductance.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Transfer Learning and Pressure Effect for Handwriting to Early Detection of Parkinson’s Disease
papers.abstract: Parkinson’s disease (PD) is a degenerative and progressive disorder that affects the nervous system. The diagnosis of this disease remains a very important area of research since there is no subjective method of diagnosis used clinically. In this work, to address this problem, we involved a deep learning model using transfer learning from a pre-trained CNN called MobilNetV2 to classify patients with Parkinson’s disease. This model was retrained with a database of patients’ handwriting spirals. To remedy the problem of the poor size of our database, we used augmentation techniques consisting of some pre-processing layers (mirror effect, rotation, saturation…) to have enough images to train the model. These images include the pressure exerted on the screen when drawing spirals by representing each level of pressure with a specified color. The obtained results have proved very promising.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Unsupervised Deep Learning Model Based on Autoencoders for Cancer Classification
papers.abstract: Genomics and proteomics activities are the fundamental controllers of the living processes of any organism. They carry out most of the work, perform most life functions, and make up the majority of cellular structures. Moreover, their abnormal behaviors are the driving factors of many genetic diseases. Therefore, understanding these activities has become a fundamental mission of large-scale genomics research. However, this study field has some limitations, such as the sensitivity, the high dimensional, and the large scale of data. That is why it is a challenging application area of deep learning. However, this last is more effective when one wants to make sense of large and complex data sets. This paper uses a deep learning method based on autoencoders to solve a classification problem, which is cancer detection using two distinct annotated data sets: the microRNA data set and the reverse phase proteins array. The results were promising and proved that our deep integrative model was superior in the Cancer Type and the PFI score classification and prediction with high accuracy.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Used Car Price Prediction with Pyspark
papers.abstract: Price prediction has always been a challenging task as there are several factors involved that tend to cause variation in prices. There is similar case with car price prediction. It is a difficult task to decide either a used car actually justifies the posted price. Factors such as mileage, model, year influence the car prices. The paper aims to develop machine learning models which include linear regression, gradient boosting algorithm, and decision tree in order to predict used car prices based on its features. In order to obtain results, we made an implementation using Apache Spark, which is a big data tool. Among several machine learning libraries used such as pandas and others, we have also preferred Apache spark for implementation. The dataset of PakWheels is utilized which originally have 56186 instances and 16 attributes. A considerable amount of work has been done on this research domain; however, very little amount of work has been done using pyspark. Our proposed models predicted accuracy with linear regression of about 50% while Gradient boosting algorithm obtained 89% and 86% accuracy with decision tree regression.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Vocal Parameters Analysis for Amazigh Phonemes Recognition System
papers.abstract: Several studies in the development of Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) systems have been yet presented and well studied for many languages. However, the particularities of the Amazigh Language due to its specific acoustic features such as the emphasis on Amazigh consonants pose many problems for developing its automatic speech recognition system. In this paper, we deal with the spoken recognition problem in Amazigh Language. We consider here a particular case of the problem, focusing on the phonemes formants frequencies which are a principal variable in specifying the phonetic substance of discourse sounds. We propose an approach allowing the vocal parameters analysis of phonetic units, as well as a recognition algorithm by analyzing the formants.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: Vulnerabilities in Android OS: Challenges and Mitigation Techniques
papers.abstract: Android is one of the most popular operating systems for smartphones. Although Android OS includes many salient features which include its open-source nature, and its affinity to be customizable but its security remains debatable. Android uses SE-Linux for its security model, which can be leveraged and easily manipulated if ignored. The rise in IoT, smart gadgets, and wearable devices that also use Android as their operating system can’t be ignored. Over the period many vulnerabilities have been discovered and exploited. This research paper focuses on some recent and most popular security vulnerabilities found in the android mobile operating system. The key contribution of this research includes the identification of mitigation techniques to overcome the challenges faced by its user The security of android devices can be significantly improved If mitigation techniques are applied effectively.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: “AskBot”: The AI Chatbot that Enhances the Learning Process
papers.abstract: The application of artificial intelligence (AI) in the field of education has increased significantly over the last decade. In fact, several works have been carried out to implement intelligent systems to help learners in their learning processes. Educational chatbot is one of the AI solutions that help tutors to capitalize their knowledge and transfer it to learners. Its main feature is to interact effectively with human requests using natural language processing (NLP) tools, in order to understand the human intention and respond to it through text or voice. As a result, chatbot improves efficiency and productivity in the field of education. In this paper, we present our chatbot's architecture “AskBot” that supports students in their learning process by giving them appropriate answers to their questions. Our proposed architecture guarantees a continuous and autonomous update of AskBot’s knowledge base (KB). To demonstrate the feasibility of our approach, we implemented the kernel components of the proposed architecture.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: A Comparative Study Between Hydrogen and Battery Storage for Residential Applications in Morocco
papers.abstract: Renewable energies are good sources to provide electricity for residential applications. The geographical location of Morocco allows to have significant potential to produce electricity throughout the year using solar and wind energy sources. Such potential can help the country to decrease its fossil reliance. However, the intermittent nature of wind and photovoltaic energies can diminish their real efficiency and rises a plenty of challenges for their use. Therefore, appropriate energy storage technology is necessary for Energy Storage Systems (ESS) to compensate and equilibrate the difference between the energy generation and consumption. Battery is the most popular energy storage regarding to hydrogen storage that is a promising technology with zero gas emission. This study focuses on the economic performance between hydrogen storage, battery storage and their hybrid storage. For this purpose, three power storage models have been designed using HOMER PRO software. Moreover, a comparison of the economic impact of the proposed models is provided. As a result, the hybrid storage has been identified as the best solution with a COE of 0.577 $/kWh.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: A Critical Review of DC/AC Converter Structures for Grid-Connected Photovoltaic Systems
papers.abstract: Work on the critical review of DC/AC converter structures for grid-connected photovoltaic systems is presented. Due to the large energy needs of different uses and the exhausting of fossil fuel supplies and the environmental impacts have become increasingly important. Photovoltaic (PV) systems use the available power of the sun and transform it from direct current to alternating current. In this review, the main constituents of a grid-connected PV plant are presented as well as the DC/AC converter structures, effects, and challenges of integration are discussed. Based on the harmonic distortion rate (THD), comparison literature is discussed to identify the DC/AC structure for grid-connected PV systems offering the best performance. This study will assist in the implementation of PV systems into the grid in new projects while avoiding the evident challenges encountered in existing projects.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: A Hybrid Recommender System for Pedagogical Resources
papers.abstract: The present paper proposes an e-learning system that combines popularity and collaborative filtering techniques to recommend pedagogical resources. A recommender system helps users get a correct and personalized decision by applying several recommendation methods such as content-based, collaborative filtering, and other hybrid approaches. However, predicting a relevant resource with a specific context, like pedagogical content, becomes a challenge. In our work, we propose a model to ameliorate the traditional collaborative filtering technique by (i) using the Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) to tackle the problem of scalability and data sparsity; (ii) extracting the most popular resources that the user does not interact with before to resolve the cold start problem; and (iii) combining the results of popularity and SVD factorization methods to improve the recommendation accuracy that evaluated by applying the recall, precision and f1-score of each approach. The comparison shows that the obtained results exhibit an encouraging performance of our model.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: A Modeling Learner Approach for Detecting Learning Styles in Adaptive E Learning Systems
papers.abstract: In adaptive E Learning systems, learner properties are often modeled to provide information about their preferences and their learning style. Thus, the learning style model is the most used personalization parameter in the modeling of learners. The major problem is how this model can be used to provide efficient learner modeling. In this paper, we are studying this important research topic to create a learner model that can facilitate the detection of learning style. The basic idea is to introduce into the proposed model a new field of information concerning the motivation about each dimension of the learning style model considered. To this end, the dimensions of Felder’s and Silverman’s learning styles model are considered. The motivation rate corresponding to each dimension is measured and then stored in the model built to allow immediate detection of the learning style by simply consulting the field associated with the motivation rate and without resorting to treatments dedicated to the detection of styles nor the use of classification techniques. The proposed modeling approach exploits the benefits of existing standards to be able to reuse other models, which makes it possible to add the proposed new information field, namely the field associated with the motivation rate. To represent and store the profiles of the learners the XML standard is used.
keywords: Felder and Silverman learning styles model
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: A New Approach to Manage the Energy Flow in a Hybrid Renewable Energy System Based on Green Hydrogen
papers.abstract: Green energy is receiving increasing attention today, and researchers are examining different ways to develop it. Of these various forms, hydrogen is attractive as a central pillar of the energy transformation needed to limit global warming to two degrees Celsius, creating significant benefits for the environment, the energy system, and the global economy. Because hydrogen is a very light gas, its storage is a real challenge. Several methods of storing hydrogen as a gas at high pressure are possible. However, this requires technical means to stock it at low temperature or high pressure. The objective of this paper is to present the current situation of hydrogen production in the world, especially in Morocco, and to propose a solution to manage the energy flow in a hybrid renewable energy system using green hydrogen. This gas is an opportunity for a strong integration of renewable energy in the energy mix.
keywords: Hydrogen storage hybrid renewable energy system
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications

papers.title: A New Dual Band Antenna with Improvement Performances for the Internet of Things Applications
papers.abstract: In the Internet of Things (IoT) age, thousands of objects will communicate with each other at the same time using multiple frequency bands. In this context, the demand for efficient antennas will increase, as they are the main part of these communication systems. These antennas must be small and operate in several frequency bands to be used in numerous applications at the same time. In this work, simple planar resonators and defected ground structure are proposed for designing a new miniature antenna for Internet of Things applications, this antenna functions two operating bands of 2.45 GHz for WLAN (Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, ZigBee, Z-wave)/IIoT/WBAN, MBAN and 5.8 GHz for WiFi. In the first section, this antenna is studied, designed, optimized, and simulated on FR-4 Substrate characterized with a dielectric thickness of 1.6 mm and a relative permittivity of 4.4; In terms of matching, bandwidth, radiation pattern, and gain, better simulation results are obtained. The CST microwave studio software is used to generate all simulation results. In the second section, the proposed antenna is fabricated on FR4 substrate commercial, measured, and compared with simulation results for both bands, a good matching is found for these bands. In the last section, the proposed antenna is compared with those published in previous works. As a result of this comparison, this antenna provides a new high level of performance in terms of matching, bandwidth, gain, radiation pattern, cost, and size.
papers.conference_name: International Conference on Digital Technologies and Applications
